<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565619 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05155: Customizing Migration

- page_id: `249565619`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565619/Customizing+Migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:39.006+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > DoDAF Migration to UPDM 1.x
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize DoDAF migration to UPDM. However, to do so, you need to have some basic knowledge of UML, especially stereotypes. For this reason, we highly recommend that you use predefined migration configurations.

Customizing DoDAF migration to UPDM requires a basic understanding of how to perform migration. Basic rules and principles of data migration to UPDM can be found in *<install.root>\profiles\DoDAFtoUPDMMapping.mdzip* project. This project consists of DoDAF, UPMD profiles and predefined relationships defining mapping between DoDAF and UPDM stereotypes.

DoDAF to UPDM mapping rules can be found in *<install.root>\profiles\DoDAFtoUPDMMapping.mdzip* project.

Data Migration is based on three types of stereotyped relationships:

1. Replace Tagged Value,
2. Replace Stereotype,
3. Replace Type.

These relationships are the basics of migration.

1. Replace tagged value relationship is drawn between tag definitions of two different stereotypes. Relationship direction shows the tag definition that is to be replaced during migration.
2. Replace stereotype relationship is drawn between two different stereotypes. Relationship direction shows the stereotype that is to be replaced during migration.
3. Replace type relationship is drawn between concrete elements. It replace the type of element dependent on the direction of relationship.

The image below shows data mapping rules for Performance Parameter Type element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Migration basics

As shown in the figure "Migration basics", *PerformanceParameterType [Property]* stereotype from DoDAF profile is marked to be replaced with *PerformanceParameter [Property]* from UPDM profile. Also threshold tag definition owned by *PerformanceParameterType* should be changed to *maxValue* tag definition owned by Measurement element from UPDM profile.

As shown in the figure "Storing derived tag data to ToDo property", another exceptional case is property value mapping to the ToDo property of the element. *TODO_Owner [Element]* stereotype from UML Standard profile is marked to replace *unitOfMeasure* tag definition owned by *PerformanceParameterType*. This case results to “*DoDAF unitOfMeasure = [value]*” in *PerformanceParameter* element **ToDo** property.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Storing derived tag data to ToDo property

For each DoDAF stereotype migration there are Diagrams created in migration profile. We suggest that you study these diagrams carefully before customizing migration to meet your specific needs.

For each DoDAF stereotype there are Diagrams created in migration profile.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can customize DoDAF migration to UPDM. However, to do so, you need to have some basic knowledge of UML, especially stereotypes. For this reason, we highly recommend that you use predefined migration configurations.</p><p>Customizing DoDAF migration to UPDM requires a basic understanding of how to perform migration. Basic rules and principles of data migration to UPDM can be found in <em>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles\DoDAFtoUPDMMapping.mdzip</em> project. This project consists of DoDAF, UPMD profiles and predefined relationships defining mapping between DoDAF and UPDM stereotypes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ebb6869-e5a2-4156-b7da-2218535d7874"><ac:rich-text-body><p>DoDAF to UPDM mapping rules can be found in <em>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles\DoDAFtoUPDMMapping.mdzip</em> project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Data Migration is based on three types of stereotyped relationships:</p><ol><li>Replace Tagged Value,</li><li>Replace Stereotype,</li><li>Replace Type.</li></ol><p>These relationships are the basics of migration.</p><ol><li>Replace tagged value relationship is drawn between tag definitions of two different stereotypes. Relationship direction shows the tag definition that is to be replaced during migration.</li><li>Replace stereotype relationship is drawn between two different stereotypes. Relationship direction shows the stereotype that is to be replaced during migration.</li><li>Replace type relationship is drawn between concrete elements. It replace the type of element dependent on the direction of relationship.</li></ol><p>The image below shows data mapping rules for Performance Parameter Type element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_basics.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Migration basics</h6><p><br /></p><p>As shown in the figure &quot;Migration basics&quot;, <em>PerformanceParameterType [Property]</em> stereotype from DoDAF profile is marked to be replaced with <em>PerformanceParameter [Property]</em> from UPDM profile. Also threshold tag definition owned by <em>PerformanceParameterType</em> should be changed to <em>maxValue</em> tag definition owned by Measurement element from UPDM profile.</p><p>As shown in the figure &quot;Storing derived tag data to ToDo property&quot;, another exceptional case is property value mapping to the ToDo property of the element. <em>TODO_Owner [Element]</em> stereotype from UML Standard profile is marked to replace <em>unitOfMeasure</em> tag definition owned by <em>PerformanceParameterType</em>. This case results to “<em>DoDAF unitOfMeasure = [value]</em>” in <em>PerformanceParameter</em> element <strong>ToDo</strong> property.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="derived_tag_to_todo.png" /></ac:image>Storing derived tag data to ToDo property</h6><p><br /></p><p>For each DoDAF stereotype migration there are Diagrams created in migration profile. We suggest that you study these diagrams carefully before customizing migration to meet your specific needs.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1649c59-64d9-4e5a-ae97-6617cc8d5f84"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For each DoDAF stereotype there are Diagrams created in migration profile.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965818 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05156: Customizing Perspectives

- page_id: `243965818`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965818/Customizing+Perspectives
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-12T13:04:29.312+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Perspective customization allows grouping of functional features into standard/expert modes. Customization also allows the user to hide unnecessary commands, making a modeling tool more convenient.

To open the **Customize Perspectives**dialog

- From the **Options**menu, choose **Perspectives**and then **Customize**.
- In the **Perspectives**dialog, click the **Customize**button.

There are seven customizable areas in different perspectives:

- Main menu
- Main toolbars
- Diagram toolbars
- Diagram modeling elements toolbar
- Smart manipulators
- Shortcut menu actions
- Create Diagram dialog

Each of these areas has a set of commands, which can be shown in the standard / expert mode or hidden.

To customize the selected area in a predefined perspective

1. In the **Customize Perspectives**dialog, select the perspective and click the **Edit**button near the selected area. The appropriate **Customize**dialog opens.
2. Expand tree sections and select radio buttons beside the items in the tree depending on your choice for **Standard and Expert**, **Expert**only or **Hidden**modes.
3. Click **OK** when you are done.

To switch between Standard/Expert modes, do one of the following

- From the toolbar shortcut menu, select/clear the **Expert Menu Mode**or **Expert Toolbar Mode**check box.
- From the diagram palette shortcut menu, select/clear **Expert Mode**check box.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Perspective customization allows grouping of functional features into standard/expert modes. <span>Customization also allows the user to hide unnecessary commands, making a modeling tool more convenient<span>.<br /> </span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span>To open the <span><strong>Customize Perspectives </strong>dialog</span></span></span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="f5f92290-d2bf-4b0f-9607-00f435b98422"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">From the <span><strong>Options </strong><span>menu, choose <span><strong>Perspectives </strong><span>and then <span><strong>Customize</strong>.</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li data-uuid="84ecd2e2-8a16-46fd-8cf0-95932abdee16"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the <span><strong>Perspectives </strong><span>dialog, click the <span><strong>Customize </strong><span>button.<br /> </span></span></span></span></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">There are seven customizable areas in different perspectives:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="6d6dddae-72a9-409a-90dc-003c27788675"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Main menu</span></li><li data-uuid="231ca3f8-6ea4-44bb-bfb8-aed0359f4d86"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Main toolbars</span></li><li data-uuid="b06b92b1-458c-4589-8351-ac8ce42db1a9"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Diagram toolbars</span></li><li data-uuid="90008e76-61a0-4675-bae6-0e3e766171fd"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Diagram modeling elements toolbar</span></li><li data-uuid="76d8cd70-7430-494e-be50-e16e90a03545"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Smart manipulators</span></li><li data-uuid="464a3b47-2401-4314-9353-6d089a3942a5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Shortcut menu actions</span></li><li data-uuid="ce1f9345-558f-45ed-98c0-f25b28f0a9ef"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Create Diagram dialog<br /></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Each of these areas has a set of commands, which can be shown in the standard / expert mode or hidden.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span>To customize the selected area in a predefined perspective</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="cec79217-6746-4273-af21-c56a443771e9"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the <span><strong>Customize Perspectives </strong><span>dialog, select the perspective and click the <span><strong>Edit </strong><span>button near <span>the selected area. The appropriate <span><strong>Customize </strong>dialog opens.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li data-uuid="0490abf3-5b34-441a-a979-e92a6a316e63"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Expand tree sections and select radio buttons beside the items in the tree depending on your <span>choice for <span><strong>Standard and Expert</strong><span>, <span><strong>Expert </strong><span>only or <span><strong>Hidden </strong>modes.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li data-uuid="8f71eafb-1a0e-4d87-93bb-e25ef374893e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Click <span><strong>OK</strong> when you are done<span>.<br /> </span></span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">To switch between Standard/Expert modes, do one of the following</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="b81297f7-8528-4277-980e-21ba0a71127c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">From the toolbar shortcut menu, select/clear the <span><strong>Expert Menu Mode </strong><span>or <span><strong>Expert Toolbar Mode </strong>check box.</span></span></span></span></li><li data-uuid="23898aa8-3005-4e85-9a16-9a6e6f1f0204"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">From the diagram palette shortcut menu, select/clear </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Expert Mode </strong><span>check box.</span></span></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005627 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 05157: Customizing requirement properties

- page_id: `249005627`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005627/Customizing+requirement+properties
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:45:57.587+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create specific properties in requirements specifications. This will help identify the requirement’s version, complexity, priority, severity, status, and other properties. Use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML Profiling and DSL Guide' space='UPDG'] to extend Requirements with custom properties. Add custom properties to the general properties list by extending the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extended Requirement' space='MED'] class.

Customizing procedure details are described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='UPDG'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='UPDG'] in the UML Profiling and DSL Guide.

To create custom properties

1. In your project, create a Package for your custom properties. How to create a new packageTo create a new package 
In the Containment tree, right-click the element.The element should be a namespace, such as a package, class, or requirement.Select **Create Element**> **Package**.Type a name for the new Package (e.g. Custom Properties).Press Enter.
2. In that package, create a new stereotype. [How to create a new stereotype >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Stereotype#Stereotype-Creatingastereotype)
3. Apply a metaclass Class to the created stereotype. How to apply metaclass for a stereotypeTo apply a metaclass to a stereotypea. In the Containment tree, right-click the newly created stereotype. 
 b. From the shortcut menu, select **Metaclass**. 
 c. In the **Metaclass** dialog, select a Class. 
 d. Click **Apply**.
4. For the stereotype with the applied metaclass Class, create custom properties. How to create custom properties for a stereotypeTo create custom properties for a stereotypea. In the Containment tree, right-click stereotype. 
 b. From the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. 
 c. In the **Create Element** dialog, select **Property**. 
 d.Type a property name and specify a value type.
5. Extend the ExtendedRequirement class using properties created in the stereotype (i.e., you must relate the stereotype with the Generalization relationship to the ExtendedRequirement class). How to extend ExtendedRequirement class by custom propertiesTo extend the ExtendedRequirement class using custom propertiesa. In the Containment tree, right-click stereotype. 
 b. From the shortcut menu, select **Create Relation** > **Outgoing**. 
 c. In the **Outgoing** dialog, select **Generalization**. 
 d. In the **Create New Generalization To** dialog, select the **List** tab, clear the **Apply Filter** check box, and select the *ExtendedRequirement [Class]*. 
 e. Click **OK**.
6. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''], click **Save Project** button and restart your project. 
Your custom properties are added to the general requirement properties. You can assign values to them in the Requirement’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] or in the Properties panel. The following figure depicts how the stereotype related to the *ExtendedRequirement [Class]* looks in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''].

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The *ExtendedRequirement [Class]* extended by the *author* property is shown in the Containment tree and diagram pane.

You can assign values to them in the Requirement’s <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> or in the Properties panel.</p><p>The following figure depicts how the stereotype related to the <em>ExtendedRequirement [Class]</em> looks in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extended_requirement_with_custom_properties.png" /></ac:image><br />The <em>ExtendedRequirement [Class]</em> extended by the <em>author</em> property is shown in the <span class="confluence-link">Containment tree</span> and <span class="confluence-link">diagram pane</span>.</h6><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969592 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05158: Customizing severity levels

- page_id: `243969592`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969592/Customizing+severity+levels
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:33.308+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules > Specifying the validation rule properties
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

You can customize the default severity levels (such as **debug**, **info**, **warning**, **error**, **fatal**) by creating new and and additional levels. For this you must edit the *SeverityKind* [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space='MED']element, located in the UML Standard Profile.

We do not recommend changing the UML Standard Profile.

To customize severity levels

1. 
2. 
3. 
4. 

After you create the custom severity level or modify the default severity, you can select it from from the drop-down list of the **Severity**property when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the validation rule properties' space='']. In the following figure the new severity level named *critical* can be selected as a value of the **Severity** property in the Specification window of validation rule (Constraint element). The value order in the **Severity** drop-down list is the same as they are listed in the *SeverityKind*Enumerationelement.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The custom severity level named critical in the drop-down list of the Severity property in the validation rule specification.

##### editEditing the UML Standard Profile

To edit the UML Standard Profile

1. Open a project.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and enable to show the Show Auxiliary Resources in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] .
3. Right-click the UML Standard Profile Package.
4. Select Project Usages > Open . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='CM']>]]> The UML Standard Profile is now editable.

##### createCreating new or modifying default severity levels

You can create new or modify default severity levels only if the UML Standard Profile is editable. To see how to edit the profile read the .

Each Enumeration Literal created for the Enumeration element corresponds to available severity levels.

To modify default severity levels (Enumeration Literals)

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , click + to expand the UML Standard Profile Package > Validation Profile Package.
2. Double-click the SeverityKind Enumeration element to open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
3. On the left side of the Specification window, select the Enumeration Literals [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='default_severity_levels.png']
4. modifyOn the right side of the Specification window, select severity level from the list: 
 - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. 
 - Click the **Up**/**Down** button to move the severity level up/down in the list. Severity levels are listed in ascending order according to severity importance. - Click the **Clone** button to create the same severity level. The Specification window of newly created Enumeration Literal opens. Type its unique name. 
 - Click the **Delete**button to remove the selected severity level from the model.
5. Click Close . The default severity levels are modified.

To create a new severity level (Enumeration Literal)

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , click + to expand the UML Standard Profile Package > Validation Profile Package.
2. Double-click the SeverityKind Enumeration element to open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
3. On the left side of the Specification window, select the Enumeration Literals [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
4. On the right side of the Specification window, click the Create button. The Specification window of the newly created Enumeration Literal opens.
5. In the Name property, type the name of the new severity level (e.g. critical).
6. Click **Close**. 
The new severity level is created and can be. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### specifyDefining new icon for severity level

You can define a different icon for a custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). For this, you must create a new stereotype and specify its **Icon** and **Metaclass** properties.

To define a new icon for the severity level (Enumeration Literals)

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , right-click the Validation Profile Package, and select Create Element > Stereotype .
2. Type its name (e.g. criticalIcon) and press Enter .
3. Double-click newly created Stereotype.
4. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of Stereotype specify the following properties: - Select the Icon property value, click [ATTACHMENT filename='image_library.png'] to select an image from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Image Library tab' space=''] , or [ATTACHMENT filename='custom_image.png'] to select an image from your file system. - Select the Metaclass property value, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button_in_specification_window.png'] and select EnumerationLiteral. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_icon_for_severity_level.png']
5. Click Close . The new stereotype with a new icon is created and ready to be applied to a severity level. >]]>

##### applyApplying new icon for severity level

After you define the new icon for severity level, you can apply it to custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). This icon is shown when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space=''] of your model.

To apply a new icon to a severity level

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , click + to expand the UML Standard Profile Package > Validation Profile Package > SeverityKind Enumeration element.
2. Double-click the severity level (Enumeration Literals element) to which you want to apply a new icon.
3. In its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , select the Applied Stereotype property value.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button_in_specification_window.png'] and select the newly created stereotype (e.g. criticalIcon) dedicated for this severity level.
5. Select Apply .
6. Click Close . The new icon for the severity level is applied. In the figure below, the criticalIcon stereotype is applied to the new critical EnumerationLiteral element. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_icon_applied_for_new_severity_level.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

For this you must edit the <em>SeverityKind</em> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Enumeration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Enumeration ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element, located in the UML Standard Profile.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cadc8c8-d89b-4462-89b6-3df3573c91a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>We do not recommend changing the UML Standard Profile.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To customize severity levels</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="edit"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Edit the UML Standard Profile.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="create"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create new or modify default severity levels.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="specify"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Define new icon for severity level.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="apply"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Apply new icon for severity level.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li></ol><p>After you create the custom severity level or modify the default severity, you can select it from from the drop-down list of the <strong>Severity </strong>property when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying the validation rule properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specifying the validation rule properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In the following figure the new severity level named <em>critical</em> can be selected as a value of the <strong>Severity</strong> property in the Specification window of  validation rule (Constraint element). The value order in the <strong>Severity</strong> drop-down list is the same as they are listed in the <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_severity_level.png" /></ac:image><br />The custom severity level named critical in the drop-down list of the Severity property in the validation rule specification.</h6><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88d746be-bf68-4add-b19a-efc25d908fbc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">edit</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Editing the UML Standard Profile</h3><p>To edit the UML Standard Profile</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a project.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png" /></ac:image> and enable to show the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Right-click the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Open</strong>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CM" ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to manage project usages >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />The UML Standard Profile is now editable.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f356329-9909-4f1d-a833-6a8370a78627"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating new or modifying default severity levels</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f75f0bb8-b7ea-43b4-af1e-e687498fc49a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create new or modify default severity levels only if the UML Standard Profile is editable. <span class="confluence-link">To see how to edit the profile read</span> the <ac:link ac:anchor="edit"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[procedure above]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Each Enumeration Literal created for the Enumeration element corresponds to available severity levels.</p><p><br /></p><p>To modify default severity levels (Enumeration Literals)</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click + to expand the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>Validation Profile</em> Package.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click the <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element to open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>On the left side of the Specification window, select the <strong>Enumeration Literals</strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ property group]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="default_severity_levels.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="028719e0-b30e-44b4-b318-2ce340166151"><ac:parameter ac:name="">modify</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>On the right side of the Specification window, select severity level from the list:<br />  - Click <ac:image ac:title="Open Specification" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Open Specification" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_specification.png" /></ac:image> to open <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.<br />  - Click the <strong>Up</strong>/<strong>Down</strong> button to move the severity level up/down in the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ea85a93-0d00-4548-8d50-7655576bb1f1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Severity levels are listed in ascending order according to severity importance.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">  - Click the <strong>Clone</strong> button to create the same severity level. The Specification window of newly created Enumeration Literal opens. Type its unique name.<br />  - Click the <strong>Delete </strong>button to remove the selected severity level from the model.</p></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The default severity levels are modified.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>To create a new severity level (Enumeration Literal)</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click + to expand the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>Validation Profile</em> Package.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click the <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element to open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>On the left side of the Specification window, select the <strong>Enumeration Literals</strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ property group]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>On the right side of the Specification window, click the <strong>Create</strong> button. The Specification window of the newly created Enumeration Literal opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Name</strong> property, type the name of the new severity level (e.g. critical).</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The new severity level is created and can be<ac:link ac:anchor="modify"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ modified by using buttons]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_severity_level_in_the_specification_of_enumeration_SeverityKind.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e13b5d38-57f0-462a-8f5b-5a272aa7c4ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="">specify</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Defining new icon for severity level</h3><p>You can define a different icon for a custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). For this, you must create a new stereotype and specify its <strong>Icon</strong> and <strong>Metaclass</strong> properties.</p><p>To define a new icon for the severity level (Enumeration Literals)</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, right-click the<em> Validation Profile</em> Package, and select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Stereotype</strong>.</li><li>Type its name (e.g. criticalIcon) and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click newly created Stereotype.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of Stereotype specify the following properties:<br />  - Select the <strong>Icon</strong> property value, click <ac:image ac:title="Image Library" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Image Library" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image_library.png" /></ac:image> to select an image from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Image Library tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Image Library]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or <ac:image ac:title="Custom Image" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Custom Image" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_image.png" /></ac:image> to select an image from your file system.<br />  - Select the <strong>Metaclass</strong> property value, click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png" /></ac:image> and select EnumerationLiteral.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_icon_for_severity_level.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The new stereotype with a new icon is created and ready to be applied to a severity level. <ac:link ac:anchor="apply"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to apply it >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aae655a1-0d9a-4276-9e43-3e86888e4b9c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">apply</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Applying new icon for severity level</h3><p>After you define the new icon for severity level, you can apply it to custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). This icon is shown when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[representing invalid elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of your model.</p><p>To apply a new icon to a severity level</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click + to expand the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>Validation Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click the <span class="confluence-link">severity level (Enumeration Literals element) to </span>which you want to apply a new icon.</li><li class="confluence-link">In its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, select the <strong>Applied Stereotype</strong> property value.</li><li class="confluence-link">Click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png" /></ac:image> and select the newly created stereotype (e.g. criticalIcon) dedicated for this severity level.</li><li class="confluence-link">Select <strong>Apply</strong>.</li><li class="confluence-link">Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The new icon for the severity level is applied. In the figure below, the <em>criticalIcon</em> stereotype is applied to the new <em>critical</em> EnumerationLiteral element.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_icon_applied_for_new_severity_level.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968332 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05159: Customizing stereotype style properties

- page_id: `243968332`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968332/Customizing+stereotype+style+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:56.539+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols > Stereotype properties
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize properties like colors, line styles, and line width, for your stereotypes. To be able to do it, you need to:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype properties' space='']
2. Once the stereotype is added, customize stereotype style properties either via the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='']** or **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']** dialog.

In the**Project Options** dialog, after specifying the stereotype style properties, click**Apply** to apply changes to existing shapes or the **OK** button to apply changes to the new shapes only.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Customizing stereotype style properties using the Project Options dialog

In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, click**Make Default** after setting the desired style properties for your stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Customizing stereotype style properties using the Symbol Properties dialog

The best practice is to create styles for stereotypes in the Profile, loading it as a project. When a project uses the Profile, styles for stereotypes will also be loaded and used. These styles will be applied right after applying the stereotype to an element and will be used in all diagrams.

Style for stereotype in custom diagram

- The custom diagram wizard allows specifying styles for stereotyped elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Stereotypes option group.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><p class="_mce_tagged_br">Once the stereotype is added, customize stereotype style properties either via the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> or <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog.</p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ec8a5cf-e0da-4f76-b655-ead647229c1b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog, after specifying the stereotype style properties, click<strong> Apply</strong> to apply changes to existing shapes or the <strong>OK</strong> button to apply changes to the new shapes only. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="customizing_stereotype_style_properties.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Customizing stereotype style properties using the Project Options dialog</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82668d2b-6f62-47f6-8cd2-ff570c20f015"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, click<strong> Make Default</strong> after setting the desired style practice is to create styles for stereotypes in the Profile, loading it as a project. These styles will be applied right after applying the stereotype to an element and will be used in all diagrams.</p><p><br /></p><p>Style for stereotype in custom diagram</p><hr /><ul><li>The custom diagram wizard allows specifying styles for stereotyped elements.</li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969674 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05160: Customizing suspect links tracking

- page_id: `243969674`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969674/Customizing+suspect+links+tracking
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:35.062+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Suspect Links
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1398447377

1398447388

1398447375

**On this page**

**53**

1398447378

In addition to tracking the changes directly affecting Requirements linked to other model elements, you can create your own custom suspect link configurations to keep track of the changes applied to selected model elements and their properties at the chosen end (source or target) of the specified dependencies.

A newly created suspect links configuration comes into effect immediately and overrides a configuration previously used in the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Suspect links tracking in the model

##### Customizing tracking

To create a custom suspect link configuration

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , right-click a package to create your configuration in, and select Create Element > Class . Name it.
2. Right-click the configuration [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'], select**Stereotype,**and apply the«SuspectLinkConfiguration»stereotype. To apply the «SuspectLinkConfiguration» stereotype, suspect links tracking must be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes' space=''] in your project (**Analyze** > **Suspect Links** > **Enable Tracking**).If you use Cameo Systems Modeler, create a Block instead of a Class in step 1, remove the «Block» stereotype from the Block, then apply the«SuspectLinkConfiguration»stereotype to that Block.
3. Open the created element's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
4. Specify the following :
  - Tracked Dependency Type
  - Tracked End
  - Tracked Element Type
  - Tracked Element Properties
5. Close the window when you are done and press F5 to refresh your project. 
The configuration is created. Please note that it is not automatically applied to relationships that existed in the model prior to creating a configuration for suspect links tracking. In such a case, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes' space=''] links manually.

##### Suspect Links configuration propertiesConfiguration properties

These are the propertiesyou need to specify for your custom suspect linkconfiguration:

| Property name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Tracked Dependency Type | Specify the types of dependencies to keep track of their source/target element changes. |
| Tracked End | Specify whether the source or the target element of the selected dependencies should be tracked. The target end-related changes are tracked by default. |
| Tracked Element Type | Specify the element types to keep track of their changes. |
| Tracked Element Properties | Specify the properties of the selected model elements to keep track of their changes. If left empty, all element properties are tracked by default. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:ref="40668c93-4092-4f14-9460-e8b14bdced70">following </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:link ac:anchor="Suspect Links configuration properties"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[configuration properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:<ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Tracked Dependency Type</strong></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Tracked End</strong></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fc1057a2-8831-469f-90c2-6f1668618e3f">Close the window when you are done and press F5 to refresh your Please note that it is not automatically applied to relationships that existed in the model <span>prior to creating a configuration for suspect links tracking. In such a case, you need to <ac:link ac:anchor="Solving untracked links issue"><ri:page ri:content-title="Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[start tracking]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> links manually. </span></p></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fb925c4-de00-4013-bb38-ecfdcc7fdbfa"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Suspect Links configuration properties</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Configuration properties</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a243d291-51bf-471e-94f6-d6291bf8c92d">These are the properties</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> you need to specify for your custom suspect link </span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f3899902-2140-4cb8-a4a9-a9ebdf960a10">configuration</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">:</span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 59.8913%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 27.4296%;" /><col style="width: 72.5704%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Tracked Dependency Type</strong></td><td>Specify the types of dependencies to keep track of their source/target element changes.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Tracked End</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>Specify whether the source or the target element of the selected dependencies should be tracked. The target end-related changes are tracked by default. </span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Tracked Element Type</strong></td><td>Specify the element types to keep track of their changes.</td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tracked Element Properties</strong></p></td><td><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fad20b55-a0ba-4081-9bd8-0ce9d4ed8ce4">Specify the properties of the selected model elements to keep track of their changes. ````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965899 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05161: Customizing the conversion options

- page_id: `243965899`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965899/Customizing+the+conversion+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:48.597+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import > Working with Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In addition to the options that you have in the **Import Enterprise Architect File**dialog, several other conversion options exist in the modeling tool, and you can specify it in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options**dialog.

To customize Enterprise Architect Import options in the **Environment Options**dialog

1. On the main menu, click Options > Environment to open the Environment Options dialog.
2. In the options group list, select Enterprise Architect Import . [ATTACHMENT filename='Environment Options.png']
3. The transformation options are classified into two groups: General and Activity element mapping :
  - Options in the General group are the same as those in the Import Enterprise Architect Files dialog.
  - The Activity element mapping group provides options to convert the EA elements to other element types as it is described in the following table:

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Convert EA WriteVariableAction to | To convert EA WriteVariableAction to either AddVariableValueAction or RemoveVariableValueAction. |
| Convert EA WriteLinkAction to | To convert EA WriteLinkAction to either CreateLinkAction or DestroyLinkAction. |
| Convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to | To convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to either AddStructuralFeatureValueAction or RemoveStructuralFeatureValueAction. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In addition to the options that you have in the <strong>Import Enterprise Architect File </strong>dialog, several other conversion options exist in the modeling tool, and you can specify it in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To customize Enterprise Architect Import options in the <strong>Environment Options </strong>dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Environment </strong>to open the <strong>Environment </strong><strong>Options </strong>dialog.</li><li>In the options group list, select <strong>Enterprise Architect Import</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Environment Options.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>The transformation options are classified into two groups: <strong>General</strong> and <strong>Activity element mapping</strong>:<br /><ul><li>Options in the <strong>General </strong>group are the same as those in the <strong>Import Enterprise Architect Files </strong>dialog.</li><li>The <strong>Activity element mapping</strong> group provides options to convert the EA elements to other element types as it is described in the following table:<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ol><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Option</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Convert EA WriteVariableAction to</strong></p></td><td><p>To convert EA WriteVariableAction to either AddVariableValueAction or RemoveVariableValueAction.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Convert EA WriteLinkAction to</strong></td><td><p>To convert EA WriteLinkAction to either CreateLinkAction or DestroyLinkAction.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to</strong></p></td><td><p>To convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to either AddStructuralFeatureValueAction or RemoveStructuralFeatureValueAction.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561374 space=MT version=7 -->
## PAGE 05162: Customizing Units

- page_id: `249561374`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561374/Customizing+Units
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:40:13.230+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Using Units
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Creating a custom Value Type

If you want to use a different [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Type' space='MED'] than our library provides, you can create a custom [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Type' space='MED'] and specify a desired [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Units' space=''] for it from the ISO-80000 package.

To create a custom Value Type

1. Select a package in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] where you want to create a new Value Type.
2. Right-click it and select Create Element .
3. In the opened dialog, search for a Value Type and select it.
4. Type its name. Following syntax is recommended : quantity name[unit name] (e.g. volume[liter]).
5. Press Enter.
6. Double-click the newly created Value Type to open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
7. In the Specification window, specify the Unit property value: 
 a. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
 b. In the **Select Instance Specification** dialog, clear the **Apply Filter** box. 
 c. Make sure the ISO-80000 library is loaded in your project. WarningIf the ISO-80000 library is not uploaded in your project, you can load it directly in the **Select Instance Specification** dialog by clicking the **Load** button. d. Select the required Unit from the ISO-80000 package (e.g., liter).
8. Click Close. The new Value Type is defined in your project and can be used as a type for a Value Property.

##### Creating a custom Unit

If you are unable to find a desired unit you can create a custom Unit. It is recommended that a[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Units' space='']be related to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quantity Kind' space='MED']. Often, equations can be expressed in terms of quantities that include Quantity Kinds without specifying units.

To create a custom Unit

1. From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] palette, expand the **Value Type** button and select the **Unit** button. If you do not see the Unit button, make sure the **Expert Mode** is turned on.
2. Click the mouse pointer on the diagram pane. The Select Classifier dialog opens automatically.
3. In the Select Classifier dialog, find the Unit Block, double-click it to select it, and click OK . The new Unit is created.
4. Type its name (e.g., mile).
5. Double-click the Unit shape to open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
6. In the Specification window, specify: The new Unit is created and can now be specified for the Value Type and then used in your project as a type of Value Property.
  - The **Quantity Kind** property value: 
 a. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
 b. In the **Select Quantity Kind** dialog, clear the **Apply Filter** box. 
 c. Make sure the ISO-80000 library is loaded in your project. WarningIf the ISO-80000 library is not uploaded in your project, you can load it directly in the **Select Quantity Kind** dialog by clicking the **Load** button. d. Select the required quantity from the ISO-80000 package (e.g., distance).
  - The symbol of Unit: a. In the Property group list , select Slots. b. Select the symbol slot and click the Create Value button. c. Type the unit symbol in the Value area (e.g., mi). d. Click Close .

Following are the QUDV library Classifiers that support [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Unit Conversion' space='MMA'].

- SimpleUnit – Base unit that other units can convert to.
- PrefixedUnit – Unit that converts to other units via prefix (for example kilo, mega, etc.).
- LinearConversionUnit – Unit that converts using predefined factor. Formula: ReferenceUnit=value*factor.
- AffineConversionUnit – Unit that converts using a predefined factor and offset. Formula: ReferenceUnit=value*factor+offset.
- DerivedUnit – Combination of several units.

After the classifier is defined, the slot window is available. The Slot window is used to define properties. Following are the properties that support Unit Conversion:

- referenceUnit – A unit to convert to. Can be a SimpleUnit or another ConversionUnit.
- prefix – Selecting a prefix suitable for the unit (only for PrefixedUnit).
- factor – Conversion factor (for LinearConversion and AffineConversion Units).
- offset – Static part for AffineConversionUnits.

##### Derived Unit

Derived units are a combination of several pre-defined units. Creating a Derived Unit follows a different logic than other types of units. Derived Units do not have a referenceUnit slot.

The factor slot is filled with UnitFactors. A UnitFactor is an instance that has a Unit and an Exponent. For example, a DerivedUnit of m/s^2 (acceleration) would have a factor of m^1 (meter with exponent 1) and s^-2 (second with exponent -2).

If the required UnitFactor is not found, then you need to create a new UnitFactor and use it to create new derived units.

To create a UnitFactor

1. Create a new Instance Specification.
2. Set its classifier to UnitFactor.
3. Fill in the values of Unit and Exponent slots.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a derived unit (acceleration).

To learn more about derived units, refer to [OMG SysML v1 Specification](https://www.omg.org/spec/SysML).

**Related pages**

- SysML Block Definition Diagram
- Instance Table
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package import' space='MED']
- Using QUDV model library
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Units' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quantity Kind' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Type' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Property' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966591 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05163: Cutting element names

- page_id: `243966591`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966591/Cutting+element+names
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:36.430+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-cut-element-name', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The element names can be cut if they are too long, which makes the relation map difficult to read.

To cut element names

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] in the Diagram toolbar.
2. Select the Cut Element Names option.

To see the entire name of an element, move the pointer over the element name. A tooltip showing the entire name appears.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The element names can be cut if they are too long, which makes the relation map difficult to read.</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b0a93e26-c193-4133-bace-ef02f45419b9">To cut element names</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Diagram toolbar.</li><li>Select the <strong>Cut Element Names </strong>option.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f601b47d-de99-4300-843a-0078e572e13f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To see the entire name of an element, move the pointer over the element name. A tooltip showing the entire name appears.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cut_element_names.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562480 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05164: CV-1 Vision

- page_id: `249562480`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562480/CV-1+Vision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:33.274+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-1', 'cv-1-vision', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229610075

229610078

229610077

**Description**

The CV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of capabilities. The purpose of a CV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architectural Description. It also provides a high-level scope for the Architectural Description which is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space='']. CV-1 purpose is identical to MODAF StV-1 product usage. The difference between CV-1 model and StV-1 product lies in different concepts and relationships between them.

**Implementation**

CV-1 can be represented using a CV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of CV-1 Vision diagram

229610074

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enterprise Goal' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enterprise Vision' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Vision Statement' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibits' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Desires' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Achieves' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-1 diagram' space='']

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07197cc3-9c0f-4cf6-acd1-addce1ca5e05"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229610075</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68a892a2-105f-43cc-988b-05c6947e17d9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229610078</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab46a6a0-19bb-4be6-adeb-15da759c7fb3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229610077</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The CV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of capabilities. The purpose of a CV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architectural Description. It also provides a high-level scope for the Architectural Description which is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. CV-1 purpose is identical to MODAF StV-1 product usage. The difference between CV-1 model and StV-1 product lies in different concepts and relationships between them.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-1 can be represented using a CV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="CV-1 Vision" ac:alt="CV-1 Vision"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cv_1.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>An example of CV-1 Vision diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e23d0a2e-8906-4a2d-a25d-48591cec8be8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229610074</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Enterprise Phase" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Strategic Phase" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Whole Life Enterprise" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Enterprise Goal" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Enterprise Vision" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Vision Statement" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Exhibits" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Desires" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Achieves" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-1 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562486 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05165: CV-2 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `249562486`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562486/CV-2+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:33.425+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-2', 'cv-2-capability-taxonomy', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229809548

229809550

229809549

**Description**

The CV-2 captures capability taxonomies. The model presents a hierarchy of capabilities. These capabilities may be presented in context of a timeline – i.e., it can show the required capabilities for current and future capabilities.

The CV-2 specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and user requirements.

The intended usage of the CV-2 includes:

- Identification of capability requirements.
- Capability planning (capability taxonomy).
- Codifying required capability elements.
- Capability audit.
- Capability gap analysis.
- Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of user requirements.
- Providing reference capabilities for architectures.

**Implementation**

CV-2 can be represented using a CV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of CV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram

The CV-2 is used to capture and organize the capability functions – required for the vision set out in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space=''].

229809547

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Desires' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-2 diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562491 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05166: CV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `249562491`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562491/CV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:46.355+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-3', 'cv-3-capability-phasing', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

230028042

230028045

230028043

**Description**

The CV-3 addresses the planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time, i.e., capability phasing. The CV-3 supports the capability audit processes and similar processes used across the different COIs by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The CV-3 indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).

The intended usage of the CV-3 includes:

- Capability planning (capability phasing).
- Capability integration planning.
- Capability gap analysis.

**Implementation**

CV-3 can be represented using a CV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To increase the diagram readability, use legends. To learn how to use legends, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space=''] chapter.

The CV-3 can be presented as a table consisting of rows representing Capabilities (derived from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] model) and columns representing phases (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space=''] model).

230028040

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562499 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05167: CV-4 Capability Dependencies

- page_id: `249562499`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562499/CV-4+Capability+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:33.832+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-4', 'cv-4-capability-dependencies', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

230106248

230106251

230106249

**Description**

The CV-4 describes the dependencies between planned capabilities. It also defines logical groupings of capabilities.

The CV-4 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management. In particular, the dependencies and groupings may suggest specific interactions between acquisition projects to achieve the overall capability.

The intended usage of the CV-4 includes:

- Identification of capability dependencies.
- Capability management (impact analysis for options, disposal etc.).

**Implementation**

CV-4 can be represented using a CV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This contrasts with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] model which also deals with relationships between Capabilities; but [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] only addresses specialization-generalization relationship (i.e., capability taxonomy). However Capabilities used in CV-4 are usually created in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] model.

230106247

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-4 diagram' space='']

However Capabilities used in CV-4 are usually created in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /></ac:link> model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="50c4744c-72a3-4fe9-b205-6dd90211bc3a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">230106247</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-4 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562504 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05168: CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping

- page_id: `249562504`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562504/CV-5+Capability+to+Organizational+Development+Mapping
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:46.691+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-5', 'cv-5-capability-to-organizational-development-mapping', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

230194542

230194544

230194543

**Description**

The CV-5 addresses the fulfillment of capability requirements.

This model shows the planned capability deployment and interconnection for a particular Capability Phase. The CV-5 can also be used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.

The intended usage of the CV-5 includes:

- Fielding planning.
- Capability integration planning.
- Capability options analysis.
- Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.
- Identification of deployment level shortfalls.

**Implementation**

CV-5 can be represented using:

- CV-5 table.
- CV-5 spreadsheet report.

To conduct a comprehensive analysis, several CV- 5 can be created to represent the different Actual Enterprise Phases. Each CV-5 represents different Actual Enterprise Phases defined in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space=''].

The CV-5 shows deployment of Capabilities defined in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] to specific organizations.

Deployed or No Longer Used Resources are taken from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''].

Actual Organizational Resources are taken from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''].

The timing is based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space=''] indicating delivery of Capabilities to Actual Organizational Resources, and also the point at which those organizational resources cease to use a particular Capability.

230194541

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Enterprise Phase' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fielded Capability' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562518 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05169: CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping

- page_id: `249562518`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562518/CV-6+Capability+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:06.982+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-6', 'cv-6-matrix', 'cv-6-capability-to-operational-activities-mapping', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

230335922

230335924

230335923

**Description**

The CV-6 describes the mapping between the capabilities required and the activities that enable those capabilities.

It is important to ensure that the operational activity matches the required capability. The CV-6 DoDAF-described Model provides a bridge between capability analyzed using CVs and operational activities analyzed using OVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix. The capability to activity mappings may include both situations where activities fully satisfy the desired capability and those where the activity only partially meets the capability requirement.

The intended usage of the CV-6 includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to operational activities.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

CV-6 can be represented using a CV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities are used as row elements and the Operational Activities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This model is analogous to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space=''] – but provides the interface between Capability and Operational Models rather than Operational to System Models.

230335921

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maps To Capability' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='']

The CV-6 DoDAF-described Model provides a bridge between capability analyzed using CVs and operational activities analyzed using OVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix. The capability to activity mappings may include both situations where activities fully satisfy the desired capability and those where the activity only partially meets the capability requirement.</p><p>The intended usage of the CV-6 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing capability requirements to operational activities.</li><li>Capability audit.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-6 can be represented using a CV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities are used as row elements and the Operational Activities are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping" ac:alt="CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cv_6.png" /></ac:image></p><p>This model is analogous to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> – but provides the interface between Capability and Operational Models rather than Operational to System Models.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ffd540e-f1d0-42ac-a4cc-2d4c5f9cb502"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">230335921</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Maps To Capability" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="350a1f46-7270-46af-a78c-7315d9a1eb74" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562524 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05170: CV-7 Capability to Services Mapping

- page_id: `249562524`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562524/CV-7+Capability+to+Services+Mapping
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:07.154+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-7', 'cv-7-capability-to-services-mapping', 'cv-7-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

231781635

231781637

231781636

**Description**

The CV-7 describes the mapping between the Capabilities required and the Capability Configuration that enable those capabilities.

It is important to ensure that the Capability Configurations match the required Capability. The CV-7 provides a bridge between Capability analyzed using CVs and Capability Configurations analyzed using SVs. Specifically, it identifies how Capability Configurations can be performed using various available Capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5a which maps system functions to Operational Activities. The Capability to Capability Configuration mappings may include both situations where a Capability Configuration fully satisfies the desired Capability and those where the Capability Configuration only partially meets the Capability requirement.

The intended usage of the CV-7 includes:

- Tracing Capability requirements to Capability Configuration s.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

CV-7 can be represented using a CV-7 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. Capability Configurations are used as row elements and Capabilities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This model is analogous to the SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix – but provides the interface between Capability and System Models rather than Operational to System Models.

231781634

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibits' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maps To Capability' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='MED']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='']

The CV-7 provides a bridge between Capability analyzed using CVs and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Capability Configurations</span> analyzed using SVs. Specifically, it identifies how <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Capability Configuration</span>s can be performed using various available Capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5a which maps system functions to Operational Activities. The Capability to <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Capability Configuration</span> mappings may include both situations where a <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Capability Configuration</span> fully satisfies the desired Capability and those where the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Capability Configuration</span> only partially meets the Capability requirement.</p><p>The intended usage audit.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-7 can be represented using a CV-7 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Capability Configuration</span>s are used as row elements and Capabilities are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="CV-7 Capability to Services Mapping" ac:alt="CV-7 Capability to Services Mapping"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cv_7.png" /></ac:image></p><p>This model is analogous to the SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix – but provides the interface between
