<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967364 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05205: Defining layout template

- page_id: `243967364`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967364/Defining+layout+template
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:14:19.857+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

57880485

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

57880487

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

57880486

If you want to reuse the style and layout of Class with its Ports in another diagram, you need to define the appearance of the Class, the style, and position of its Ports in the Class diagram. It means that you define a new layout template. When defining the appearance of the Class, you can change its color, font, size, layout, and other style properties. To do this, follow the procedure below.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Only one Class shape with its Ports can be defined in one layout template diagram.

To define the layout template

1. Create the Class diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']]]>
2. Create a new or represent an existing Class with its Ports on the diagram pane.
3. Specify the appearance of all shapes by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']Symbol Properties dialog and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] .
4. Set the positions of Ports on Class shape manually. 
The appearance of Class and its Ports shapes are defined (see the figure below). [IMAGE alt='' src='']The comparison of default and defined appearance of the Modem Card Block, style, and the position of its ports. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.

#### INFO: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

- From the version 19.0 SP1, you can define the appearance and layout of a Part Property with its Ports in the Composite Structure Diagram and set this diagram as layout template.
- If a Composite Structure Diagram displays several Part Properties, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating layout template' space=''] directly from a particular Part Property. It's appearance and layout will be copied to the template automatically.

57880484

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figure of this page is the **Modem Cable** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1481817717837&api=v2).*

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968764 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 05206: Defining properties of the customized element to be spell checked

- page_id: `243968764`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968764/Defining+properties+of+the+customized+element+to+be+spell+checked
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-09T15:29:54.839+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Spelling Checker
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can check both DSL customization classes and their properties. You can choose which properties of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Elements' space='UPDG'] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']with a «Customization» [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED']) to be spell checked.

You accomplish this by defining a customization class [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing tagged value' space='MED']*checkSpelling* value, found in the properties tag group. By creating a value for this tag, you can choose String properties to check spelling for. By default, there are no properties marked as checkable.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing tagged value' space='MED']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spelling Checker' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can check both DSL customization classes and their properties. You can choose which properties of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDG" ri:content-title="Customization Elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customized element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>with a «Customization» <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) to be spell checked.</p><p>You accomplish this by defining a customization class <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Editing tagged value" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tag ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em>checkSpelling</em> value, found in the properties tag group. By creating a value for this tag, you can choose String properties to check spelling for. By default, there are no properties marked as checkable.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="8846de97-d471-4c9d-8adc-5f6433e15445"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Editing tagged value" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="75f757a6-f50b-4e54-a1ca-bbeb27b839bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Spelling Checker" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566449 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05207: Definitions

- page_id: `249566449`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566449/Definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:58:26.247+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > Common BPMN Elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Definitions element is a root model in a business modeling project. This element is the outermost containing object for all BPMN elements. It defines the visibility scope and the namespace for all of the BPMN elements in a model.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Definitions element in Containment tree

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Definitions element is a root model in a business modeling project. This element is the outermost containing object for all BPMN elements. It defines the visibility scope and the namespace for all of the BPMN elements in a model.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="definition.png" /></ac:image></strong></p><h6>Definitions element in Containment tree</h6><p><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563196 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05208: Deleting actual projects from NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249563196`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563196/Deleting+actual+projects+from+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:19.363+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To delete an actual project from the chart

1. Select an actual project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click **Delete From Chart**. Actual Sub Projects can only be deleted from the model.

To delete an actual project from the model

1. Select an actual project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Delete .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To delete an actual project from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete From Chart</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b8e8cd0-4702-4d6d-97cb-d1b8d236e815"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Actual Sub Projects can only be deleted from the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To delete an actual project from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243973806 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05209: Deleting and executing Macros

- page_id: `243973806`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243973806/Deleting+and+executing+Macros
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T19:18:21.576+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Macro engine
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

851813206

851813217

851813204

**On this page:**

851813216

You can click the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** or **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** button in the **Organize Macros** dialog to delete or execute a selected macro. You can also press the pre-designed mnemonic keys to delete or run a macro as described in****Organizing Macros Dialog Mnemonic Keys below.

##### Deleting a Macro

To delete a macro from the **Organize Macros** dialog

1. Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros to open the Organize Macros dialog.
2. Select a macro from the table and either click Delete or press Alt + D . A dialog will open, asking whether you want to delete the macro, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. Click **Yes** and the macro will be deleted from the **Organize Macros** dialog.

##### Executing a Macro

To execute a macro from the **Organize Macros** dialog

1. Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros The Organize Macros dialog will open, see figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='organize-macros.png']
2. Select a macro from the table and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . After the macro has been executed, a message will open: The macro <macro name> has been executed .
3. If you have the parameters in the **Macro Information** dialog, you need to specify the value in the **Macro Arguments** dialog, see figure below, before running the macro. For an array datatype, you need to click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the **Value** column in the **Macro Arguments** dialog and enter each value into each line. The value of the first line will be the value in array index 0.An ElementPath is a Qualified Name. You can find this information in the specification dialog of each element, see figure below.The **Macro Arguments** dialog, see the figure below, will be displayed if the**[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box is not selected.If you want to save argument values in the **Macro Arguments** dialog, you need to select the **Set as default values** check box before you click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- You can also execute a macro from the main browser by pressing the shortcut keys that you have defined in the Organize Macros dialog.
- You can only run macro one at a time.
- If there is an error while running a macro, for example, syntax error, the following message will open: <Modeling tool name> cannot execute the <macro language> macro, please make sure that <path, filename, extension> is correct. <error description> .
- If the modeling tool cannot find a macro file in the location that you have specified in the Open dialog, the following message will open: <Modeling tool name> cannot find the macro: <path, filename, extension> .

##### Organizing Macros Dialog Mnemonic Keys

Macro Engine also provides mnemonic keys to add, edit, delete, and run a macro from the **Organize Macros** dialog. The table below shows the **Organize Macros** dialog mnemonic keys and their function.

*Table listing of Organize Macros Dialog Mnemonic Keys*

| Mnemonic keys | Button | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Alt + A |  | To add a macro in the Macro Information dialog. |
| Alt + E |  | To edit a macro in the Macro Information dialog. |
| Alt + D |  | To delete a macro from the Organize Macros dialog. |
| Alt + O |  | Code To edit source code in Macro Editor. |
| Alt + R |  | To run a macro from the Organize Macros dialog. |
| Alt + C |  | To click the Close button. |
| Alt + H |  | To click the Help button. |

- You can click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the Organize Macros dialog, see figure above, to edit and save source code in the Macro Editor dialog, see figure below.
- You can click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the source code or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to run the macro in the Macro Editor dialog, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566949 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05213: Dependency

- page_id: `249566949`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566949/Dependency
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:25.363+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Supportive Diagrams Concepts > Process Definition Diagram > BPMN Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1488402433

1488402435

1488402435

**Description**

A Dependency indicates a semantic relationship between two model elements (or two sets of model elements). Dependency indicates a situation in which a change to a supplier (target) element may require a change to a client (source) element in the dependency.

A Dependency is shown as a dashed arrow between model elements. The model element at the start point of the arrow (the client element) depends on the model element at the arrowhead (the supplier element). The arrow can be labeled with an optional individual name.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1488402432

**Related element**

- BPMN Process

**Related diagram**

- Process Definition Diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Dependency indicates a situation in which a change to a supplier (target) element may require a change to a client (source) element in the dependency.</p><p>A Dependency is shown as a dashed arrow between model elements. The model element at the start point of the arrow (the client element) depends on the model element at the arrowhead (the supplier element). The arrow can be labeled with an optional individual name.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dependency.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="059774f3-a82c-447e-a007-6dbd72ad9663"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1488402432</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related element</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Process">BPMN Process</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Process+Definition+Diagram">Process Definition Diagram</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966477 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 05214: Dependency Matrix

- page_id: `243966477`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966477/Dependency+Matrix
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-10T12:46:11.278+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Demo introducing Dependency Matix.

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The Dependency Matrix is a quick method for representing dependencies compactly. Diagrams, UML elements, and extended UML elements serve as row and column entries. The cells in the matrix show where these elements are associated (related).

Dependency matrices can show dependency criteria, such as

- UML relationships
- BPML, SysML v1, UPDM, and other DSL relationships
- Semantic dependencies (dependencies through properties)
- Relationships through tags (tags allow for relating UML elements that cannot be represented on the same diagram)
- Transitive relationships (through the OCL or meta chain)

Dependency matrices are useful for

- Quickly visualizing and creating dependency criteria.
- Compactly visualizing relations of a large system whose system relations cannot be represented by a diagram on a single sheet of paper due to the size of the system.
- Studying relations from a particular scope and type of element by filtering the unimportant data. Hierarchical columns allow for browsing directly in the created matrix through the model scope and visualize the required area.
- Showing relations that cannot be represented on diagrams, such as a representation (a class by the lifeline), a behavior representation in other diagrams, an operation representation by Call Behavior Action, Use Case relations with describing activities through the property Owned Behavior, etc. The semantic dependency matrix is needed for deeper model analysis. The Dependency Matrix allows the representation of any kind of relations through element properties.

To learn about Dependency Matrix properties, please click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new dependency matrix type' space='UPDG'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01f32448-f2c6-483d-b046-12f186af6ed0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Demo introducing Dependency Matix.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4401ba9-3355-484d-a927-d0252bd8340b"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7Utc3D2vU1I" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01079131-6990-4c53-b5b2-3ee1cdc343d1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The Dependency Matrix is a quick method for representing dependencies compactly. Diagrams, UML elements, and extended UML elements serve as row and column entries. The cells in the matrix show where these elements are associated (related).</p><p>Dependency matrices can show dependency criteria, such as</p><ul><li data-uuid="540837f3-6e04-4f6d-8a77-878b14704f01">UML relationships</li><li data-uuid="ac4e49ee-e816-414c-8265-2c26adfad653">BPML, SysML v1, UPDM, and other DSL relationships</li><li data-uuid="2ca43f87-f1e0-4531-b740-f6d29af03d6f">Semantic dependencies (dependencies through properties)</li><li data-uuid="db395e70-4a15-438c-b01d-3b49cd5f016d">Relationships through tags (tags allow for relating UML elements that cannot be represented on the same diagram)</li><li data-uuid="58d8ed6a-937d-4bdf-9071-e1c91ae16712">Transitive relationships (through the OCL or meta chain)</li></ul><p>Dependency matrices are useful for</p><ul><li data-uuid="1a40e542-7051-4e59-be2c-39ec95089727">Quickly visualizing and creating dependency criteria.</li><li data-uuid="9c0d5a4f-1b96-49e9-add3-085df6b0d406">Compactly visualizing relations of a large system whose system relations cannot be represented by a diagram on a single sheet of paper due to the size of the system.</li><li data-uuid="a5ceb5fe-367e-41c0-8a4a-1432bdb9a48b">Studying relations from a particular scope and type of element by filtering the unimportant data. Hierarchical columns allow for browsing directly in the created matrix through the model scope and visualize the required area.</li><li data-uuid="f38ef2ec-9400-4cf0-a7e3-2229cab368ef"><p>Showing relations that cannot be represented on diagrams, such as a representation (a class by the lifeline), a behavior representation in other diagrams, an operation representation by Call Behavior Action, Use Case relations with describing activities through the property Owned Behavior, etc. The semantic dependency matrix is needed for deeper model analysis. The Dependency Matrix allows the representation of any kind of relations through element properties.</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="525fa5a5-ef0d-406e-9c44-f625e82fc12d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn about Dependency Matrix properties, please click <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDG" ri:content-title="Creating new dependency matrix type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 05215: Dependency Matrix Criteria area

- page_id: `243966562`

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966562/Dependency+Matrix+Criteria+area
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:35.099+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Dependency Matrix environment
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To select the specified value in the Model Browser or to open its Specification window, you can right-click on the value boxes (except for the Dependency Criteria) in the **Criteria** area.

See the descriptions of the dependency matrix Criteria area in the following table.

| Element Name | Element Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Row Element Type | Text box with | Click the ... button to select an element type or multiple element types to show in rows of the dependency matrix. |
| Column Element Type | Text box with | Click the ... button to select an element or multiple element types to show in columns of the dependency matrix. |
| Row Scope | Text box with | Click the ... button to define a scope of the model (packages/profiles) from which elements should be displayed in rows of the dependency matrix.Click to to open the Query dialog and specify the criteria that define row scope. |
| Column Scope | Text box with | Click the ... button to define a scope of the model (packages/profiles) from which elements should be displayed in columns of the dependency matrix.Click to to open the Query dialog and specify the criteria that define column scope. |
| Dependency Criteria | Text box with | Click the ... button to define what relationships between row and column elements you wish to display in the dependency matrix cells. |
| Direction | Drop-down list | Select a direction of relationships for the relationship analysis and representation in the dependency matrix. Be aware that new relationships will be created with the selected direction. |
| Show Elements | Drop-down list | Select to show only related (by a selected dependency criteria), only non-related, or all elements. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966565 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05217: Dependency Matrix shortcut menu

- page_id: `243966565`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966565/Dependency+Matrix+shortcut+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:05.300+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Dependency Matrix environment
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open the dependency matrix shortcut menu

- Right-click an empty space on one of the following [ATTACHMENT filename='Dep_matrix_shortcut_menu.png']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix Criteria area' space='']
  - Matrix pane

| Command | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Specification (Enter) | Opens the of a dependency matrix. |
| Go To | Navigates back or forward to the diagram that was open in the current diagram tab, finds and navigates to diagrams wherein the matrix is used, or to the matrix owner in the , or to a hyperlinked object, or to an element to which the selected element is related through a traceability relationship. |
| Select in Containment Tree (Alt+B) | Selects the dependency matrix in the of the . |
| Related Elements | Allows selecting whether to create a for the matrix or search for model elements which depends on the matrix or are used by the matrix. |
| Refactor | the matrix to a diagram indicated by the user. |
| Tools | Opens a list of that are available for the matrix. |
| Print Active Diagram | Prints a matrix. |
| Show Tabs in Full Screen (F11) | Shows a matrix in the full screen mode. Click Close Full Screen to return to the previous view. |
| Close Tab (Ctrl+F4) | Closes an active matrix. |
| Close All Tabs But Current (Ctrl+Shift+F4) | Closes all diagrams except an active one. |
| Close All Tabs(Ctrl+Alt+F4) | Closes all opened diagrams. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 05218: Dependency Matrix tool

- page_id: `243971284`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971284/Dependency+Matrix+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:09.866+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352667888

1352667888

1352667888

The Dependency Matrix tool allows templates to:

- Access data from Dependency Matrix.
- Use diagrams to get data from Dependency Matrix.
- Get row nodes and elements.
- Get column nodes and elements.
- Get dependencies in a cell (between specific row and column nodes).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Dependency Matrix diagram.

Like other Custom tools, the Generic Table tool '*dependencymatrixtool.jar*' must be presented in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom tools and the installation, see section *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom tool' space='']*.

To import the Dependency Matrix tool to a template, type the following code in the template:

```text

```

1352667887

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971286 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05219: Dependency matrix tool API

- page_id: `243971286`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971286/Dependency+matrix+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:09.972+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Dependency Matrix tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352819143

1352819146

1352819140

**On this page**

4

1352819146

Dependency Matrix tool allows templates to access data from a Dependency Matrix, use diagrams to get data, row elements, column names, or relations between row and column elements from the matrix.

You can use the following Dependency Matrix tool API in the template.

**Class DependencyMatrixTool**

Use the following methods to use diagrams to get data.

- +getMatrix( diagram : Diagram) : Matrix
- +getMatrix( diagramName : String) : Matrix

**Class Matrix**

Use the following methods to get matrix row elements or nodes, column elements or nodes, and dependencies between these nodes/elements.

- +getRowNodes() : List<ElementNode>
- +getColumnNodes() : List<ElementNode>
- +getCellDependencies( rowNode: ElementNode, columnNode: ElementNode)
- +getRowElements() : List<Element>
- +getColumnElements() : List<Element>
- +getRelation( row: Element, column : Element) : List<Relation>

**Class Relation**

Describes dependency in a Dependency Matrix cell. Use the following methods for class relations.

- +getName() : String
- +getElement() : Element
- +getSemanticType() : String
- +getDirection() : String

##### Using Diagrams to Get Data from Dependency Matrix

A Dependency Matrix is a special diagram. You can retrieve it using the $Diagram variable. Thus, every method must accept a diagram instance or a diagram’s name. Use the following methods to return a Dependency Matrix instance. You can retrieve rows and columns from the Dependency Matrix instances.

##### Getting Dependency Matrix Instances from Diagram Elements

**getMatrix(diagram : Diagram) : Matrix** 
To get a Dependency Matrix instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – a diagram element

Return an instance of Dependency Matrix.

For example:

```text

```

##### Getting Dependency Matrix Instances from Diagram Names

**getMatrix(diagramName : String) : Matrix** 
To get a Dependency Matrix instance from a specified diagram’s name.

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – a diagram’s name

Return a Dependency Matrix instance.

Example code:

```text

```

##### Getting Rows and Columns

The Matrix consists of rows and columns. Each row or column is an instance of ElementNode object. Each ElementNode represents a single model Element. There might be multiple ElementNodes for the same model Element in a Dependency Matrix diagram, since the same model Element can be represented multiple times in a diagram.

##### Getting All Rows

**getRowNodes() : List<ElementNode>**

Use this method to retrieve a list of rows, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.

```text

```

The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method **getElement() : Element** can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a row.

To print all row elements' names, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

##### Getting all Columns

**getColumnNodes() : List<ElementNode>**

Use this method to retrieve a list of columns, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.

```text

```

The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method **getElement() : Element** can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a column.

To print all column elements' names, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

##### Getting Dependencies between Rows and Columns

**getCellDependencies(rowNode: ElementNode, columnNode: ElementNode)**

Use this method to retrieve the relations in a specific matrix cell, which is identified by provided row and column nodes.

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- rowNode – ElementNode of a row
- columnNode – ElementNode of a column

The returned value is a list of Relations.

The Relation class contains the following methods:

- getSemanticType : String Return the semantic type
- getElement : Element Return a relationship element or null if the relationship is not an element, for example, tag name.
- getName : String Return a relationship name. If the relationship is a tag name, it will return the tag name. If the relationship is NamedElement, it will return the element name; otherwise, return a human name.
- getDirection : String Return the direction.

To print the row, column, and its relationship name, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

To print rows, columns, and relations in a spreadsheet file format, for example, type the following code:

| #import('depmatrix','com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DependencyMatrixTool')#forpage ($diagram in $report.filterDiagram($Diagram,["Dependency Matrix"]))#set($matrix=$depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))$bookmark.create($diagram.name) |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | #forcol ($col in $matrix.columnNodes)$col.element.name#endcol |  |
| #forrow($row in $matrix.rowNodes)$row.element.name | #forcol ($col in $matrix.columns)#if(!$report.isEmpty($matrix.getCellDependencies($row,$col)))->#end#endcol | #endrow |
| #endpage |  |  |

You can retrieve rows and columns from the Dependency Matrix instances.</p><h3>Getting Dependency Matrix Instances from Diagram Elements</h3><p><strong>getMatrix(diagram : Diagram) : Matrix</strong><br />To get a Dependency Matrix instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fb3ec99-7085-4d08-a2ce-b6ee4e90c5aa"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>diagram – a diagram element</li></ul><p>Return an instance of Dependency Matrix.</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1eeac84-576f-424b-a258-933e831a39f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Dependency Matrix"))

````html
#set($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting Rows and Columns</h3><p>The Matrix consists of rows and columns. Each row or column is an instance of ElementNode object. Each ElementNode represents a single model Element. There might be multiple ElementNodes for the same model Element in a Dependency Matrix diagram, since the same model Element can be represented multiple times in a diagram.</p><h3>Getting All Rows</h3><p><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">getRowNodes() : List&lt;ElementNode&gt;</span></strong></p><p>Use this method to retrieve a list of rows, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a742c90-fbaa-40f9-8fc1-fc0ab63d716a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$matrix.getRowNodes()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method <strong>getElement() : Element</strong> can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a row.</p><p>To print all row elements' names, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65eabb1b-3fbe-49bd-bbc9-a58dd91223f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($rowNode in $matrix.rowNodes) 
$rowNode.element.name 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting all Columns</h3><p><strong>getColumnNodes() : List&lt;ElementNode&gt;</strong></p><p>Use this method to retrieve a list of columns, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="778b1227-f91a-4dca-929f-7e764a388888"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$matrix.getRowNodes()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method <strong>getElement() : Element</strong> can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a column.</p><p>To print all column elements' names, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b930c55b-1af1-4d2d-a759-56410c12be65"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($col in $matrix.columns) 
$colNode.element.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting Dependencies between Rows and Columns</h3><p><strong>getCellDependencies(rowNode: ElementNode, columnNode: ElementNode)</strong></p><p>Use this method to retrieve the relations in a specific matrix cell, which is identified by provided row and column nodes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3cfac07-2d4a-4984-9227-d082e318c001"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$matrix.getCellDependencies($rowNode, $columnNode)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>rowNode –  ElementNode of a row</li><li>columnNode –  ElementNode of a column</li></ul><p>The returned value is a list of Relations.</p><p>The Relation class contains the following methods:</p><ul><li><strong>getSemanticType</strong> : String<br />             Return the semantic type</li><li><strong>getElement</strong> : Element <br />             Return a relationship element or null if the relationship is not an element, for example, tag name.</li><li><strong>getName</strong> : String <br />             Return a relationship name. If the relationship is a tag name, it will return the tag name. If the relationship is NamedElement, it will return the element name;              otherwise, return a human name.</li><li><strong>getDirection</strong> : String <br />             Return the direction.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To print the row, column, and its relationship name, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="722e0feb-cfdd-4eea-b81e-ddf75989285c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($row in $matrix.rowNodes) 
   #foreach($col in $matrix.columnNodes)
      #foreach($rel in $matrix.getCellDependencies($row, $col))
         $row.element.name has $rel.name with $col.element.name
      #end
   #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To print rows, columns, and relations in a spreadsheet file format, for example, type the following code:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 90.779%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 53.1113%;" /><col style="width: 40.3155%;" /><col style="width: 6.57318%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><pre>#import('depmatrix','com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DependencyMatrixTool')</pre><pre>#forpage ($diagram in $report.filterDiagram($Diagram,[&quot;Dependency Matrix&quot;]))</pre><pre>#set($matrix=$depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))</pre><pre>$bookmark.create($diagram.name)</pre></td><td><br /></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="1" data-highlight-colour="grey"><pre>#forcol ($col in $matrix.columnNodes)$col.element.name#endcol</pre></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><pre><br /></pre><pre><br /></pre><pre>#forrow($row in $matrix.rowNodes)$row.element.name</pre></td><td><pre>#forcol ($col in </pre><pre>$matrix.columns)#if(!$report.isEmpty($matrix.getCellDependencies($row,$col)))</pre><pre>-&gt;#end#endcol</pre></td><td colspan="1"><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><pre>#endrow</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>#endpage</pre></td><td><br /></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966556 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 05220: Dependency Matrix toolbar

- page_id: `243966556`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966556/Dependency+Matrix+toolbar
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:51:06.692+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Dependency Matrix environment
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See the detailed descriptions of the dependency matrix toolbar buttons in the following table.

| Button | Button name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Back (Alt+Left) | Navigates back to the previous diagram that was open in the current diagram tab. |
|  | Forward (Atl+Right) | Navigates forward to the diagram in the current diagram tab. |
|  | Select in Containment Tree (Alt+B) | Opens the in the and selects the current diagram or symbol in the Containment tree. |
|  | Delete (Ctrl+D) | Deletes the selected element from the matrix and model.If you undo the deletion, you should refresh the matrix to make the deleted elements appear in the matrix again. |
|  | Remove From Matrix (Delete) | Removes the selected element from the matrix. The selected element is not removed from the model. |
|  | Change Axes | Transposes rows and columns. |
|  | Export | Exports a matrix as a Comma Separated Values ( .csv) file. The file can be opened with Microsoft Excel. |
|  | Refresh | Refreshes the matrix after updating the criteria or data. |
|  | Validate Diagram | Validates the matrix against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored. |
|  | Options | Expands the menu with other options. |
|  | Specification - opens the matrix Specification window wherein you can specify matrix properties.Legend Location - lists legend location options and selection to not show it.Include Additional Content - includes additional content of elements that fall within the specified row/column scope. For example, if a Smart Package comes within the scope of the dependency matrix, its content is collected and represented in the matrix. |  |
|  | Suppress/Expand Criteria Area | Suppresses or expands the matrix Criteria area. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
