The diagram frame is primarily used in cases where the diagrammed element has graphical border elements (like ports for classes and components, entry/exit points on state machines).</p><p>By default the diagram frame is displayed on the new diagram pane. The frame is a rectangle in all diagrams, except state machine and activity. State machine and activity diagram frames have rounded corners.</p><p>The frame can be re-sized manually by dragging its corners or borders.</p><p><br /></p><p>To hide the diagram frame, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the diagram and on the shortcut menu, cancel the selection of the <strong>Show Diagram Frame</strong>.</li><li>Open the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog and set the <strong>Show Diagram Frame</strong> property to false.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the diagram frame properties, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Project Options dialog &gt; Symbol styles &gt; Diagram &gt; Diagram Frame options group, change the desired options.</li><li>In the<strong style="line-height: 1.42857;"> Diagram Properties</strong> dialog &gt; <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Diagram Frame</strong> property group, change the properties.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To hide the icon on the diagram frame</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog, change the <strong>Show Stereotypes</strong> property value to <strong>Text</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Diagram frame" ac:alt="Diagram frame"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_frame.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p>When a new diagram is created, the diagram stereotype icon is displayed on the diagram frame header by default, though you can change it to the context stereotype icon.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display the context stereotype icon on the diagram frame</p><hr /><ul><li><p>In the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog &gt; <strong>Diagram Frame</strong> property group, change the <strong>Use Stereotype</strong> property value to <strong>Context</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d06be97e-e462-46dc-9301-37ccbd2bd6c3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Use Stereotype</strong> property takes effect only in case the <strong>Show Stereotypes</strong> property value is <strong>Text and Icon</strong> or <strong>Icon</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">There is a possibility to show the abbreviation of a diagram type instead of the full diagram type in the diagram frame header.</span></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display the abbreviated diagram type</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog &gt; <strong>Diagram Frame</strong> property group, select the <strong>Show Abbreviated Type</strong> check box.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The abbreviated diagram types are listed in the following table.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th>Diagram name</th><th>Abbreviation</th></tr><tr><td>Use Case</td><td>uc</td></tr><tr><td>Communication</td><td>comm</td></tr><tr><td>Sequence</td><td>sd</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">State Machine</td><td colspan="1">stm</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Activity</td><td colspan="1">sct</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Composite Structure</p></td><td colspan="1">cs</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b00deff-4f95-4549-801f-2894bb2b2fa2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">568201750</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Note" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967441 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05232: Diagram image export

- page_id: `243967441`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967441/Diagram+image+export
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:44:49.649+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page:**

3

Before exporting a diagram, you should specify the Image Export options in the **Project Options** dialog. You can also make additional image export changes in the**Environment Options** dialog. These options are applicable to all operations dedicated to exporting or previewing diagrams, such as:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Systems Synthesis' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving as image' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Printing' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Printing' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing Cameo Collaborator documents' space='MCS']

##### Specifying Project Options for diagram image export

Via the Image Export options in the **Project Options** dialog, you can specify the dimensions for the exported diagram, what should be done if these dimensions are exceeded, and the time it can take to build it (the latter applies only to tables and matrices). The changes you make in the **Project Options** dialog apply only to a specific project.

To specify project options for diagram image export

- In the main menu, select Options > Project > under the General group, select Diagrams > modify the options in the Image Export group.

In the table below, you can see the descriptions of the diagram Image Export options and their default values.

| Project Option | Default Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Time Limit to Build Diagram | 60 | Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export diagram without the time limit.The option applies to tables and matrices only. |
| Image Height Limit | 10 000 | Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit. |
| Image Width Limit | 10 000 | Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export diagram without the image width limit. |
| Export Preference When Limits Exceeded | Export Cropped Diagram | Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded.If the Export Preference When Limits Exceeded value is set to Export Cropped Diagram, the previewed or exported diagram is cropped based on the specified dimensions, displaying an incomplete view of the diagram that looks like the image below. |

##### Specifying Environment Options for image export

You can also make additional image export changes in the modeling tool's environment, such as image resolution and compression quality. The modifications you make in the **Environment Options** dialog persist when the application is closed and re-opened and thus apply to all projects.

To specify environment options for image export

- In the main menu, select **Options** > **Environment** > under the **General** group, select**Image Export** > modify the options in the **Image Export** group.

In the table below, you can see the descriptions of the environment Image Export options and their default values.

| Environment Option | Default Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Save Diagram Background in Image | false | Set to true to save the diagram together with its background as an image. Otherwise, the diagram background becomes white after saving the diagram as an image. |
| JPEG Compression Quality | 1.0 | Specify the quality for saving an image as a JPEG file. Set the value to 1.0 to define the highest quality. |
| Use SVG <text> Tag for Text Output | false | Set to true to turn all text into SVG shapes during the image conversion. |
| Letter Spacing Reduction in SVG | 3 | Specify spacing reduction between letters when exporting SVG with <text> tag. Use the slider to specify a number between 0 and 10, where 1 means -0.1 px. Increasing the number reduces the spacing between the letters. Enable the Use SVG <text> Tag for Text Output option to use the Letter Spacing Reduction in SVG option. |
| Render SVG Icons as Raster Images in EMF | false | Set to true to render SVG icons as raster images in the EMF output. Otherwise, the SVG icons are rendered as vector graphics. |
| Image Resolution (DPI) | 72 | Specify the measure for spatial printing. |
| Exported Image Size [%] | 100 | Specify exported image scaling ratio in percent. If the set value is higher than 100, then the view is enlarged (zoomed in) before generating an image. Raster image will not lose its quality as additional pixels are introduced. |
| TIFF Color Space | RGB | Specify the color format for the tagged image file format (TIFF). |
| TIFF Compression | LZW | Specify the type for compression of the tagged image file format (TIFF). |

##### Diagram image export notifications and statistics

The following table displays the expected results of specific diagram image export options.

| Export option | Successful export | Diagram does not comply with Image Export options or the export failed |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Save as Image | All diagrams are saved as they are in the modeling tool.The file location is opened. | Single diagram export | Multiple diagrams export |
| The diagram is saved as it is in the modeling tool.The file location is opened. | A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.: Click Details... to see Diagram Image Export Statistics. |  |  |
| MDZipX Export | All diagrams are exported as they are in the modeling tool.The file location is opened. | A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.: Click Details... to see Diagram Image Export Statistics. |  |
| MDZipX Publish | All diagrams are published as they are in the modeling tool.A notification is displayed with the information about successful publishing, e.g.: | A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.: Click Details... to see Diagram Image Export Statistics. |  |

The **Details...** link in the notifications opens the **Notification Window**, which displays **Diagram Image Export Statistics**with the following information:

- Total : the number of exported diagrams.
- Cropped : the number of diagrams exported with limited dimensions if the Export Preference When Limits Exceeded option is set to Export Cropped Diagram .
- **Failed**: the number of diagrams not exported due to the exceeded building time and/or dimension limits if the **Export Preference When Limits Exceeded** option is set to *Do Not Export*. It also displays the number of diagrams whose export failed. The **Failed**and**Cropped** indications are only displayed if there are any cropped or failed diagram images. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Image export as a SVG file

Diagrams and symbols that were created in the model can be saved as images in the Scalable Vector Graphics (*.svg) format via the following options:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Systems Synthesis' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving as image' space='']

###### Disabling SVG image caching

By default, SVG images are cached in the modeling tool. However, you can disable the caching anytime by following the procedure below.

To disable SVG image caching

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located at <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property: -Dexport.svg.without.caching=true
3. Save the file and restart the modeling tool.

However, you can disable the caching anytime by following the procedure below.</p><p>To disable SVG image caching</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em> file, which is located at <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em>.</li><li>In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property: <em>-Dexport.svg.without.caching=true</em></li><li>Save the file and restart the modeling tool.</li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967130 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05233: Diagram name and its context name synchronization

- page_id: `243967130`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967130/Diagram+name+and+its+context+name+synchronization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:21.174+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

568146964

568146967

568146965

The diagram name and its context name are synchronized automatically.

Create an Activity diagram. Type a name for the Activity diagram, for example, Receive. The name of the Activity automatically changes to Receive. And conversely - change the name of the Activity and the Activity diagram name will be changed automatically. This is a synchronization of a diagram name and its context name:

Synchronization works in the following cases:

- Activity and Activity diagram inside.
- Interaction and Communication or Sequence diagram inside.
- (Protocol) State Machine and (Protocol) State Machine diagram inside.
- Class and all available inner diagrams inside.

To turn off the synchronization

- In the Environment options dialog, General > Editing options group, cancel the selection of the Synchronize the diagram name with its context name option.

568146963

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78bfcf3b-4016-4881-be8c-dd5db857c05d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">568146964</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="855a4de3-120c-4f10-948f-eb913959e42c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">568146967</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="240fa403-e162-4103-8233-caaa3b00edb6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">568146965</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The diagram name and its context name are synchronized automatically.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="999cf4b2-be18-4eb2-ac4a-23fbd0bff1e7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Create an Activity diagram. Type a name for the Activity diagram, for example, Receive. The name of the Activity automatically changes to Receive. And conversely - change the name of the Activity and the Activity diagram name will be changed automatically. This is a synchronization of a diagram name and its context name:</p><p>Synchronization works in the following cases:</p><ul><li>Activity and Activity diagram inside.</li><li>Interaction and Communication or Sequence diagram inside.</li><li>(Protocol) State Machine and (Protocol) State Machine diagram inside.</li><li>Class and all available inner diagrams inside.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To turn off the synchronization</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Environment</strong> options dialog, <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Editing</strong> options group, cancel the selection of the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Synchronize the diagram name with its context name</strong> option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cf1d89c-0435-418c-b5ed-a6bc481cb496"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">568146963</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968098 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05234: Diagram Overview

- page_id: `243968098`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968098/Diagram+Overview
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:50.207+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Auxiliary diagram symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionality Availability

490535609

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionality Availability

490535611

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionality Availability

490535610

#### NOTE: Functionality Availability

Functionality Availability

The diagram overview functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

A diagram overview shape provides the ability to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Overviewing other diagrams' space=''] on a diagram pane. When using the diagram overview shape, you can overview [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tables' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relation Map' space=''] on the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of using the Diagram Overview functionality

You can format the diagram overview representation properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

To open the particular diagram's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''], double-click the diagram overview shape. The description of each diagram property appears in the same window. Descriptions are found in the description area at the bottom of the Specification window.

To create a diagram overview shape from the diagram palette

1. On the diagram palette, under the Common category, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-28 at 5.16.31 PM.png'] Diagram Overview button. The Select Diagram dialog opens.
2. Select the diagram for which you want to create a diagram overview shape and click OK .
3. Click the diagram pane outside the dialog. The diagram overview shape is created and shows the full content of the corresponding diagram.

490535608

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Auxiliary diagram symbols' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']

****

****

**Sample model**

A diagram overview sample project in*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\diagram overview.mdzip*.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967077 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05235: Diagramming

- page_id: `243967077`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967077/Diagramming
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:18.533+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When working with diagrams it is helpful to keep the following concepts in mind:

- A shape refers to the notation of a model element, such as a package, class, state, use case, object, etc.
- A path refers to the notation for the various kinds of relationships, such as associations, aggregations, dependency, message, and links.
- Both paths and shapes are defined as symbols.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When working with diagrams it is helpful to keep the following concepts in mind:</p><ul><li>A shape refers to the notation of a model element, such as a package, class, state, use case, object, etc.</li><li>A path refers to the notation for the various kinds of relationships, such as associations, aggregations, dependency, message, and links.</li><li>Both paths and shapes are defined as symbols.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005064 space=MT version=10 -->
## PAGE 05237: Diagrams ownership

- page_id: `249005064`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005064/Diagrams+ownership
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:07:41.654+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Organizing your model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The correct organization of your diagrams in the model ensures model consistency, reusability of required views, and quick navigation in the model.

##### SysML v1 diagram taxonomy

The figure below shows the SysML v1 diagram taxonomy, which reflects how SysML v1 diagrams are organized. You can use nine kinds of SysML diagrams when modeling:

- Requirement Diagram
- SysML Block Definition Diagram
- SysML Internal Block Diagram
- SysML Package Diagram
- SysML Parametric Diagram
- SysML Sequence Diagram
- SysML State Machine Diagram
- SysML Activity Diagram
- SysML Use Case Diagram

The relationship between SysML diagrams is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MED']:

- Activity, Sequence, State Machine, and Use Case diagrams are types of behavior diagrams.
- Block Definition, Internal Block, and Package diagrams are types of structure diagrams.
- Parametric diagrams are types of Internal Block diagrams.
- Requirement diagrams are a category by themselves.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SysML diagram taxonomy.

##### Diagram owners

The owner that a diagram can represent depends on the diagram kind you are creating. All SysML diagrams and their available owners are listed in the following table.

A Behavior diagram must have a context which is always a behavior which owns this diagram.

| SysML Diagram | Owner |
| --- | --- |
|  | , , |
|  | , , , |
|  |  |
|  |  |
| SysML Package Diagram | , , |
|  | Activity |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  | , |

##### Find diagrams in your project

You can find all diagrams of your model by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagrams tab' space=''] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagrams tab' space=''] page describes how to open and use this tab.

##### Working with diagrams

All basic procedures of working with SysML v1 diagrams can be found in the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram context' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram context' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965752 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05238: Diagrams tab

- page_id: `243965752`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965752/Diagrams+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:44.651+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425670349

1425670351

1425670350

INLINE

The Diagrams tab in the Model Browser represents diagrams created in your project. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the **Diagrams** tab, diagrams are by default grouped by their types (see the image above).

To open the Diagrams tab, do one of the following

- At the top of the Model Browser, click the Diagrams tab.
- If the Diagrams tab is hidden, from the **Window** menu, select **Diagrams**. You can change some Diagrams tab properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog in the **Browser** options group.

The Diagrams tab consists of the following two components:

- Diagrams tab toolbar
- Diagrams tree

##### Diagrams tab toolbar

The Diagrams tab toolbar contains the following button:

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all of the current elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only the selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Group by Diagram Name | Groups diagrams into packages by the first symbol of their name when the model contains more than 99 diagrams. |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favoritesNavigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Quick Find | Opens the Quick Find dialog. |

Some of these actions may be performed right in the shortcut menu of the Diagrams tree.

##### Working with model elements in the Diagrams tree

You can work with diagrams in the Diagrams tree using the commands available in the selected diagram's shortcut menu. These are the commonly used commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Open | Opens the selected diagram. |
| Print | Opens the Print dialog. Active for the open diagram only. |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Group by Diagram Name | Groups diagrams into packages by the first symbol of their name when the model contains more than 99 diagrams. |

1425670348

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

/><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagrams_tab.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In the <strong>Diagrams</strong> tab, diagrams are by default grouped by their types (see the image above).</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the Diagrams tab, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>At the top of the Model Browser, click the <strong>Diagrams</strong> tab.</li><li><p class="_mce_tagged_br">If the Diagrams tab is hidden, from the <strong>Window</strong> menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="faa45ec8-915d-4d23-927e-4b425c76be92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change some Diagrams tab properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> in the <strong>Browser</strong> options group.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p>The Diagrams tab consists of the following two components:</p><ul><li>Diagrams tab toolbar</li><li>Diagrams tree</li></ul><h3>Diagrams tab toolbar</h3><p>The Diagrams tab toolbar contains the following button:</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 55.3322%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 6.76768%;" /><col style="width: 20.404%;" /><col style="width: 72.8283%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Button</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Tooltip text</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Collapse_all.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Collapse All</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">C</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ollapses all of the current elements in the Containment tree.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Collapse_selected_recursively.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Collapse Selected Recursively</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Collapses only the selected node and all subnodes in that node.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="20" ac:width="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="group_by_diagram_name.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Group by Diagram Name</p></td><td><p>Groups diagrams into packages by the first symbol of their name when the model contains more than 99 diagrams.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="17" ac:width="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorite_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Favorites</p></td><td><p>Opens the Favorites menu for choosing <br />to do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Add a selected element to favorites</li><li>Open the dialog for managing favorites</li><li>Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="19" ac:width="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_find_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Quick Find</p></td><td><p>Opens the <strong>Quick Find</strong> dialog.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p>Some of these actions may be performed right in the shortcut menu of the Diagrams tree.</p><h3>Working with model elements in the Diagrams tree</h3><p>You can work with diagrams in the Diagrams tree using the commands available in the selected diagram's shortcut menu. These are the commonly used commands:</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 55.4439%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 26.0081%;" /><col style="width: 73.9919%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Command</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Function</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the selected diagram.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Print</strong></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>O<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b24269ec-de6f-407f-8912-d635e02b9b4f">pens the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b24269ec-de6f-407f-8912-d635e02b9b4f">Print</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b24269ec-de6f-407f-8912-d635e02b9b4f"> dialog.</ac:inline-comment-marker> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7875c34d-efa0-411c-a90e-bb3100d636cb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Active for the open diagram only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e2d5e060-c05e-43e8-a3fd-09cd8bdace6f">Select in Containment</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e2d5e060-c05e-43e8-a3fd-09cd8bdace6f"> </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e2d5e060-c05e-43e8-a3fd-09cd8bdace6f">Tree</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Group by Diagram Name</strong></td><td colspan="1">Groups diagrams into packages by the first symbol of their name when the model contains more than 99 diagrams.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="417d0ee5-6ad3-4354-a404-70d81604b260"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425670348</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965828 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05239: Dialog for customizing selected modeling tool area

- page_id: `243965828`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965828/Dialog+for+customizing+selected+modeling+tool+area
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:46.976+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The **Customize <Area>** dialog enables you to customize your own environment, from simple to complex or vice versa. You choose which menus and commands should be displayed and which should be hidden. Customization allows you to make your own working environment.

The name of this dialog depends on the area you select. For example, if you select Main Menu, the dialog's name is **Customize Main Menu**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the open dialog, select each environment element individually and choose the mode this element should be available in. There are three modes:

- Standard and Expert . The selected element will be visible in the Standard as well as in the Expert mode. The Standard mode is less complicated and is meant for standard users.
- Expert . The selected elements will be visible only in the Expert mode.
- Hidden . The selected element will not be shown in any mode.

To assign an element to the mode

1. Select an element.
2. Click the radio button under the desired mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51c1ff3b-199f-4010-95ec-e3be1fc5409f"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Customize &lt;Area&gt;</strong> dialog enables you to customize your own environment, from simple to complex or vice versa. You choose which menus and commands should be displayed and which should be hidden. Customization allows you to make your own working environment.</p><p>The name of this dialog depends on the area you select. For example, if you select Main Menu, the dialog's name is <strong>Customize Main Menu</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Customize Main Menu" ac:alt="Customize Main Menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="customize_main_menu.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In the open dialog, select each environment element individually and choose the mode this element should be available in. There are three modes:</p><ul><li><strong>Standard and Expert</strong>. The selected element will be visible in the <strong>Standard</strong> as well as in the <strong>Expert</strong> mode. The Standard mode is less complicated and is meant for standard users.</li><li><strong>Expert</strong>. The selected elements will be visible only in the Expert mode.</li><li><strong>Hidden</strong>. The selected element will not be shown in any mode.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To assign an element to the mode</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element.</li><li>Click the radio button under the desired mode.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971213 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05240: Dialog tool

- page_id: `243971213`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971213/Dialog+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:08.647+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1358524304

1358524305

1358524305

The purpose of a Dialog tool is to enable report templates to call functions for creating dialogs to interact with users during the report generation process. To modify the report templates, you can use the Dialog tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Class Diagram: DialogTool.

The Dialog tool uses the following methods:

- message This method will create message dialogs.

- confirm This method will create confirmation dialogs and return the Boolean value ‘true’ when you click the OK button or return ‘false’ when you click the Cancel button.

- input This method will create input dialogs and return the input value as a string.

- **sort** 
This method will create Sort and Enable dialogs and return a collection of newly-sorted results.

- **select** 
This method will create a Selection dialog and return a collection of the selected elements in the dialog.

Like other Custom tools, the Dialog tool (*dialogtool.jar*) must be installed in the ‘extensions’ folder of the Report Wizard plugin.

To import a Dialog tool to a template, type the following:

```text

```

1358524303

**Related page**

dialogs.</li></ul><ul><li><strong>confirm</strong> <br />This method will create confirmation dialogs and return the Boolean value ‘true’ when you click the OK button or return ‘false’ when you click the Cancel button.</li></ul><ul><li><strong>input</strong> <br />This method will create input dialogs and return the input value as a string.</li></ul><ul><li><p><strong>sort</strong><br />This method will create Sort and Enable dialogs and return a collection of newly-sorted results.</p></li></ul><ul><li><p><strong>select</strong><br />This method will create a Selection dialog and return a collection of the selected elements in the dialog.</p></li></ul><p>Like other Custom tools, the Dialog tool (<em>dialogtool.jar</em>) must be installed in the ‘extensions’ folder of the Report Wizard plugin.</p><p>To import a Dialog tool to a template, type the following:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ## PAGE 05241: Dialog tool API

- page_id: `243971216`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971216/Dialog+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:08.729+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Dialog tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

815916921

815916923

815916919

**On this page**

32

815916922

#### ‘message’ Method

**message(String message) : void**. This method will create a **Message** dialog.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Message dialog.

#### ‘confirm’ Method

**confirm(String message) : boolean**. This method will create a **Confirmation** dialog. It will return the Boolean value ‘true’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘false’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the dialog.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Confirmation dialog.

#### ‘input’ Method

##### Input dialog with text

**input(String message) : String**. This method will create an **Input** dialog from a message. It will return the input values as Strings when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘null’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the dialog.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Input dialog with text.

##### Input dialog with text and initial value

**input(String message, String initialValue)** : String. This method will create a message and add an initial value in an **Input** dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘null’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Input dialog with text and an initial value.

##### Input dialog with text and initial value Array

**input(String message, Collection options)** : String. This method will create a message and add initial value arrays in an **Input** dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘null’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Input dialog with text and initial value arrays.

#### ‘sort’ Method

##### Sort and Enable dialog

**sort(Collection list) : Collection**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Sort and Enable dialog.

##### Sort and Enable dialog with text

**sort(String message, Collection list)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Sort and Enable dialog with text.

##### Sort and Enable dialog with a default element selection option

**sort(Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with a dialog title

**sort(Collection list, String title, defaultEnabled : Boolean)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with a dialog title. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description

**sort(String message, Collection list)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description and a default element selection option

**sort(String message, Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option

**sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons

**sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled, Boolean showElementIcon)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

#### 'select' Method

##### Default Selection dialog

**select(Collection list) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from a collection. Items in the collection will be listed in the **Selection** dialog. You can select the items and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to return a collection of the selected items or null when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Selection dialog.

##### Selection dialog with a title

**select(Collection list, String title) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog with a specified dialog title. The default **Selection** dialog title is **Selection**. You can select the items and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Selection dialog with title.

##### Selection dialog without selection

**select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from the collection without selecting the items in the dialog. You can specify whether all listed items will be selected or not. The default value for defaultSelected is true. Otherwise, all listed items will not be selected. You can select the items and click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Selection dialog without selection.

##### Selection dialog with description text

**select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Selection dialog with text.

##### Selection dialog with an option for hiding element icons

**select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText, Boolean showElementIcon)**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. With this method, you can also hide element icons in the dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c004677-7e7f-4d5c-ad0f-6041d127cb3f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916921</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62fc515f-d9cd-4ffc-b71e-c3c344e852a0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916923</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae66343e-dc33-476f-8447-53f654f95b19"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916919</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5788a78-023d-417a-bb7d-7e98e39b332d"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91e9ae09-de8b-4f61-a45a-8446283c85cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916922</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2>‘message’ Method</h2><p><strong>message(String message) : void</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Message</strong> dialog. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b8245b9-9013-4a69-acbe-cafa02079d8e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$dialog.message("Click OK to close dialog")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 1- Message Dialog.png" /></ac:image>The Message dialog.</h6><h2>‘confirm’ Method</h2><p><strong>confirm(String message) : boolean</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Confirmation</strong> dialog. It will return the Boolean value ‘true’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or ‘false’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image> in the dialog. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e588332-753e-421d-838c-29368f7edbbd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#if($dialog.confirm("Do you want to generate section A?"))
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 2- Confirmation Dialog.png" /></ac:image>The Confirmation dialog.</h6><h2>‘input’ Method</h2><h3>Input dialog with text</h3><p><strong>input(String message) : String</strong>. This method will create an <strong>Input</strong> dialog from a message. It will return the input values as Strings when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or ‘null’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image> in the dialog. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e8e5c24-a4eb-4a58-8b7e-e7600c0d1129"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($userText = $dialog.input("Enter your name"))
#if ($userText)
Your name is $userText
#else
Please specify your name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 3-Input Dialog with Text.png" /></ac:image>The Input dialog with text.</h6><h3>Input dialog with text and initial value</h3><p><strong>input(String message, String initialValue)</strong> : String. This method will create a message and add an initial value in an <strong>Input</strong> dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or ‘null’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac29d2bc-4673-44ee-a29b-c0e2e2c305f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($userText = $dialog.input("Enter a date", "Sep28,2016"))
Date is $userText]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 4- The Input Dialog with Text and an Initial Value.png" /></ac:image>The Input dialog with text and an initial value.</h6><h3>Input dialog with text and initial value Array</h3><p><strong>input(String message, Collection options)</strong> : String. This method will create a message and add initial value arrays in an <strong>Input</strong> dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or ‘null’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f13fca1d-36c0-41e2-8943-88c619afc6a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($selectedOption = $dialog.input("Choose your favorite fruit", ["Apple", "Orange", "Banana"]))
Your favorite fruit is $selectedOption]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 5- Input Dialog with Text and Initial Value Array.png" /></ac:image>The Input dialog with text and initial value arrays.</h6><h2>‘sort’ Method</h2><h3>Sort and Enable dialog</h3><p><strong>sort(Collection list) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64ff03e1-5242-4f63-867c-a137bab037fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.sort($Diagram))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 6- Sort and Enable Dialog..png" /></ac:image>The Sort and Enable dialog.</h6><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with text</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09fb2a92-95c1-4689-aba8-e1bcf52457b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.sort("Rearrange diagram for presentation report",$Diagram))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 7- Sort and Enable Dialog with Text..png" /></ac:image>The Sort and Enable dialog with text.</h6><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with a default element selection option</h3><p><strong>sort(Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with a dialog title</h3><p><strong>sort(Collection list, String title, defaultEnabled : Boolean)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with a dialog title. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description and a default element selection option</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled, Boolean showElementIcon)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><p><br /></p><h2>'select' Method</h2><h3>Default Selection dialog</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from a collection. Items in the collection will be listed in the <strong>Selection</strong> dialog. You can select the items and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> to return a collection of the selected items or null when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7e72fea-9047-4630-89f9-318454f58595"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($class in $dialog.select($Diagram))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Dialog 1.png" /></ac:image>The Selection dialog.</h6><h3>Selection dialog with a title</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog with a specified dialog title. The default <strong>Selection</strong> dialog title is <strong>Selection</strong>. You can select the items and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image></strong>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aea59cd0-54b8-4b8a-bd4f-9a742dbdef27"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.select($Diagram, "Selection Dialog"))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Dialog 2.png" /></ac:image>The Selection dialog with title.</h6><h3>Selection dialog without selection</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from the collection without selecting the items in the dialog. You can specify whether all listed items will be selected or not. The default value for defaultSelected is true. Otherwise, all listed items will not be selected. You can select the items and click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef295ca9-3dd5-4f57-8a06-269337e8a62b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.select($Diagram, "Selection unchecked Dialog", false))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Unchecked.png" /></ac:image> The Selection dialog without selection.</h6><h3>Selection dialog with description text</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image></strong> to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="283f7f60-238c-446a-ac2f-04dce126994d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.select($Diagram,“Select all feature”, false, "Please select your required element"))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select All.png" /></ac:image>The Selection dialog with text.</h6><h3>Selection dialog with an option for hiding element icons</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText, Boolean showElementIcon)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image></strong> to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png" /></ac:image>. With this method, you can also hide element icons in the dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561255 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05243: Direction prefixes

- page_id: `249561255`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561255/Direction+prefixes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T10:21:43.636+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Defining interfaces using ports > Creating ports
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display and hide the direction prefixes. They help to identify directions of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='MED'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']. Read the following sections to learn how to work with direction prefixes:

33

##### Displaying direction prefixes of Proxy Port

The direction prefix of the Proxy Port helps to identify its direction.

The direction prefixes of Proxy Ports are displayed:

- 
- 
- 
- 

###### compIn Block Compartment

To display the Proxy Port direction prefixes in the Block Compartment

1. Right-click the Block and select the **Symbol Properties**.
2. In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, set the **Show Proxy Port Direction in Compartment** property value to *true*. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### browserIn the Model Browser

The direction prefixes of the Proxy Port in the Model Browser are displayed automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

###### proxy_portOn the Proxy Port shape when its name is displayed inside the shape

To display the direction prefix on the Proxy Port shape

1. Right-click the Proxy Port shapeand select **Position of Labels** > **Name and Type Labels Inside** or**All Labels Inside**.
2. Right-click the Proxy Port shape and select the **Symbol Properties**.
3. In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, set the **Show Direction Prefix Inside Port** property value to *true*. 
The direction prefix is displayed on the selected Proxy Port shape. [ATTACHMENT filename='direction_prefix_on_proxy_port_shape.png']

###### tooltipOn the ToolTip

The direction prefixes of the Proxy Port on the ToolTip are displayed automatically. To see it, move the pointer over the Proxy Port or its name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying combined direction on Proxy Port shape

Combined direction consists of all owned and inherited flow properties and directed features of the Proxy Port. The flow properties direction are shown by default on the Proxy Port shape. If all features have direction "out" or "provided", the combined direction is "out". If all features have direction "in" or"required", the combined direction is "in". Otherwise the direction is "inout".

To include directed features into combined direction of the Proxy Port

1. From the main menu, select **Options** > **Project**.
2. In the**Project Options** dialog, on the right side, click **General** > **SysML**.
3. Set the **Include Directed Features into Combined Direction of Proxy Port** property value to *true*. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Hiding direction prefixes of Flow Property on Part Property shape

To hide direction prefix of Flow property on Part Property shape

1. Right-click the Part property shape and select **Symbol Properties**.
2. In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, set the **Show Flow Property Direction in Compartment** property value to *false*.

##### Displaying direction prefixes of Full Port

The direction prefix of the Full Port helps to identify its direction.

The direction prefixes of FullPorts are displayed:

- 
- On the FullPort shape
- 

###### modIn the Model Browser

The direction prefixes of the Full Port in the Model Browser are displayed automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### fullOn the Full Port shape when its name is displayed inside the shape

To display the direction prefix on the Full Port shape

1. Right-click the Full Port shapeand select **Position of Labels** > **Name and Type Labels Inside** or**All Labels Inside**.
2. Right-click the Full Port shape and select the **Symbol Properties**.
3. In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, set the **Show Direction Prefix Inside Port** property value to *true*. 
The direction prefix is displayed on the selected Full Port shape. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### tipOn the ToolTip

The direction prefixes of the Full Port on the ToolTip are displayed automatically. To see it, move the pointer over the Full Port or its name. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Port</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="combined_direction.png" /></ac:image></span></li></ol><h3>Hiding direction prefixes of Flow Property on Part Property shape</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To hide direction prefix of Flow property on Part Property shape</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the Part property shape and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Show Flow Property Direction in Compartment</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Displaying direction prefixes of Full Port</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The direction prefix of the Full Port helps to identify its direction.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The direction prefixes of <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Full </span>Ports are displayed:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span class="confluence-link"> <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="mod"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[In the Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="full"><ac:link-body>On the Full<span class="confluence-link"> </span>Port shape</ac:link-body></ac:link></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="tip"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[On the ToolTip]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></span></li></ul><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a32ba11-1bb2-4d47-8988-4fc482ab8f20"><ac:parameter ac:name="">mod</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>In the Model Browser</span></span></span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The direction prefixes of the Full Port in the Model Browser are displayed automatically.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="full_port_direction_prefix_in_model_browser.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></p><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="516f5eb7-08b7-41d7-8ce1-dde84a0c07cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="">full</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>On the Full Port shape when its name is displayed inside the shape</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To display the direction prefix on the Full Port shape</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the Full <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Port shape</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> and select</span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Position of Labels</strong></span> &gt; <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Name and Type Labels Inside</strong> or<strong> All Labels Inside</strong></span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the Full <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Port shape and</span></span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">select <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, set the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Show Direction Prefix Inside Port</strong> property value to <em>true</em></span>.<br />The direction prefix is displayed on the selected Full Port shape.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="direction_prefix_on_full_port_shape.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e182e7f4-df59-436e-9170-fac5a3b3bdfc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">tip</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>On the ToolTip</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The direction prefixes of the Full Port on the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ToolTip</span> are displayed automatically.</span> To see it, move the pointer over the Full Port or its name.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="full_port_direction_prefix_on_tooltip.png" /></ac:image></span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965831 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05244: Disabling inbound network activity

- page_id: `243965831`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965831/Disabling+inbound+network+activity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:52:25.961+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Useful information

INLINE

Note that after disabling inbound network activity, you will not be able to open the modeling tool Help or submit an issue.

#### TIP: Useful information

Useful information

If you disable inbound network activity, double-clicking a project file (.mdzip) opens the project in a new modeling tool window.

To disable inbound network activity

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\bin and open the properties file, e.g., <modeling tool name>.properties .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following argument:
3. Save and close the file.

ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Note that after disabling inbound network activity, you will not be able to open the modeling tool Help or submit an issue.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4602c53-adae-4ca0-9514-aa04dc3c211f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful window.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To disable inbound network activity</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em> and open the properties file, e.g., <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following argument:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7702a6ab-72a0-4154-bcc8-c64d588df4f1"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-DDISABLEINBOUNDCON=true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the
