<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563148 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05245: Display all sub capabilities in NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `249563148`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563148/Display+all+sub+capabilities+in+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:18.147+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display all sub capabilities

1. Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.
2. In the NCV-3 chart toolbar, click the Display All button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To display all sub capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.</li><li>In the NCV-3 chart toolbar, click the <strong>Display All</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563146 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05246: Display sub capabilities removed from NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `249563146`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563146/Display+sub+capabilities+removed+from+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:18.077+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The capability name becomes highlighted (the color changes) when one or more its sub capabilities are removed from the chart.

To display only the selected sub capabilities

1. Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the sub capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all sub capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4bd530ef-d938-4850-90ee-b4f73791cab8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The capability name becomes highlighted (the color changes) when one or more its sub capabilities are removed from the chart.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To display only the selected sub capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to restore.</li><li>From the selected capability’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Show/Hide Sub Capabilities</strong> and do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the sub capabilities you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>Select All</strong> to display all sub capabilities.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Apply</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561317 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 05247: Displaying actual Connectors and ports

- page_id: `249561317`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561317/Displaying+actual+Connectors+and+ports
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-09T12:13:43.765+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram > Implied Connectors
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When trying to see the detailed view underneath the implied relation, you can display actual Connectors and ports directly from the implied relation in the following ways:

- Using the Semantic Zoom In button to show actual ports and Connectors by expanding only one level into deep at a time. How to use the Semantic Zoom >>
- Using the Display Underlying Connectors command to show all actual ports and Connectors at once. How to use **Display Underlying Connectors** command >>

##### Using the Semantic Zoom

This feature allows you to view details of the implied relation and display nested ports level by level into deep. The **Semantic Zoom****In** button displays ports and/or Connectors which are underneath the implied relation by expanding only one level into deep at a time. You can use that button until all actual ports and Connectors are displayed and implied relation disappears. The **Semantic Zoom****Out** button collapses displayed actual ports and/or Connectors on the diagram one level at a time.

To display/collapse actual ports and Connectors one level into deep at a time

1. Select the implied relation.
2. From its smart manipulator toolbar select: 
- The**Semantic Zoom In** button ([IMAGE alt='' src='']) to display actual ports and Connectors one level into deep. 
- The**Semantic Zoom Out** button ([IMAGE alt='' src='']) to collapse displayed actual ports and Connectors one level into deep.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying/collapsing all actual ports and Connectors by using the Semantic Zoom In/Out button.

##### Using the Display Underlying Connectors command

You can display all actual Connectors and ports from implied relations at once with one click.

To display all actual Connectors and ports at once

1. Select the implied relation.
2. From its shortcut menu, select the Display Underlying Connectors command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying all actual Connectors and ports by using the Display Underlying Connectors command.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connecting parts through interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hiding direction arrow on port shape' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Autowire Parts' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property path notation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enforce Ports Compatibility mode' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED']

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures on this page is the **Implied Connectors**sample model. Download [ATTACHMENT filename='Implied Connectors.mdzip'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968607 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05248: Displaying and navigating through changes

- page_id: `243968607`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968607/Displaying+and+navigating+through+changes
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:32.803+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['displaying-changes', 'merged-results-navigation', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1421694493

1421694496

1421694494

You can customize the content of the **Merged Result** tree/list as well as navigate through changes in both the **Merged Results** tree/list and the **Specification** panel using the toolbar that is located at the top of the **Merge** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The toolbar buttons for customizing the **Merged** **Results** tree/list and navigating through changes are described in the following tables.

| Button / Shortcut keys | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Accept the selected change and go to the next change | Click to accept the selected element change and go to the next change (might be a property or the element). |
|  | Accept the selected change, its property changes and go to next change | Click to accept the selected element change together with the element property changes and go to the next changed element. |
|  | Accept the selected change, its property changes, all subelement changes and go to next change | Click to accept all changes from both contributors starting from the selected element and go to the next changed element (which is not a subelement). For example, if the root package Model selected, then all changes for the whole project will be accepted. |
|  | Reject the selected change and go to the next change | Click to reject the selected element change and go to the next change (might be a property or the element). |
|  | Reject the selected change, its property changes and go to next change | Click to reject the selected element change together with the element property changes and go to the next changed element. |
|  | Reject the selected change, its property changes, all subelement changes and go to next change | Click to reject all changes from both contributors starting from the selected element and go to the next changed element (which is not a subelement). For example, if the root package Model is selected, then all changes for the whole project will be rejected. |
| Alt + Home | Go To First Change | Click to select the first change.The button is not available if the first change is already selected. |
| Alt + Up arrow | Go To Previous Change | Click to select the previous change. |
| Alt + Down arrow | Go To Next Change | Click to select the next change. |
| Alt + End | Go To Last Change | Click to select the last change. The button is not available if the last change is already selected. |
| Alt + Page Down | Go To Next Conflict | Click to select the next conflict. The button is not available if there are no conflicts. |
|  | Annotate Merged Diagram | Click to turn on showing merged diagram annotations. |
|  | Report | Click to generate a Project Merge differences report in Microsoft Word format. |
| Options menu |  |  |
| Icon | Option | Description |
| > | Filter | Click to open the Select Element Type dialog. Clear the checkbox next to an element that you want to hide in the Merged Result tree/list. |
| > | Show Elements from Used projects | Click to show or hide elements from used projects in the Merged Result tree/list. |
| Show Source | Click to show or hide Source column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is shown. |  |
| Show Ancestor | Click to show or hide Ancestor column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is hidden. |  |
| Show Target | Click to show or hide Target column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is shown. |  |
| Show Merge Result Preview | Click to show or hide Show Merge Result Preview column in the Specification panel. This column shows final changes in the merged project and by default, this column is shown. |  |
| Summary/Legend | Click to show or hide the Summary/Legend panel. |  |
| Reset Windows Configuration | Select to place all tabs and windows to their default positions. |  |

1421694492

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing and managing merge results' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding content of the Merged Result panel' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Inspecting changes in the Change details panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quickly navigating through conflicting changes' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accepting and rejecting changes using shortcut menu' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing and analyzing changes of modified diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating a differences report' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="079d2924-483d-46d2-8f3f-80d2cd2d064d"><ac:parameter <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969444 space=MT version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 05249: Displaying and navigating through differences

- page_id: `243969444`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969444/Displaying+and+navigating+through+differences
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:24:00.505+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['displaying-changes', 'comparison-result-navigation', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the content of the **Differences** tree as well as navigate through differences in both the **Difference**tree and the **Specification** panel using the toolbar that is located at the top of the **Difference Viewer**window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The toolbar buttons for customizing the **Differences** tree and navigating through differences are described in the following tables.

| Button / Shortcut keys | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Expand | Click to expand all nodes in the Differences tree. |
|  | Collapse | Click to collapse all nodes in the Differences tree. |
| Alt+Home | Go To First Change | Click to select the first change. The button is not available, if the first change is already selected. |
| Alt+ up arrow | Go To Previous Change | Click to select the previous change. |
| Alt + down arrow | Go To Next Change | Click to select the next change. |
| Alt+End | Go To Last Change | Click to select the last change. The button is not available, if the last change is already selected. |
|  | Report | Click to generate a project comparison differences report in Microsoft Word format. |
| > | Filter | Click to open the Items Filter dialog. Clear the check-box next to an element that you need to hide in the Differences tree. |
| > | Reset Windows Configuration | Click to place all tabs and windows to their default positions. |

You can also filter the elements displayed in the **Differences** tree by change type. Use the **Display** drop-down list to specify a change type. Be advised that filtering elements by a particular change type displays both elements with the particular changes and elements with the particular owned element changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967306 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05250: Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes

- page_id: `243967306`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967306/Displaying+and+suppressing+compartments+on+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:27.984+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Compartments
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following

- On the diagram, click the shape and click the *Compartments* smart manipulator. Then select or cancel the selection of the particular compartment, for example, Attributes, Operations. The Compartments smart manipulator exists on shapes that have compartment boxes. For example, pins, and initial nodes do not have the Compartments smart manipulator.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- On the diagram, select a shape and click the Suppress <compartment name> smart manipulator. [ATTACHMENT filename='supress_attributes.png']
- On the diagram, right-click the shape and select Symbol Properties . Then select or cancel the selection of the Suppress <compartment name> check box.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>On the diagram, click the shape and click the <em>Compartments</em> smart manipulator. Then select or cancel the selection of the particular compartment, for example, Attributes, Operations.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9656a37f-5122-411f-9ed7-4b5acae41ad2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Compartments smart manipulator exists on shapes that have compartment boxes. For example, pins, and initial nodes do not have the Compartments smart manipulator.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartment_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>On the diagram, select a shape and click the <em>Suppress &lt;compartment name&gt;</em> smart manipulator. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="supress_attributes.png" /></ac:image></li><li>On the diagram, right-click the shape and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. Then select or cancel the selection of the <strong>Suppress </strong>&lt;compartment name&gt; check box.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565296 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05251: Displaying Custom Measurements

- page_id: `249565296`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565296/Displaying+Custom+Measurements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:41.252+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Measurements > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['add-measurement', 'delete-measurement', 'remove-measurement', 'clear-all', 'display-custom-measurements', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display custom Measurements in addition to predefined ones in the <viewpoint name>* Connectivity and Role-based Connectivity tables by . Measurements can be displayed for the <viewpoint specific>** Exchanges. 
When you are adding a new Measurement column, Measurements are collected from the elements used only in the active table. The collected Measurements are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurements dialog' space='']**Measurements** dialog. You can select the desired Measurement which will be displayed as a newly created column in the table.

You can manage custom Measurements byremoving them. 
 
addTo add a column with Measurements

1. On the table toolbar, click **Columns** > **Measurements**.
2. In the**Measurements** dialog, click the <viewpoint specific>** Exchange or <viewpoint specific>** Exchange Item tab .
3. In the selected tab, click to choose the desired Measurement.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

removeTo remove columns with Measurements

1. On the table toolbar, click **Columns** > **Measurements**.
2. In the **Measurements**dialog, click the <viewpoint specific>** Exchange or <viewpoint specific>** Exchange Item tab .
3. In the opened tab, click the **Clear** **All** button to clear all the selected check boxes.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

The Measurement column is automatically removed after doing one of the following actions:

- Removing the <viewpoint specific>** Exchange from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed <viewpoint specific>** Exchange is used by the other <viewpoint specific>** Exchange in the table.
- Removing the conveyed element from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed conveyed element is used by the other <viewpoint specific>** Exchange Item in the table.
- Removing the Measurement Set which includes the Measurement represented by a table column from the model.

*** <viewpoint name>**- Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services, Security (Operational and Resource).

**** <viewpoint specific>** - Operational, Resources, Services.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can display custom Measurements in addition to predefined ones in the &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Connectivity and Role-based Connectivity tables by <ac:link ac:anchor="add"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[adding new columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Measurements can be displayed for the &lt;viewpoint specific&gt;** Exchanges.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> When you are adding a new Measurement column, Measurements are collected from the elements used only in the active table. The collected Measurements are displayed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Measurements dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Measurements</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. You can select the desired Measurement which will be displayed as a newly created column in the table.</span></p><p>You can manage c<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">ustom Measurements</span> <span class="confluence-link">by </span><ac:link ac:anchor="remove"><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">removing them</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>.<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7847d2a2-92d7-4f25-be1f-b874421c7b7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">add</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add a column with Measurements</span></p><hr /><ol><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">On the table <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">toolbar</span>, click <strong>Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Measurements</strong>. </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the<span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Measurements</span></strong> dialog</span></span></span>, click the </span><strong>&lt;viewpoint specific&gt;** Exchange</strong> or <strong>&lt;viewpoint specific&gt;** Exchange Item</strong> tab<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">. </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the selected tab, click to choose the desired Measurement.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done. </span></li></ol><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></p><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08505d56-d8c8-4f19-bdb6-7304ea23017d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To remove columns with Measurements</span></p><hr /><ol><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">On the table <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">toolbar</span></span>, click <strong>Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Measurements</strong>.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span>In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong>Measurements</strong></span> </span>dialog</span>, click the </span><strong>&lt;viewpoint specific&gt;** Exchange</strong> or <strong>&lt;viewpoint specific&gt;** Exchange Item</strong> tab<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the opened tab, click the <strong>Clear</strong> <strong>All</strong> button to clear all the selected check boxes.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="46e39ee0-6028-478a-94fe-36a95b282b78"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Measurement column is automatically removed after doing one of the following actions:</p><ul><li>Removing the &lt;viewpoint specific&gt;** Exchange from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed &lt;viewpoint specific&gt;** Exchange is used by the other &lt;viewpoint specific&gt;** Exchange in the table.</li><li>Removing the conveyed element from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed conveyed element is used by the other &lt;viewpoint specific&gt;** Exchange Item in the table.</li><li>Removing the Measurement Set which includes the Measurement represented by a table column from the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><hr /><p><strong>* &lt;viewpoint name&gt;</strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>- Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services, Security (Operational and Resource).</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>** &lt;viewpoint specific&gt;</strong> - Operational, Resources, Services.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969643 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05252: Displaying dependency details

- page_id: `243969643`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969643/Displaying+dependency+details
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T08:49:33.765+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Model Visualizer > Package Dependency Diagram Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package diagram' space=''], the virtual dependencies (dependency links) are created. All virtual dependencies are stored in the **Virtual dependencies** package, which you can find in the model browser.

You can use the **Show Dependency Details** command to see the dependencies of a particular package. Dependency results are displayed in the dependency details panel (see the following example).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display the dependency details

1. In the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, right-click the dependency with the details you want to display.
2. In the shortcut menu, select Related Elements > Show Dependency Details . The dependency details panel opens.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package diagram" /></ac:link>, the virtual dependencies (dependency links) are created. All virtual dependencies are stored in the <strong>Virtual dependencies</strong> package, which you can find in the model browser.</p><p>You can use the <strong>Show Dependency Details</strong> command to see the dependencies of a particular package. Dependency results are displayed in the dependency details panel (see the following example).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Package_diagram_dep.details.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To display the dependency details</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b51d8020-1fa1-4c88-b08f-668798c33b7a">In the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, right-click the dependency with the details you want to display.</li><li data-uuid="07f7a5ba-e3ee-466d-adb8-e6cda7dbd92d">In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt; <strong>Show Dependency Details</strong>. The dependency details panel opens.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="37ec7a24-f373-4dc1-b290-832e6a90c410"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Dependency" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="93ec554a-b3d5-4066-bf7f-bd6736165f1e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966596 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05253: Displaying element numbering

- page_id: `243966596`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966596/Displaying+element+numbering
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:37.248+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-element-numbering', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To show element numbering

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] in the Diagram toolbar.
2. Select the Show Element Numbers option.

The element numbering appears before its name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To show element numbering</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Diagram toolbar.</li><li>Select the <strong>Show Element Numbers </strong>option.</li></ol><p>The element numbering appears before its name.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Element numbering in the relation map" ac:alt="Element numbering in the relation map"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_element_numbering.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967087 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05254: Displaying elements

- page_id: `243967087`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967087/Displaying+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:19.102+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

210053124

210053139

210053129

The modeling tool includes a number of display commands that allow you to represent elements in different diagrams. However, displaying elements is only possible if they are already created somewhere in the model.The display commandsare interactive, and depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. The table below lists all of the commands which are available, and a short description of each. The table also provides a link to a more in depth description of its functionality, and lists the diagrams in which they can be used.

| Command names | Description | Usage in diagrams |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Display Related Elements | Opens the Display Related Elements dialog in which you can specify the relation type(s), their direction, scope, depth, and other options. All specified criteria define which elements and relations will be displayed on the diagram pane of the selected element. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Paths | Displays paths among shapes that already exist in the model. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Internal Structure | Displays internal structure inside a selected classifier shape that contains its own internal structure. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Parts/Ports | Opens the Display Parts/Ports dialog from where you can specify the exact properties and/or ports you want to display on the diagram pane. Read more > | Class, Composite Structure, Component, Package, Deployment, Object diagrams |
| Hide All Ports | Hides all ports of the selected shape(s) or diagram frame. ]]> | Class, Composite Structure, Component, Package, Deployment, Object diagrams |
| Display Inner Elements | Displays internal elements and paths inside selected shape or diagram frame. There are different procedures when trying to display inner elements in:The Package diagram. ]]>The Activity diagram. ]]>The State Machine diagram. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Provided/Required Interfaces | Displays provided or required interfaces of the selected port shape. ]]> | Class, Composite Structure, Component, Package, Deployment, Object diagrams |
| Display Pins | Opens the Select Pins dialog from where you can select the exact pins you want to display on the selected shape. ]]> | Activity diagram |
| Display Parameter Nodes | Opens the Select Parameters dialog from where you can select exactly which Activity Parameter Nodes you want to display on the frame of the Activity Diagram. ]]> | Activity diagram |
| Display Expansion Nodes | Displays the input or output Expansion Nodes on the Expansion Region border. ]]> | Activity diagram |
| Display Points | Opens the Select Points dialog in which you can select which Connection Point References you want to display on a submachine State shape. ]]> | State Machine diagram |
| Display Lifelines | Opens the Display Lifelines dialog from where you can select which lifelines you want to display. ]]> | Sequence diagramCommunication diagram |
| Display Messages | Opens the Select Messages dialog from where you can select which messages of the selected connector you want to display. ]]> | Communication diagram |

210053122

**Related pages**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968306 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05255: Displaying icon or image

- page_id: `243968306`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968306/Displaying+icon+or+image
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:55.790+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display icon or image on the symbol or instead of the symbol.

The descriptions given in the following table will help you to understand this functionality better.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Stereotype | Every element can be extended by applying a . For more information about applying stereotype properties, see . |
| Icon | Icon of stereotype.Icon is a small image displayed in the top-right corner of shape.To assign an icon to a stereotype in the window, define the Icon property. |
| Text | Stereotype name, displayed on the symbol. |
| Image | Image which can be assigned to element and displayed as icon or instead of element shape. To assign image to element in the element , assign the Image property. |

To change the icon visibility mode on the element shape, do one of the following

- Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog and in the Show Stereotypes property specification cell, select the icon visibility mode.
- Select an element shape and click the Compartments button. From the menu, select Stereotypes and choose the icon visibility mode.

The stereotype visibility modes are described in the following table:

| Mode | Example | Icon and name visibility | Image visibility |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Icon and Text |  | Icon of stereotype displayed.Name of stereotype displayed. | Image of element is displayed in the corner of shape. |
| Icon |  | Icon of stereotype displayed in the corner of symbol.Name of stereotype is not displayed. | Image of element is displayed in the corner of shape. |
| Text Only |  | Icon of stereotype is not displayed.Name of stereotype is displayed. | Image of element is not displayed. |
| Shape Image and Text* |  | Icon of stereotype is displayed instead of shape.Name of stereotype is displayed. | Image of element is displayed instead of shape. |
| Shape Image* |  | Icon of stereotype is displayed instead of shape.Name of stereotype is not displayed. | Image of element is displayed instead of the shape. |
| Do Not Display |  | Icon of stereotype is not displayed.Name of stereotype is not displayed. | Image of element is not displayed. |

* - When the **Shape Image and Text** or **Shape Image** property is selected, all symbol compartmentsshould be suppressed in orderto display the icon of a stereotype instead of the element symbol. If the symbol contains port or pins, these ports or pins are displayed around that symbol.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Shape Image and Text** and **Shape Image** properties are not added to the **Path** element properties list.

If element has assigned both - image and stereotype icon - then image of element will be displayed on the shape.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can display icon or image on the symbol or instead of the symbol.</p><p>The descriptions given in the following table will help you to understand this functionality better.</p><table><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Stereotype</td><td><p>Every element can be extended by applying a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. For more information about applying stereotype properties, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Stereotype properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Icon</td><td><p>Icon of stereotype.</p><p>Icon is a small image displayed in the top-right corner of shape.</p><p>To assign an icon to a stereotype in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Stereotype Specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> window, define the Icon property.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Text</td><td>Stereotype name, displayed on the symbol.</td></tr><tr><td>Image</td><td><p>Image which can be assigned to element and displayed as icon or instead of element shape. To assign image to element in the element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, assign the Image property.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><p>To change the icon visibility mode on the element shape, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and in the <strong>Show Stereotypes</strong> property specification cell, select the icon visibility mode.</li><li>Select an element shape and click the <em>Compartments</em> button. From the menu, select <strong>Stereotypes</strong> and choose the icon visibility mode.</li></ul><p> </p><p>The stereotype visibility modes are described in the following table:</p><table><tbody><tr><th><p>Mode</p></th><th><p>Example</p></th><th><p>Icon and name visibility</p></th><th><p>Image visibility</p></th></tr><tr><td>Icon and Text</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_st_1.png" /></ac:image></td><td><p>Icon of stereotype displayed.</p><p>Name of stereotype displayed.</p></td><td><p>Image of element is displayed in the corner of shape.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Icon</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_st_2.png" /></ac:image></td><td><p>Icon of stereotype displayed in the corner of symbol.</p><p>Name of stereotype is not displayed.</p></td><td><p>Image of element is displayed in the corner of shape.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Text Only</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_st_3.png" /></ac:image></td><td><p>Icon of stereotype is not displayed.</p><p>Name of stereotype is displayed.</p></td><td><p>Image of element is not displayed.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shape Image <br />and Text*</p></td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_st_4.png" /></ac:image></td><td><p>Icon of stereotype is displayed instead of shape.</p><p>Name of stereotype is displayed.</p></td><td><p>Image of element is displayed instead of shape.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Shape Image*</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_st_5.png" /></ac:image></td><td><p>Icon of stereotype is displayed instead of shape.</p><p>Name of stereotype is not displayed.</p></td><td><p>Image of element is displayed instead of the shape.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Do Not Display</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_st_6.png" /></ac:image></td><td><p>Icon of stereotype is not displayed.</p><p>Name of stereotype is not displayed.</p></td><td><p>Image of element is not displayed.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40bec0d2-cf19-4dd1-a2b7-d42c7e34fa9c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>* - When the <strong>Shape Image and Text</strong> or <strong>Shape Image</strong> property is selected, <span>all symbol compartments </span><span>should be suppressed in order </span>to display the icon of a stereotype instead of the element symbol. If the symbol contains port or pins, these ports or pins are displayed around that symbol.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ports_pins_tip.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ea9a5db-aefc-41f6-b9b7-637f4c072eb5"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Shape Image and Text</strong> and <strong>Shape Image</strong> properties are not added to the <strong>Path</strong> element properties list.</p><p>If element has assigned both - image and stereotype icon - then image of element will be displayed on the shape.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e69d02c3-e690-401a-905b-f90fd93cdb1d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="84709ea2-4b5c-4f4b-8c48-c07a718d63dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561313 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 05256: Displaying implied Connectors

- page_id: `249561313`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561313/Displaying+implied+Connectors
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-09T12:12:02.634+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram > Implied Connectors
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display implied relations between Part Properties, ports, diagram frames which are connected through ports/interfaces.

To display implied relations

1. Select the Part Property shape, diagram pane, or diagram frame you want to display implied relations.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display Implied Relations .
  - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Display Implied Relations .
  - Press Ctrl+Alt+V.

The implied relations are displayed between Part Properties, ports, and/or diagram frame. By default, the implied relation is represented as a dashed line with a short summary in label of how many actual connectors are underneath. You can specify a different style of the implied relation by using its the [**Symbol Properties** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Symbol+Properties+dialog). The **Label Display Mode** property is described separately in the [Changing the label display mode](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Changing+the+label+display+mode) page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Three implied relations displayed between**Part Properties typed by *TV* and *TV Tuner* Blocks and diagram frame by using the Display button from the diagram toolbar.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connecting parts through interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hiding direction arrow on port shape' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Autowire Parts' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property path notation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enforce Ports Compatibility mode' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED']

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures on this page is the **Implied Connectors**sample model. Download [ATTACHMENT filename='Implied Connectors.mdzip'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565152 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05257: Displaying information indirectly related to element through Derived Properties

- page_id: `249565152`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565152/Displaying+information+indirectly+related+to+element+through+Derived+Properties
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T19:09:10.008+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

While working with your models, sometimes in the same diagram or table, you need to see information that is not directly related to an element. You can display that information in the following ways:

- In the note connected to an element
- In the table, as a new column

To display the information indirectly related to an element, you need to create derived properties. A property whose values are calculated is called a derived property.

The modeling tool gives you the possibility to create, edit, or delete derived properties

- in the element’s Specification window
- in the table

You can manage derived properties using the **Criterion Editor** dialog.

To create a new derived property

1. Open the Criterion Editor dialog by doing one of the following:
  - In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group and then click the Create button.
  - In the toolbar of an active table, click Show Columns and then select New Derived Property .
2. In the opened dialog, specify the name and expression for the derived property. For more information about Criterion Editor , see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining expressions' space='UPDG'] .
3. Click OK .

If you want to create a derived property with a result other than an element, you need to change the derived property type manually. For more information about how to change the derived property type, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties' space='UPDG'].

To edit a derived property

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and then click the Edit button.
  - In the active table, from the derived property column heading’s shortcut menu, select Edit Expression .
2. In the opened Criterion Editor dialog, make changes and click OK .

To remove a derived property, do one of the following

- In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and then click the Delete button.
- In the active table, from the derived property column heading’s shortcut menu, select Remove Derived Property .

To display derived properties on a note shape

1. Draw a note for the selected element or select an existing one.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select a note and click Edit Compartment. [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_compartment.png']
  - On the note’s shortcut menu, click Edit Compartment > Element Properties . The Compartment Edit dialog opens.
3. In the All list, move properties you want to display on a note shape to the Selected list by doing one of the following:
  - Select one or more properties and click the > or >> button.
  - Double-click to select properties one by one.
4. Click OK . Selected derived properties are displayed in the note.

To display derived properties in a table

1. In the toolbar of an active table, click Show Columns and then click the Select Columns command.
2. In the opened Select Columns dialog, select properties you want to display.
3. Click OK when you are done.

**Related example**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>While working with your models, sometimes in the same diagram or table, you need to see information that is not directly related to an element. You can display that information in the following ways:</p><ul><li>In the note connected to an element</li><li>In the table, as a new column</li></ul><p>To display the information indirectly related to an element, you need to create derived properties. A property whose values are calculated is called a derived property.</p><p>The modeling tool gives you the possibility to create, edit, or delete derived properties </p><ul><li>in the element’s Specification window</li><li>in the table</li></ul><p>You can manage derived properties using the <strong>Criterion Editor</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a new derived property</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Criterion Editor</strong> dialog by doing one of the following:<ul><li>In the selected element’s <strong>Specification</strong> window, click the <strong>Traceability</strong> property group and then click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li><li>In the toolbar of an active table, click <strong>Show Columns</strong> and then select <strong>New Derived Property</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the opened dialog, specify the name and expression for the derived property. For more information about <strong>Criterion Editor</strong>, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDG" ri:content-title="Defining expressions" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4dd451b3-912b-47d5-b6a9-f7b99d087f5c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to create a derived property with a result other than an element, you need to change the derived property type manually. For more information about how to change the derived property type, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDG" ri:content-title="Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To edit a derived property</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the selected element’s <strong>Specification</strong> window, click the <strong>Traceability</strong> property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and then click the <strong>Edit</strong> button.</li><li>In the active table, from the derived property column heading’s shortcut menu, select<strong> Edit Expression</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the opened <strong>Criterion Editor</strong> dialog, make changes and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove a derived property, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the selected element’s <strong>Specification</strong> window, click the <strong>Traceability</strong> property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and then click the <strong>Delete</strong> button.</li><li>In the active table, from the derived property column heading’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Remove Derived Property</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display derived properties on a note shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Draw a note for the selected element or select an existing one.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select a note and click Edit Compartment.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_compartment.png" /></ac:image></li><li>On the note’s shortcut menu, click <strong>Edit Compartment</strong> &gt; <strong>Element Properties</strong>. The <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog opens.</li></ul></li><li>In the All list, move properties you want to display on a note shape to the Selected list by doing one of the following:<ul><li>Select one or more properties and click the<strong> &gt;</strong> or <strong>&gt;&gt;</strong> button.</li><li>Double-click to select properties one by one.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. Selected derived properties are displayed in the note.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To display derived properties in a table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the toolbar of an active table, click <strong>Show Columns</strong> and then click the <strong>Select Columns</strong> command.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Columns</strong> dialog, select properties you want to display.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related example</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="559124a0-b666-4b47-a772-4c5e2b814e2f" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967106 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05258: Displaying internal structure on structured classifiers

- page_id: `243967106`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967106/Displaying+internal+structure+on+structured+classifiers
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:47.381+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

567827949

567827951

567827950

You can represent the internal structure of the structured classifier on the diagram pane in all diagrams. The internal structure of the selected shape can be represented only if the selected element contains its own internal structure. For example, in a Composite Structure diagram on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MED'] shape or in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''] on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'] shape, you can display its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MED'], parts, and connectors.

The following procedure will show you how to display the internal structure of a selected classifier.

To display the internal structure of a selected classifier

1. On the diagram pane, select the classifier shape in which you want to display the internal structure.
2. Do one of the following: 
- Open the selected shape's shortcut menu, click **Display** > **Display Internal Structure**, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure. 
- On the diagram toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], choose the **Display Internal Structure** command, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure. After those steps are completed, the internal structure is displayed inside the selected classifier (see figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

567827948

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Composite structure diagram** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='composite structure diagram.mdzip'] .
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

For example, in a Composite Structure diagram on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part" /></ac:link> shape or in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link> on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> shape, you can display its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, parts, and connectors.</p><p>The following procedure will show you how to display the internal structure of a selected classifier.</p><p>To display the internal structure of a selected classifier</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram pane, select the classifier shape in which you want to display the internal structure.</li><li><p>Do one of the following:<br />- Open the selected shape's shortcut menu, click <strong>Display</strong> &gt; To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite structure diagram.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</li><li>Find in modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561218 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05259: Displaying internal structures on structured classifiers

- page_id: `249561218`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561218/Displaying+internal+structures+on+structured+classifiers
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:13:01.020+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Decomposing Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1096867653

1096867656

1096867654

You can represent the internal structure of the structured classifier on the diagram pane in all diagrams.

The internal structure of the selected shape can be represented only if the selected element contains its own SysML Internal Block Diagram.

For example, in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] on a Part shape or in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] shape, you can display its ports, parts, and connectors.

In the SysML Internal Block Diagram you can display:

- Parts
- Nested parts
- Ports on the frame
- Ports on every part
- Paths for every part and port

Nested parts can be displayed with the dot notation in addition to using structure compartment. The following procedure shows how to display the internal structure of a selected classifier.

To display the internal structure of a selected classifier

1. On the diagram pane, select the classifier shape for which you want to display the internal structure.
2. Do one of the following: 
- Open the selected shape's shortcut menu, click **Display** > **Display Internal Structure**, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure. 
- On the diagram toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], choose the **Display Internal Structure** command, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure. After those steps are completed, the internal structure is displayed inside the selected classifier (see figure below).

1096867651

**Related pages**

- Displaying parts and ports
- [Displaying elements](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Displaying+elements)
- [Nesting parts that are association ends](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Nesting+parts+that+are+association+ends)
- SysML Internal Block Diagram
- Property path notation

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34ea917a-380a-4910-b57e-62f5aaef49aa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1096867653</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8999f54-b7ea-4bcb-8fda-4125a7eed753"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1096867656</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="acc3984d-c173-4911-a423-80fd405300c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1096867654</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can represent the internal structure of the structured classifier on the diagram pane in all diagrams. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="800bbacc-e6a8-4747-81ad-fb93700b8f29"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The internal structure of the selected shape can be represented only if the selected element contains its own SysML Internal Block Diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example, in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link> on a Part shape or in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Block Definition Diagram" /></ac:link> on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> shape, you can display its ports, parts, and connectors.</p><p>In the SysML Internal Block Diagram you can display:</p><ul><li>Parts</li><li>Nested parts</li><li>Ports on the frame</li><li>Ports on every part</li><li>Paths for every part and port</li></ul><p>Nested parts can be displayed with the dot notation in addition to using structure compartment. The following procedure shows how to display the internal structure of a selected classifier.</p><p>To display the internal structure of a selected classifier</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram pane, select the classifier shape for which you want to display the internal structure.</li><li><p>Do one of the following:<br />- Open the selected shape's shortcut menu, click <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Internal Structure</strong>, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure.<br />- On the diagram toolbar click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span>, choose the <strong>Display Internal Structure</strong> command, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure.</p><p>After those steps are completed, the internal structure is displayed inside the selected classifier (see figure below).</p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b952fad4-d5dd-4347-afd2-279ffed8d96e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1096867651</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Displaying+parts+and+ports">Displaying parts and ports</a></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Displaying+elements">Displaying elements</a> </p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Nesting+parts+that+are+association+ends">Nesting parts that are association ends</a></p></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/SysML+Internal+Block+Diagram">SysML Internal Block Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Property+path+notation">Property path notation</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565293 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05260: Displaying Measurement Values

- page_id: `249565293`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565293/Displaying+Measurement+Values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:37.006+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Measurements > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['edit-measurement-values', 'display-measurement-values', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

While working with diagrams, you can display and for the element in the:

- .
- .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

in_compartmentTo display measurement values in the compartment area of the element shape

1. Right-click the element, for which you want to display measurements.
2. Click Edit Compartments and select Actual Measurements tab.
3. Select the measurements you want to display.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

In the Symbol Properties dialog, you can select to display the name of the measured element.

in_note_or_commentTo display measurement values in the note or comment

1. Create a note or comment for the element which measurements you want to display.
2. Right-click the note or comment and select Edit Compartments .
3. Choose Measurements tab and select the measurements you want to display.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

- Only measurements with predefined values can be displayed.
- You can also display indirectly related measurement values.

editTo edit measurement values

1. Select an element with the displayed measurement values you want to edit.
2. Double-click a measurement value you want to edit.
3. Type a new value.
