<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967309 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05261: Displaying or hiding elements in compartments on shapes

- page_id: `243967309`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967309/Displaying+or+hiding+elements+in+compartments+on+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:28.081+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Compartments
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the **Compartment Edit**dialog to control the list of elements and properties visible in compartments on shapes. You can display or hide stereotypes, constraints, tagged values, attributes, operations, and others. If there are hidden properties on the boxable compartment, the **...** is displayed.

You can also display or hide the element specification properties, such as *Name*, *Is Active*, *Owner*, *To Do* in the **Compartment Edit**dialog under the **Element Properties** tab.

To open the **Compartment Edit** dialog from the diagram pane

- Select a shape and click the Compartments smart manipulator. On the menu, click the arrow to expand and then select Edit Compartments .
- Right-click a single or any number of shapes or classifier compartments (e.g. Properties, Operations, Receptions, Ports, and etc.), and from the shortcut menu, select **Edit Compartments**. Compartment Edit dialog of multiple shapesWhen you edit compartments of more than one shape at once, the**Compartment Edit** dialog works with the following rules:- If you select multiple shapes of the same element, the **Compartment Edit** dialog is the same as for a single shape, but changes are applied for all selected shapes after clicking **OK**. 
 - If you select multiple shapes of different elements, the **Compartment Edit** dialog shows a union of compartments and their items. 
 - If you select multiple shapes, but at least one of them hides the common compartment item while another shows it, the **Compartment Edit** dialog displays that item in italics in the **Element Properties** tab > Selected area. If you move the conflicting item to the Hidden area, it becomes non-italicized, and changes are applied for both shapes after clicking **OK**.
- On the Note shape, click the Edit Element Properties smart manipulator.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use the <strong>Compartment Edit </strong>dialog to control the list of elements and properties visible in compartments on shapes. You can display or hide stereotypes, constraints, tagged values, attributes, operations, and others. If there are hidden properties on the boxable compartment, the <strong>...</strong> is displayed.</p><p>You can also display or hide the element specification properties, such as <em>Name</em>, <em>Is Active</em>, <em>Owner</em>, <em>To Do</em> in the <strong>Compartment Edit </strong>dialog under the <strong>Element Properties</strong> tab.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog from the diagram pane</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a shape and click the <em>Compartments</em> smart manipulator. On the menu, click the arrow to expand and then select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click a single or any number of shapes or classifier compartments (e.g. Properties, Operations, Receptions, Ports, and etc.), and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8be6e385-af39-4175-a17e-56adb613602a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Compartment Edit dialog of multiple shapes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you edit compartments of more than one shape at once, the<strong> Compartment Edit</strong> dialog works with the following rules:</p><p> - If you select multiple shapes of the same element, the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog is the same as for a single shape, but changes are applied for all selected shapes after clicking <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> - If you select multiple shapes of different elements, the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog shows a union of compartments and their items.<br /> - If you select multiple shapes, but at least one of them hides the common compartment item while another shows it, the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog displays that item in italics in the <strong>Element Properties</strong> tab &gt; Selected area. If you move the conflicting item to the Hidden area, it becomes non-italicized, and changes are applied for both shapes after clicking <strong>OK</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>On the Note shape, click the <em>Edit Element Properties</em> smart manipulator.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561628 space=MT version=9 -->
## PAGE 05262: Displaying parameters and properties

- page_id: `249561628`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561628/Displaying+parameters+and+properties
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2025-09-08T17:54:33.137+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling parametric constraints
- labels: ['display-parts', 'display-ports', 'hide-ports', 'enable-part-compartments', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] with ports (including deep nested ports), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Property' space='MED'], and other propertiesthat have already been created in the model.This functionality is driven by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED'], which allow you to display properties on which stereotypes have been applied. If you have applied your own stereotypes for the elements (e.g., electrical, mechanical, optical), you can use those stereotypeswhen selecting the properties you want to display on the diagram pane.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

For more information about how to creating and applying custom stereotypes, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML Profiling and DSL Guide' space='UPDG'].

This page contains the following information:

43

##### Ways to display parameters and properties

You can display ports and properties in the following ways:

44

###### Using the Display Parameters/Properties dialog

You can use the **Display Parameters/Properties** dialog to control how much of a structure should be displayed. The **Display Parameters/Properties** dialog****opens automatically when creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space=''] for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] element that owns parameters and properties. It can also be accessed and opened in thestructure diagrams that have already been created.The******Display Parameters/Properties**dialog allows you to:

- Preview all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED'],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] , and other properties in one place.
- Manually or automatically select required parameters and properties you want to display.
- Manually or automatically choose deeply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Nesting parts that are association ends' space=''] ports or/and properties you want to display.
- Define layout options for properties .
- Specify layout options for parameters.

The **Display Parts/Ports**dialog consists of the following:

- The *Dialog toolbar* allows you to show or hide additional information about listed elements.
- The *Element tree* displays all owned internal structure of the context[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']Block or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'].
- The *Panel* is a context-sensitive area thatdepends on the selected property from the Element tree. It allows you to batch select parameters/properties in the Element tree. You can select all, clear all, or choose properties by type, stereotype, or aspect.
- The*Options* area allows you to choose the layout of the selected properties and parameterswhen they appear in the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

The following procedure outlines working with the **Display Parameters/Properties** dialog when you want to display required parameters and properties on the diagram pane.

To display parameters and properties using the **Display Parameters/Properties**dialog

1. Open the **Display Parameters/Properties** dialog:
  - When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space=''] , the dialog opens automatically.
  - When editing an already created [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space=''] , the dialog can be opened in the following ways: - Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and, o n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose **Display Parameters/Properties**. - Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select **Display** > **Display Parameters/Properties**.
2. (Optional) Enable buttons from the **Display Parameters/Properties** dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full path of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full types of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited properties.
3. Choose the properties you want to display by using one or more of the following methods: WarningIf some check boxesare inactive in the element list,those elements are already displayed in the diagram.
  - Use the element tree located on the left side of the dialog:- Manually select individual check boxes one by one. 
- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element. Example 1All [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED']*C*, *gt*, *h* of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] typed by *Grinding* [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] are selected recursively by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box from the element treein the **Display Parameters/Properties** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Use the context-sensitive panel located on the right side of the dialog:- **Select Properties** group - displays all property types (including your own [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED']) that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Select Parameters** group - displays all parameter types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.- **Select All** - selects all check boxes that are expanded from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Clear All** - clears all check boxes, including those that are collapsed (deep nested), from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. Example 2All [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED'] C, gt, and h of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] typed by *Grinding* [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] are selected in the element tree by using the **Select parameters** group in the context-sensitive panelof the **Display Parameters/Properties** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the layout options in the Options area:
  - Properties layout: make sure the Layout Parts check box is selected; otherwise, the property shapes will not be arranged in the automatic layout.
  - Usage of layout template: make sure the Use Layout Template check box is selected; otherwise, the layout template will not be applied. To apply the layout template, check the User Layout Template box.If you leave this check box selected, the selection in the dialog works in this way: when selecting the Constraint Property, which is included in the layout template, all it's from the layout template are selected automatically.
  - Port/parameters layout on Part/Constraint Property shape: - Top/Bottom - displays ports/parameters on the Part/Constraint Property shape from top to bottom. [ATTACHMENT filename='parameter_layout_on_part_shape_top_bottom.png'] - Left/Right - displays ports/parameters on the Part/Constraint Property shape from left to right. [ATTACHMENT filename='parameter_layout_on_part_shape_left_right.png']
5. Click **OK**.

After applying the steps outlined above, all parameters and properties that were selected in the **Display Parameters/Properties** dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.

###### Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space=''] that have already been created, you can display[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED']Constraint Parameters, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'], or other properties and hide all Constraint Parameters by using the commands from:

- The Display button on the diagram toolbar.
- The **Display** command group under the shortcut menu.

You can also use the **Display**button and **Display** command group from the shortcut menu as their functions are identical. All those commands are interactive and depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Constraint Property shape is selected on the diagram pane, the menu contains only the commands that can be applied to the selected Constraint Property shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, the menu shows commands that can be applied to the whole diagram.

These commands under the **Display** button and shortcut menu are comprised ofelements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:

- Metatypes.
- Stereotypes.
- Types stereotypes.

The following procedures show:

- How to display Constraint Parameters, Constraint Properties using the **Display** button.
- 
- 

**button**To display Constraint Parameters, Constraint Properties using the **Display** button

1. Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.
2. O n the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='display_related_elements_button.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select the required properties or parameters: - All Parameters that are available to display are listed under the **Display Parameters** command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the **Display Parameters** command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: Constraint Parameters are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level Constraint Properties. - All properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. After selecting the required command from the **Display** button, the appropriate properties and parameters are displayed on the diagram pane.

**shortcut**To display parameters, properties using the shortcut menu

1. Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.
2. From the shortcut menu, select the Display command group. [ATTACHMENT filename='Display_command_parameters.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select the required parameters: 
 - All parameters that are available to display are listed under the **Display Parameters**command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the **Display Parameters**command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts. - All properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. 
After selecting the required command from the **Display** command menu, the appropriate properties and parameters are displayed on the diagram pane.

**hide**To hide [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED']Constraint Parameters

- Select either a diagram pane or any number of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] shapes. O n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose**Hide All Parameters**.
- Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of Constraint Property shapes. Select **Display** >**Hide All Parameters**. 
After selecting the command, the parameters of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane. However, they will not be removed from the model. WarningIf the **Hide All Parameters**command does not appear, the selection or diagramdoes not contain any parameters that can be hidden. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the smart manipulator toolbar

When the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] are already displayed on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space=''],you can display their [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED'] using the **Display All Parameters**button from the smart manipulator toolbar. Follow the steps below to learn how to display Constraint Parameters of a selected Constraint Property shape.

To display Constraint Parameters on Constraint Property shape

1. Select theConstraint Property shape or a diagram frame.
2. From the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
All first level Constraint Parameters are displayed on the selected Constraint Property shape. WarningIf the **Display All Parameters**button is not available, the selected element or diagramdoes not own any Constraint Parameters. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

After completing the steps outlined above, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] with all its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED'], and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] with ports (including deep nested ports), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Property' space='MED'] can be displayed on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space=''].

##### Displaying properties of a Constraint Parameter

If you display properties of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED'], all properties of its type will be displayed inside the Constraint Parameter shape and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] compartments will be enabled for that parameter.

To enable Part Property compartments on the Constraint Parameter shape

1. Select a Constraint Parameter shape whose type has properties.
2. Do one of the following:
  - O n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - From the shortcut menu, point to the Display command group.
3. Select the required properties to display.

The properties are displayed inside the Constraint Parameter shape. Part Property compartments are enabled for the Constraint Parameter.

**Related pages**

- SysML Parametric Diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Constraint Parameters automatically' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property path notation' space='']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Coffee Machine** sample model that comes with Cameo Simulation Toolkit. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='CoffeeMachine.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\simulation\CoffeeMachine.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span class="confluence-link" style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can display<span class="confluence-link"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> with <span class="confluence-link" style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with ports (including deep nested ports), <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Value Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Value Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and other properties </span>that have already been created <span class="confluence-link">in <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the model</span>.</span><span> This <span>functionality is driven by <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which allow you <span>to display properties on which stereotypes have been applied.</span> </span> <span> <span> <span> If you have applied your own stereotypes for the elements (e.g., electrical, mechanical, optical), you can use those stereotypes</span> </span>when selecting the properties you want to display on the diagram pane.</span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cef6af2-7409-4277-b81c-7bc4aa793c79"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about how to creating and applying custom stereotypes, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDG" ri:content-title="UML Profiling and DSL Guide" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span class="confluence-link">This page contains the following information:</span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b8f1b62-83be-44e1-96bb-662639b6113c"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3><span class="confluence-link">Ways to display parameters and properties</span></h3><p><span class="confluence-link">You can display <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ports and properties</span> in <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the following ways</span>:</span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc59d602-87c8-4325-8821-7aa06088f9f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h4>Using the Display Parameters/Properties dialog</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can use the <strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> dialog to control how much of a structure should be displayed. The <span><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> dialog<strong> </strong> </span>opens automatically when creating a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link> for a <span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /></ac:link> </span> element that owns parameters and properties.</span> <span>It can also be accessed and opened in the </span><span class="confluence-link"><span>structure diagram</span>s that have already been created</span>.<span> The<strong> </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> </span>dialog allows you to:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="99ae6591-71db-4ada-9ad8-9a081324118a">Preview all <span class="confluence-link" style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">,<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></span>, and other properties in one place.</li><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="9fc614e1-81c0-403f-a8cf-e49d046c3d6a">Manually or automatically select required parameters and properties you want to display.</li><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="50ee5976-8d22-46a9-ad68-d90c668b7d5b">Manually or automatically choose deeply <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Nesting parts that are association ends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[nested]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> ports or/and properties you want to display.</li><li data-uuid="dce6c816-0f31-47d1-8c45-16465144a91c">Define layout options for <span class="confluence-link">properties</span>.</li><li data-uuid="2fecae05-d95d-49c8-a750-dc11e0b733c8">Specify layout options for parameters.</li></ul><p>The <span> <strong>Display Parts/Ports </strong>dialog consists of the following:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="00d2afec-f97b-437f-89b7-6820df839220"><span> The <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span> <em>Dialog toolbar</em> allows you to show or hide additional information about listed elements.<br /></span> </span> </span></li><li data-uuid="37e82c68-83f1-4af9-beb2-2d2af6857500"><span> The <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span> <em>Element tree</em> displays all owned internal structure of the context<span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Block</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> or <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /></ac:link></span>.<br /></span> </span> </span></li><li data-uuid="0a23f267-4541-40e3-a4d1-45975202c02a"><span> The <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span> <em>Panel</em> is a context-sensitive area that<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> depends on the selected property from the Element tree. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">It allows you to batch select <span class="confluence-link">parameters</span>/<span class="confluence-link">properties</span> in the Element tree. </span></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can select all, clear all, or choose properties by  type, stereotype, or aspect.</span></li><li data-uuid="d4d092ca-7d84-46af-8077-017c383d0547"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>The<em> Options</em> area allows you to choose the layout of the selected properties and <span class="confluence-link">parameters </span>when they appear in the diagram.</span></span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><br /><span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  </span><br /></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Display Parameters/Properties dialog of the Coffee Grinder Block with the four highlighted main sections of the dialog: Toolbar, Element tree, Panel, and Options area."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_parameters_properties_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></strong></p><p>The following procedure outlines working with the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> </span> dialog when you want to display required parameters and properties on the diagram pane.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display parameters and properties using the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> </span>dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="75f926fb-16a0-45e0-b8ae-9575eafec49f"><p>Open the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> </span> dialog:</p><ul><li>When creating a new <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link></span>, the dialog opens automatically.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li><li>When editing an already created<span class="confluence-link"> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link></span>, the dialog can be opened in the following ways:<br />- Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and, o<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span> and choose <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> </span>.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br />- Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Parameters/Properties </strong>.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="3e69fb5e-c32c-4589-b89d-5480d6a459b3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>(Optional) Enable buttons from the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> </span>dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the full path of the elements"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the full path of elements.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /></span></li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the qualified name of type in the typed elements text"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_qualified_name_of_type_in_the typed_elements_text.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the full types of elements.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_inherited_button.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the inherited elements.<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Private Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_private_inherited.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the inherited properties.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="ec0cfd98-f74e-4478-b4b8-6ba659e73816"><p>Choose the properties you want to display by using one or more of the following methods:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ebed955-61f3-499b-9133-06607c2df4c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If some <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">check boxes</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> are inactive in the element list, </span>those elements are already <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">displayed</span> in the diagram.<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Use the element tree located on the left side of the dialog:</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">- Manually select individual check boxes one by one.  <br />- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element. </span></span><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0493b945-788e-4921-bc9c-25ecc6a7fa93"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>All <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em> C</em>, <em>gt</em>, <em>h</em> of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /></ac:link> typed by <em>Grinding</em> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /></ac:link> are selected recursively by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box from the element tree<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> in the <strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> dialog.</span><sub><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /></span></sub></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Display Parameters/Properties dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursively_selected_nested_elements_par.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Use the context-sensitive panel located on the right side of the dialog:</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">- <strong>Select Properties</strong> group - displays all property types <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(including your own <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</span> that are owned by the selected element from the element tree  located on the left side of the dialog.<br />- <strong>Select Parameters</strong> group - displays all parameter types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.<br /></span>- <strong>Select All</strong> - selects all check boxes that are expanded from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.<br />- <strong>Clear All</strong> - clears all check boxes, including those that are collapsed (deep nested), from the element tree located on <span>the left side of the dialog.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="728daf61-336a-4786-ba52-fe698621106c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> C, gt, and h of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /></ac:link> typed by <em>Grinding</em> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /></ac:link> are selected <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">in <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the element tree</span> by using the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Select parameters</strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">group</span></span> in the context-sensitive panel </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">of the <strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> dialog.</span></span></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Using the context-sensitive panel of the Display Parameters/Properties dialog when trying to select required parameters."><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_parameters_using_context_sensitive_panel.png" /></ac:image></p><sub><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></sub></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="7ddb9dbe-6284-44fb-8c25-719814361d6c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>Specify the layout options in the <strong>Options</strong> area:<br /><ul><li>Properties layout: make sure the <strong>Layout Parts</strong> check box is selected; otherwise, the property shapes will not be arranged in the automatic layout.</li><li>Usage of layout template: make sure the <strong>Use Layout Template</strong> check box is selected; otherwise, the layout template will not be applied. To apply the layout template, check the <strong>User Layout Template </strong>box.If you leave this check box selected, the selection in the dialog works in this way: when selecting the Constraint Property, which is included in the layout template, all it's from the layout template are selected automatically.</li><li>Port/parameters layout on Part/Constraint Property shape:<br />- <strong>Top/Bottom</strong> - displays ports/parameters on the Part/Constraint Property shape from top to bottom.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter_layout_on_part_shape_top_bottom.png" /></ac:image><br />- <strong>Left/Right </strong> - displays ports/parameters on the Part/Constraint Property shape from left to right.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter_layout_on_part_shape_left_right.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="64815560-b571-4d4c-832e-0d76f2c725b3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After applying the steps outlined above, all  parameters and properties that were selected in the <strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /></span></p><h4>Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu</h4><p class="confluence-link">In the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link></span> that have already been created, you can display<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Parameters</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or other properties</span> and hide all Constraint Parameters by using the commands from:</p><ul><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="67c95cd3-8a7f-4fe0-b2e9-07c09838ac4f">The <strong>Display </strong>button on the diagram toolbar.</li><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="a32ff8d7-f809-4e1d-a17d-fccb8041ae60">The <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display</strong> command group under the shortcut menu.</span></li></ul><p>You can also use <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>the <strong>Display </strong>button</span></span> and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display</strong> command group from the shortcut menu as</span> their functions are identical. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All those commands are interactive and depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Constraint Property shape is selected on the diagram pane, the menu contains only the commands that can be applied to the selected Constraint Property shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, the menu shows commands that can be applied to the whole diagram.</span></p><p>These <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link">commands under the <strong>Display</strong> button and shortcut menu are comprised of </span></span>elements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3327ce8f-5baa-4232-9df7-a58a6bd1f814">Metatypes.</li><li data-uuid="7d45ef5d-91d3-4006-9802-61ca7fa6334d">Stereotypes.</li><li data-uuid="61f3a08c-2c92-49c6-b2be-71382ffff7a6">Types stereotypes.</li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link">The following procedures show:</span></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="bf5a9370-3aa2-4e26-b54d-0ed2964aac04"><ac:link ac:anchor="button"><ac:link-body>How to display Constraint Parameters, Constraint Properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button.</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="1e99afc8-da9a-4727-b7f7-54c49b6c6c27"><ac:link ac:anchor="shortcut"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display Constraint Parameters, Constraint Properties using the shortcut menu.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="679fedba-3dc5-4147-a7b5-8a5560ab0e80"><ac:link ac:anchor="hide"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to hide Constraint Parameters.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="151e0ef2-cf5f-42c8-9b7b-b70191dd1dcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">button</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Constraint Parameters, Constraint Properties</span> using the <strong>Display</strong> button</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f724273f-5b6a-47b5-97bb-ba25c87c2f75">Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li data-uuid="5d3333c0-89e0-4d54-88f3-7c8dd55bcc76">O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="After selecting the Thermal Calculations Parametric diagram pane and clicking the Display button, opened menu allows to display properties and/or parameters."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_button.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="7144a766-6404-4034-91c3-5cded3d65d11"><p>From the opened command menu, select the required properties or parameters:</p><p><br />        - All Parameters that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Parameters</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ef07d2a-8928-47ac-9bee-363460e3bac9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Parameters</strong> command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: Constraint Parameters are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level Constraint Properties.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.</p><p><span>After selecting the required command from the</span> <strong>Display</strong> button<span>, the appropriate properties and parameters are displayed on the diagram pane.</span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f27456dd-5abb-464b-b57a-d14bee0cbd81"><ac:parameter ac:name="">shortcut</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display parameters, properties using the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="031b6ff4-9872-4b32-aa94-6eebd20f3c7d">Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li data-uuid="f2919dff-e78e-47cb-958e-14cc474c03a5">From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Display</strong> command group.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="The Display command group menu allows to display Constraint Parameters of the selected Constraint Property shape." ac:alt="The Display command group menu allows to display Constraint Parameters of the selected Constraint Property shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_command_parameters.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="e40cdde3-7740-499f-9c7a-58c551af07a7"><p>From the opened command menu, select the required parameters:<br />        - All parameters that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Parameters </strong>command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="327c3ff9-f8cc-4d9e-a0b5-0c763e6c790e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Parameters </strong>command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.<br />After selecting the required command from the <strong>Display</strong> command menu, the appropriate properties and parameters are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4794c2e6-db49-41b6-ac94-03bcb79efedc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">hide</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To hide <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:link-body>Constraint Parameters<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><br /></span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="f091d166-7a00-44e5-8279-6a57ba96f6e6">Select either a diagram pane or any number of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /></ac:link> shapes. O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>  and choose<strong> Hide All Parameters</strong>.</span></li><li data-uuid="94fbbb7a-3760-4020-ae2c-a004f11b902c"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of Constraint Property shapes. Select <strong>Display</strong> &gt;<strong> Hide All Parameters</strong>.<br />After selecting the command, the parameters of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane. However, they will not be removed from the model.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff629db0-f28c-4b9f-aa61-48e542d03bba"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Hide All Parameters </strong>command does not appear, the selection or <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">diagram </span>does not contain any parameters that can be hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:title="Hiding all Constraint Parameters of the Constraint Property typed by Boiler Water Mass Constraint Block." ac:alt="Hiding all Constraint Parameters of the Constraint Property typed by Boiler Water Mass Constraint Block."><ri:attachment ri:filename="hide_all_parameters.png" /></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><h4>Using the smart manipulator toolbar<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></h4><p><span class="confluence-link" style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are already displayed on the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link></span><span class="confluence-link">, </span>you can display their <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> using the <strong>Display All Parameters </strong>button from the smart manipulator toolbar. Follow the steps below to learn how to display Constraint Parameters of a selected Constraint Property shape.</span></p><p>To display <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Parameters</span></span> on <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Property shape</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6e102c8a-cd57-4873-a00b-81e50693f7c4">Select<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> the </span><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Property shape</span> or a diagram frame.</span> </span></li><li data-uuid="51ddeadb-eabe-49c6-bd37-e966c19e0576"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> From the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_parameters_button.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><span>All first level <span class="confluence-link">Constraint Parameters</span> are displayed on the selected Constraint Property shape.</span> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb6590f-3710-4154-9154-2c9ce8edfa7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> If the <strong>Display All Parameters </strong>button is not available, the selected element or <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">diagram </span>does not own any <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Parameters</span></span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_parameters_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image></span><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After completing the steps outlined above, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Property]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with all its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and/or <span class="confluence-link" style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with ports (including deep nested ports), <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Value Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Value Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span> can be displayed on the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Parametric diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></p><h3>Displaying properties of a Constraint Parameter</h3><p>If you display properties of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /></ac:link>, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the Constraint Parameter shape and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> compartments will be enabled for that parameter.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enable Part Property compartments on the Constraint Parameter shape</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7ba4ca3c-1290-4be7-a4f1-c5c0011d4061">Select a Constraint Parameter shape whose type has properties.</li><li data-uuid="d6e83f38-cccc-4c4d-ae5f-216b293fe42b">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, point to the <strong>Display</strong> command group.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="3d1ff0b9-2430-4920-9b7a-dd5c65475059">Select the required properties to display.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The properties are displayed inside the Constraint Parameter shape. Part Property compartments are enabled for the Constraint Parameter.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="077de8b3-d271-492e-b05a-eb80ed093955"><a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype" rel="nofollow">SysML Parametric Diagram</a></li><li data-uuid="87ec1e9e-cf34-4e34-8885-5bfb1dfcf7c2"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Constraint Parameters automatically" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="59bccbab-d388-496d-92bb-7e1616243fcf"><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="5a6ffb53-43c6-494f-94e1-adb9c05389a4"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Property path notation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Coffee Machine</strong> sample model that comes with Cameo Simulation Toolkit. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="872fc3f0-c1a3-4945-b780-1db9dd7682db">Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="CoffeeMachine.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></em></span></li><li data-uuid="45ca9323-18ad-4808-9ac7-a477fc9fb116">Find in modeling tool <em><span>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\simulation\CoffeeMachine.mdzip.</span></em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967088 space=MT version=7 -->
## PAGE 05263: Displaying parts and ports

- page_id: `243967088`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967088/Displaying+parts+and+ports
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-09-10T12:31:21.972+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: ['display-parts', 'display-ports', 'hide-ports', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display ports, properties, and deep nested properties that have already been created in all [ATTACHMENT filename='uml_diagrams.png'].This functionality is driven by stereotypes, which allow you to display properties on which [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED'] have been applied.If you have applied your own [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED'] for the elements (e.g., electrical, mechanical, optical), you can use those stereotypeswhen selecting the properties you want to display on the diagram pane.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

For more information about how to create custom stereotypes and apply them, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML Profiling and DSL Guide' space='UPDG'].

##### Ways to display parts and ports

###### Using the Display Parts/Ports dialog

You can use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog to control how much of a structure should be displayed. The **Display Parts/Ports**dialog****opens automatically when creating a new[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'] element that owns properties. It can also be accessed and opened in thestructure diagrams that have already been created.The**Display Parts/Ports** dialog allows you to:

- Preview all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MED'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MED'] , and other properties in one place.
- Manually or automatically select required Parts, Ports, and other properties you want to display.
- Manually or automatically choose deeply nested Parts, Ports, and other properties you want to display.
- Define layout options for Parts.
- Specify layout options for Ports.

The **Display Parts/Ports**dialog consists of the following areas:

- *Dialog toolbar* allows you to show or hide additional information about listed elements.
- *Element tree* displays all owned internal structure of context Class.
- *Panel* is a context-sensitive area thatdepends on the selected property from the Element tree. It allows you to batch select Parts/Ports in the Element tree. You can select all, clear all, or choose only properties by type, stereotype, or aspect.
- *Options* area allows you to choose the layout of the selected parts and Ports when they appear in the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

The following procedure will guide you in working with the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog when you want to display required properties on the diagram pane.

To display Ports and Properties using the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog

1. Open the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog:
  - When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] , the dialog opens automatically.
  - When editing an already created [ATTACHMENT filename='uml_diagrams.png']structure diagram , the dialog can be opened in the following ways: - Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and, o n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose **Display Parts/Ports**. - Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select **Display** > **Display Parts/Ports**.
2. (Optional) Enable buttons from the Display Parts/Ports dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full path of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full types of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited properties.
3. Choose the properties you want to display by using one or more of the following methods: WarningIf some check boxesare inactive in the element list,those elements are already displayed in the diagram.
  - Use the element tree located on the left side of the dialog:- Manually select individual check boxes one by one. 
- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element. Example 1All properties (*socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn*) of the *Ee* Property are selected recursively by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box from the element treein the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Use the context-sensitive panel located on the right side of the dialog:- **Select Properties** group - displays all property types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Select Ports** group - displays all Port types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by a selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.- **Select All** - selects all check boxes that are expanded from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Clear All** - clears all check boxes, even those that are collapsed (deep nested), from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. Example 2All ports (*socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn*) of the *Ee* Property are selected in the element tree by using the **Select Ports** group in the context-sensitive panelof the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the layout options in the Options area:
  - Parts layout: select the Layout Parts check box if you want the Part shapes to be arranged in the automatic layout.
  - Usage of layout template: select the Use Layout Template check box if you want the layout template to be applied. When this check box is selected, the Part, which is included in the layout template, and all its Ports from the layout template are selected automatically.
  - Showing connectors: select the Show Connectors check box if you want the connectors to be displayed.
  - Ports layout on Parts shape: - Top/Bottom - displays Ports on the Part shape from top to bottom. - Left/Right - displays Ports on the Part shape from left to right.
5. Click **OK**.

After those steps, all parts, ports, and other properties that were selected in the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.

###### Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu

In the structure diagrams that have already been created, you can display [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MED'], Properties, and hide all Ports by using the commands from:

- The Display button on the diagram toolbar.
- The **Display** command group under the shortcut menu.

You can also use the**Display** button and **Display** command group from the shortcut menu as their functions are identical. All those commands are interactive and depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Part shape is selected on the diagram pane, both menu contains only the commands that can be applied to the selected Part shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, both the menu shows commands that can be applied to the whole diagram.

These commands under the **Display** button and shortcut menu are comprised ofelements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:

- Metatypes.
- Stereotypes.
- Types stereotypes.

The following procedures will show you how to:

- How to display Ports, Properties using the **Display** button.
- 
- 

**button**To display Ports, Properties using the **Display** button

1. Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.
2. O n the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='display_related_elements_button_MD.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display: 
 - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the **Display Ports** command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the **Display Ports** command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts. - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. After selecting the required command from the **Display** button, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.

**shortcut**To display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu

1. Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.
2. From the shortcut menu, select the Display command group. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_command_group_MD.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display: 
 - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the **Display Ports** command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the **Display Ports** command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts. - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. 
After selecting the required command from the Display command menu, the appropriate properties, Ports are displayed on the diagram pane.

**hide**To hide Ports

- Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and, o n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose**Hide All Ports**.
- Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select **Display** >**Hide All Ports**. 
After selecting the command, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MED']of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane but not removed from the model. WarningIf the **Hide All Ports** command doesn't appear, the selection or diagramdoesn't contain any [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MED']that can be hidden. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the smart manipulator toolbar

When the properties are already displayed on the[ATTACHMENT filename='uml_diagrams.png']structure diagrams, you can display their direct ports using the **Display All Ports** button from the smart manipulator toolbar. It does not allow you to display deeply nested ports, only first-level ports. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog. Follow the steps below to learn how to display ports of a selected property shape.

To display Ports on shapes

1. Select the required element shape or a diagram frame.
2. From the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
All first-level Ports of the selected shape will be displayed on the diagram pane. WarningIf the **Display All Ports** button is not available, the selected element or diagramdoesn't own any Port. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the drag-and-drop operationin the Composite Structure diagram

The most efficient way to create a new property and display it with all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MED'] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] is by dragging the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'] or Property elements. This method does not allow you to display deeply nested properties, only first-level properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog. The following procedures will show you:

- 
- How to display a Property shape and all its Ports by dragging the Property element.

drag_classYou can create a new Property by dragging the Class element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']Model Browserto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] pane. The newly created Property will be typed by the dragged Class and displayed on the diagram pane. If this Class has Ports defined, you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3).

****

To create and display a Property by dragging a Class element

- Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.
- Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag). Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the **Create Property Symbol**command. 
The new Property is created in the model and displayed on the diagram pane. The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.

To create and display a Property and display all its Ports by dragging a Class element

- Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).
- Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the **Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command. The new Property is created in the model and displayed with all its Ports on the diagram pane. The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.

#### INFO: Tip

Tip

After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following: 
 - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the **Select in Containment Tree** command. 
 - Select the shape and press Alt+B.

#### TIP: Example 3

Example 3

After selecting the **Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command anddragging (long drag) the Class element to *Internal Structure of Workplace* diagram pane, itcreates a new Property typed by a*Duplex Power Outlet*Class and displays all Ports on it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

drag_propertyYou can display a Property shape by dragging a Property element from the Model Browser to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] pane. If this property has [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MED'] defined, you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).

The following procedures describe how to display Property shape and all its Ports by dragging the Property element.

To display a Property by dragging a Property element

- Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.
- Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag) . Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the**Create Property Symbol**command. The Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane.

To display a Property with all of its Ports by dragging a Property element

- Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).
- Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the**Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command. The Property shape is displayed with all its Ports on that shape.

After selecting the**Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports**command, and dragging (long drag) the Property element to*Internal Structure of Workplace*diagram, it displays Property shape and all Ports on it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After completing the steps outlined above, the Property shape and/or all its Ports are displayed on the Composite Structure diagram.

##### Displaying properties of a Port

If you display properties of a Port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the Port shape and part compartments will be enabled for that Port.

To enable Part compartments on the Port shape

1. Select a Port shape whose type has properties.
2. Do one of the following:
  - O n the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] .
  - From the shortcut menu, point to the Display command group.
3. Select the required properties to display.

The properties are displayed inside the Port shape. Part compartments are enabled for the Port.

The example below shows the *p1* Port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the Port shape are the *Pressure Status* Flow Property and the *suspensionSensor* Part Property, which are the properties of the selected Port type *iSuspension* Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the *p1* Port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Sample model**

The models used in the figures of this page are:

- The Composite structure diagram sample model. To open this sample go to <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.
