style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li><li>When editing an already created <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML v1 diagram descriptions" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">structure diagram</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>, the dialog can be opened in the following ways:<br />- Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes. O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span> and choose <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong>.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br />- Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong>.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="1f03937e-4268-4499-9354-54b2efcb9790"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>(Optional) Enable buttons from the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the full path of the elements"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the full path of elements.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /></span></li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the qualified name of type in the typed elements text"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_qualified_name_of_type_in_the typed_elements_text.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the full types of elements.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_inherited_button.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the inherited elements.<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Private Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_private_inherited.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the inherited properties.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="029c098c-d73a-4b73-aaed-2bf830f68f44"><p>Choose the properties you want to display, using one or more of the following methods:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ebed955-61f3-499b-9133-06607c2df4c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If certain <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">check boxes</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> are inactive in the element list, </span>those elements are already <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">displayed</span> in the diagram.<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Use the element tree located on the left side of the dialog:</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">- Manually select individual check boxes one by one.  <br />- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /></span></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58147caf-ac97-47ac-bfdd-939da1408e1f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All properties (in this example all ports<em> AirFlowPort, ElectricityPort, AutomotiveBusesPort</em>) of the <em>Air Distribution</em> Part Property are selected recursively <span>by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box</span> from the element tree</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Recursively selected nested properties in the Display Parts/Ports dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursively_selected_properties.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Use the context-sensitive panel located on the right side of the dialog:</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">- <strong>Select Properties</strong> group - displays all property types <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(including your own <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</span> that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.<br />- <strong>Select Ports</strong> group - displays all port types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.<br /></span>- <strong>Select All</strong> - selects all check boxes that are expanded from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.<br />- <strong>Clear All</strong> - clears all check boxes, even those that are collapsed (deep nested), from the element tree located on <span>the left side of the dialog.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34fa4ff9-c35d-49d7-855f-d85a81261e84"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All Electrical ports (in this example only one <em>ElectricityPort</em> port) of the <em>Data Transfer</em> Part Property are selected <span>in the element tree by using the <span><strong>Select Ports</strong> group</span> in the context-sensitive panel </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">of the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Using the context-sensitive panel of the Display Parts/Ports dialog when trying to select required ports."><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_ports_using_context_sensitive_panel.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="0c896059-7faf-4250-883f-0103ccc569cb"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>Specify the layout options in the <strong>Options</strong> area:<br /><ul><li>Parts layout: select the <strong>Layout Parts</strong> check box if you want the Part Property shapes to be arranged in the automatic layout.</li><li>Usage of layout template: <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">select the </span><strong>Use Layout Template</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> check box if you want the layout template to be applied. When this check box is selected, the Part Property, which is included in the layout template, and all its Ports from the layout template are selected automatically.</span></li><li>Showing connectors: select the <strong>Show Connectors</strong> check box if you want the connectors to be displayed.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li>Ports layout on Part Property shape:<br />- <strong>Top/Bottom</strong> - displays ports on the Part Property shape from top to bottom.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The ports are layouted from top to bottom on the Compressor Part property shape." ac:alt="The ports are layouted from top to bottom on the Compressor Part property shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_layout_top_bottom.png" /></ac:image><br />- <strong>Left/Right </strong> - displays ports on the Part Property shape from left to right.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The ports are layouted from left to right on the Compressor Part property shape." ac:alt="The ports are layouted from left to right on the Compressor Part property shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_layout_left_right.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="0d3d3db7-c16d-46be-ab2c-468be107608c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After those steps, all parts, ports, other properties that were selected in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /></span></p><h4>Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu</h4><p class="confluence-link">In the structure diagrams that have already been created, you can display <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>, <span class="confluence-link">properties</span>, and hide all <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> by using the commands from:</p><ul><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="e6e1d3fc-c7d6-4b7c-a105-3c9f9e0b041e">The <strong>Display</strong> button on the diagram toolbar.</li><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="6600c34c-0eed-4e2d-8943-0cb5c9bac986">The <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display</strong> command group under the shortcut menu.</span></li></ul><p>You can also use <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>the <strong>Display </strong>button</span></span> and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display</strong> command group from the shortcut menu as</span> their functions are identical. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All those commands are interactive and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Part Property shape is selected on the diagram pane, the menu contains only the commands that can be applied to the selected <span class="confluence-link">Part</span> Property shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, the menu shows commands that can be applied to the whole diagram.</span></span></p><p>These <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link">commands under the <strong>Display</strong> button and shortcut menu are comprised of </span></span>elements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:</p><ul><li data-uuid="05b9e036-9464-43c2-9c44-75f182a59a0f">Metatypes.</li><li data-uuid="3720ecd7-8c5b-4db5-9366-dedd6a3672f3">Stereotypes.</li><li data-uuid="5928f08c-5d39-45b5-85eb-a730b1dc0d9a">Types stereotypes.</li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link">The following procedures will show you how to:</span></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="d5c7da92-ea38-4251-844a-b1e9beb2cac1"><ac:link ac:anchor="button"><ac:link-body>How to display Ports, Properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button.</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="3e64e3e6-7600-43c8-8d83-6b6de46a6f7a"><ac:link ac:anchor="shortcut"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="436a6722-c96f-4fbe-af19-14f5785cc9f3"><ac:link ac:anchor="hide"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to hide Ports.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="151e0ef2-cf5f-42c8-9b7b-b70191dd1dcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">button</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display ports, properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3ff476de-a712-4490-97e3-ed593d2c7bfb">Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li data-uuid="de698ecb-3211-4946-92a7-444e6fe049b3">O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="After selecting the Data Transfer and Control Groups Internal Block diagram pane and clicking the Display button, opened menu allows to display properties and/or ports."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_element.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="f899dab3-a96a-46b8-bc98-21bb5f0a13d7"><p>From the opened command menu, select required properties or ports to display:<br />        - All ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ef07d2a-8928-47ac-9bee-363460e3bac9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.</p><p><span>After selecting the required command from the</span> <strong>Display</strong> button<span>, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.</span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f27456dd-5abb-464b-b57a-d14bee0cbd81"><ac:parameter ac:name="">shortcut</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display ports, properties using the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7ee4d15f-ea13-44a4-8394-c255fc425310">Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li data-uuid="3b575662-48fd-4a89-9c63-ec36b27a7beb">From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Display</strong> command group.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_command.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="8022b43e-2bf0-4a33-ba0f-7ba39621bdc9"><p>From the opened command menu, select required properties to display:<br />        - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="327c3ff9-f8cc-4d9e-a0b5-0c763e6c790e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first-level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.<br />After selecting the required command from the <strong>Display</strong> command menu, the appropriate properties' ports are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4794c2e6-db49-41b6-ac94-03bcb79efedc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">hide</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To hide ports</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="42afebbf-54ab-4dc3-8119-14fe5a6464f7">Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes. O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>  and choose<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.</span></li><li data-uuid="4d20f7a7-f582-4591-84b5-e2c8914a682c"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt;<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After selecting the command, the <span class="confluence-link">ports </span>of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane, but not removed from the model</span>.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff629db0-f28c-4b9f-aa61-48e542d03bba"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Hide All Ports</strong> command doesn't appear, the selection or <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">diagram </span>doesn't contain any <span class="confluence-link">ports </span>that can be hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:title="Hiding all Proxy Ports of the Part Property typed by Data Transfer Block." ac:alt="Hiding all Proxy Ports of the Part Property typed by Data Transfer Block."><ri:attachment ri:filename="hide_all_ports.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p></li></ul><h4>Using the smart manipulator toolbar<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>When the properties are already displayed on the<span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML v1 diagram descriptions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[structure diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>, you can display their direct <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> using the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button from the smart manipulator toolbar. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">It only allows you to display first level ports, and does not allow you to display deeply nested <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog</span>.</span> The steps below show how to display ports of a selected property shape.</span></span></p><p>To display Ports on shapes</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="45be4179-a173-40f7-8517-93b2db25a32d">Select<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> the required</span> element shape or a diagram frame.</span> </span></li><li data-uuid="ba56b495-b99c-424b-8ddb-5d7b4e95b5a2"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">From the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display All Ports" ac:alt="Display All Ports"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><span>All first level Ports of the selected shape will be displayed on the diagram pane.</span> <br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb6590f-3710-4154-9154-2c9ce8edfa7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> If the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button is not available, the selected element or <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">diagram </span>doesn't own any Port.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:title="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the Part Property shape typed by Data Transfer Block." ac:alt="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the Part Property shape typed by Data Transfer Block."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></p></li></ol><h4>Using the drag-and-drop operation in the SysML Internal Block diagram</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The most efficient way to create a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> and display it with all <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">SysML Internal Block diagram</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> is by dragging the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link></span> or Property elements. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This method only allows you to display first-level properties, and does not allow you to display deeply nested properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog. The following procedures will show you:</span></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="2f10c228-5188-4b01-a67a-cdabe1522219"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_class"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a Part Property shape and how to display all its ports when dragging the Block element.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="1937fb34-e4ec-496b-9487-0690c4c6ade7"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_property"><ac:link-body>How to display a Part Property shape and all its <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> by dragging the Part Property element.</ac:link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9f47650-536d-46c6-8f68-c5881f221387"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_class</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can create a new Part Property by dragging the <span class="confluence-link">Block </span>element <span class="confluence-link">from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>to the SysML Internal Block diagram pane</span>. The newly created Part Property will be typed by the dragged <span class="confluence-link">Block</span> and displayed on the diagram pane. If this <span class="confluence-link">Block</span> has <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> defined, you can display them by using the long drag, and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3). <br /></span></p><p><strong> </strong></p><p>To create and display a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> by dragging a Block element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="92ce0694-1f28-4901-a230-babfc6faf2d4"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drag a single or a multiple Block element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="8ad923f2-33cc-49d0-be83-f80e2a1a34d4"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drag a single or a multiple Block element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, </span>and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag). <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol</strong> command.</span><br />The new Part Property is created in the model, and displayed on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The newly created Part Property is typed by the dragged Block automatically.</span></span> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><p>To create and display a Part Property and display all its ports by dragging a Block element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="0860d016-011b-4de0-a300-263df149682f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drag a single or a multiple Block element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li><li data-uuid="81620624-add5-4573-8f33-72bc51bd7c49"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The new Part Property is created in the model, and displayed with all its ports on the diagram pane. The newly created Part Property is typed by the dragged Block automatically.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35a694c8-df84-455d-8e7f-eaa249205f99"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following:<br />        - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong> command.<br />        - Select the shape and press Alt+B.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a3ba930-c9af-4a64-8b14-28f87fe613a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 3</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After selecting the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command, and</span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">dragging (long drag) the Block element to <em>Control and Air Distribution Group</em> Internal Block diagram pane, it</span> </span>creates a new Part Property typed by a <em>Air Distribution</em> Block and displays all ports on it.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_block.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="593bf531-1a65-4783-ac36-35a678036e74"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_property</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can display a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> shape by dragging a Part Property element from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> pane. If this Part Property has <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> defined, you can display them by using the long drag, and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).<br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The following procedures describe how to display Part Property shape and all its <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> by dragging the Part Property element.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To display a Part Property shape by dragging a Part Property element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="067f13c3-1334-4ad6-8f63-bebaaa7fe512">Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the Model Browser onto the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="07d66c1a-a294-4bf7-8c11-80ba29524de2">Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the<strong> Create Part Property Symbol</strong> command. </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Part Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane</span>.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>To display a Part Property with all of its ports by dragging a Part Property element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="8318c4af-e706-486f-b747-b2ce5b0fadfb">Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li><li data-uuid="3f9d2ce5-6bf4-4d5b-bc7c-1cf329126c9c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the<strong> Create Part Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Part Property shape is displayed with all its ports on that shape.<br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9bbc855a-c19c-4afe-8c7c-56e981ef78a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 4</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After selecting the </span><strong>Create Part Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> command, and dragging (long drag) the Part Property element to</span><em> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Control and Air Distribution Group</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Internal Block diagram, it displays Part Property shape and all ports on it.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_part_property.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After completing the steps outlined above, the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Property ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">shape and/or all its ports are displayed on the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></p><p><br /></p><h3>Displaying properties of a port</h3><p>If you display the properties of a port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the port shape and Part Property compartments will be enabled for that port.</p><p>To enable Part Property compartments on the port shape</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="80e27c1f-2712-4675-a3bf-5fc7c771bb2d">Select a port shape whose type has properties.</li><li data-uuid="5b172f13-c700-4c92-be0c-8275d490e264">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, point to the <strong>Display</strong> command group.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="c9600801-5ac2-4287-8f41-5e78a630093a">Select the required properties to display.</li></ol><p>The properties are displayed inside the port shape. The Part Property compartments are enabled for the port.</p><p>The example below shows the <em>p1</em> port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the port shape are the <em>Pressure Status</em> Flow Property and the <em>suspensionSensor</em> Part Property, which are the properties of the selected port type <em>iSuspension</em> Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the <em>p1</em> port.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_compartments_on_port_shape.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="37a7a71d-b5d0-43ba-8661-a9616d5738df"><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Applying Stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span> </span></li><li Interface]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="099b3f0f-548e-4476-99bf-75029dc5fefe"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enforce Ports Compatibility mode" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="054ac21b-f6d1-4d7f-9dcf-28e2a58e99b3"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Hiding direction arrow on port shape" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Hiding direction arrow on Port shape]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="3ccf341c-b046-422b-bab4-42172d4ad847"><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Property path notation" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967117 space=MT version=5 -->
## PAGE 05266: Displaying paths

- page_id: `243967117`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967117/Displaying+paths
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:49:22.546+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality allows you to visualize relationships that are created between elements in the model, but are not displayed on the diagram pane. There are two options to display paths among the element shapes:

- 
- 

displayedTo display paths between selected element shapes

1. On a diagram pane, select two or more elements.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display In-Between Paths .
  - On the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] and select Display In-Between Paths .

- 
  - On the main menu, click the **Analyze** > **Display In-Between Paths**. The **Display In-Between Paths** command is available only when two or more shapes are selected.

The existing paths between selected element shapes are displayed on the diagram pane.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

The paths won't be displayed if: 
 - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram. 
 - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane. 
Paths to self also will not be displayed.

not_displayedTo display all existing paths of the selected shape

1. On a diagram pane, select one or more elements.
2. Do one of the following: All the existing paths among the selected shape and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed. If the element has a path to self, this path will be displayed as well.
  1. 
    - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display All Paths .
    - On the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] and select Display All Paths .
  - 
    - On the main menu, click the Analyze > Display All Paths .

#### TIP: Example of the Display All Paths command

Example of the Display All Paths command

This example presents the workflow on representing the paths between shapes on the diagram pane when they are already created in the model.

The Class shapes without any paths between them are represented in the *Room infrastructure model* Class diagram. In this case, paths between those classes exist in the Containment tree under the Relations node (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you want to visualize how the *Workplace* Class is related with other shapes displayed on the diagram pane, you can do so easily by using the**Display All Paths** command. One way to reach this command is from the shortcut menu of the selected shape (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After selecting the**Display All Paths** command, all paths between the *Workplace* Class and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed (see the figure below). The diagram shows that the *Room* consists of between one and an arbitrary number of *Workplaces*. The *Workplace* consists of *Electric Extension* and *Works Station*. Also, the *Workplace* can contain *Duplex Power Outlet*, *External HDD2*, *Digital Camera*, and *External_HDD*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display paths among element shapes automatically when displaying elements

1. On the main menu, select Options > Environment .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space=''] , find the Display Paths on Element Drop option and set it's value to true .
3. Click OK .
4. Select the elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] and drag them on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='']. The shapes and paths between them are displayed. WarningThe paths won't be displayed if: 
 - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram. 
 - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with paths and relationships' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Paths' space='DEVG']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Composite structure diagram** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='composite structure diagram.mdzip'].
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>This functionality allows you to visualize relationships that are created between elements in the model, but are not displayed on the diagram pane. There are two options to display paths among the element shapes:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3e9d88a2-9363-4093-88e7-e6b3b13168c0"><ac:link ac:anchor="displayed"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying paths only between selected element shapes.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="4263af3c-8238-43a0-9fe8-adf544a22a32"><ac:link ac:anchor="not_displayed"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying all existing paths among the shapes.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a5c1a99-dc96-41e3-bbf0-54960c3255f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">displayed</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display paths between selected element shapes</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="db721365-a551-4143-a513-baa15ca804ac"><p>On a diagram pane, select two or more elements.</p></li><li data-uuid="5e459070-bbb8-4b77-9b6f-2c4871618d15">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="a1ef07b9-919a-4bfe-92f3-757024d0bf49"><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the main menu, click the <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df015cf7-dece-418b-bf0b-e0e34099a919"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong> command is available only when two or more shapes are selected.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">The existing paths between selected element shapes are displayed on the diagram pane. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13128f02-7f4d-4518-a6cf-efd94ab17c80"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The paths won't be displayed if:<br />       - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram.<br />       - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.<br />Paths to self also will not be displayed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5110f86-ce4d-44a9-86c7-8b422d41d784"><ac:parameter ac:name="">not_displayed</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display all existing paths of the selected shape</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="779e5831-3500-44ca-92c4-21b4df5eb149">On a diagram pane, select one or more elements.</li><li data-uuid="084a728d-1763-40ae-91e9-e4c4b68513de">Do one of the following:<ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>On the main menu, click the <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul>All the existing paths among the selected shape and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed. If the element has a path to self, this path will be displayed as well.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe1f11cf-477d-4351-82cb-af2526300618"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of the Display All Paths command</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This example presents the workflow on representing the paths between shapes on the diagram pane when they are already created in the model.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The Class shapes without any paths between them are represented in the <em>Room infrastructure model</em> Class diagram. In this case, paths between those classes exist in the Containment tree under the Relations node (see the figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="relations_node_and_class_shapes.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">If you want to visualize how the <em>Workplace</em> Class is related with other shapes displayed on the diagram pane, you can do so easily by using the<strong> Display All Paths</strong> command. One way to reach this command is from the shortcut menu of the selected shape (see the figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-11-3 18:13:4.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">After selecting the<strong> Display All Paths</strong> command, all paths between the <em>Workplace</em> Class and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed (see the figure below). The diagram shows that the <em>Room</em> consists of between one and an arbitrary number of <em>Workplaces</em>. The <em>Workplace</em> consists of <em>Electric Extension</em> and <em>Works Station</em>. Also, the <em>Workplace</em> can contain <em>Duplex Power Outlet</em>, <em>External HDD2</em>, <em>Digital Camera</em>, and <em>External_HDD</em>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-11-3 18:13:18.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To display paths among element shapes automatically when displaying elements</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="10921e2b-0bd1-4065-80ac-8d54c24ef3d8">On the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="72156740-7f58-42dc-91cf-2219c01eb359">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Environment Options dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, find the <strong>Display Paths on Element Drop</strong> option and set it's value to <em>true</em>.</li><li data-uuid="b00df968-a80a-444a-90c9-b0ff362babf9">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="220a18b1-75aa-44d6-b7d5-861a4e8f7e2c"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the elements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> and drag them on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">The shapes and paths between them are displayed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99aac734-b4b5-4ee4-afa4-790fc249f3f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The paths won't be displayed if:<br />       - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram.<br />       - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="56304e0b-b190-401e-a45e-ebb68ca9a2bd"><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="b479432f-a580-46b6-bcc2-223de9bf1657"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with paths and relationships" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="2ae50c17-ef86-4563-8a14-600869a812da"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Paths" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Composite structure diagram</span></strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6af24d2f-7e75-4cc1-9328-a828671fd2cb">Download<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite structure diagram.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></span></span></em></li><li data-uuid="a09eb2a9-6824-41ed-a226-7c211e9dc9be">Find in modeling tool <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.</span></em></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563248 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05267: Displaying possible Operational Exchanges on selected Operational Activity Edge

- page_id: `249563248`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563248/Displaying+possible+Operational+Exchanges+on+selected+Operational+Activity+Edge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:20.839+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint > NOV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display possible Operational Exchanges on the selected Operational Activity Edge

1. Select Operational Activity Edge and on the smart manipulator, click the Operational Exchange Manager button.
2. Select the possible Operational Exchanges to realize.

You can also click the **Operational Exchange** button on the diagram palette and click the Operational Activity Edge to open the **Operational Exchange** creation wizard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To display possible Operational Exchanges on the selected Operational Activity Edge</p><hr /><ol><li>Select Operational Activity Edge and on the smart manipulator, click the <strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> button.</li><li>Select the possible Operational Exchanges to realize.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can also click the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> button on the diagram palette and click the Operational Activity Edge to open the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> creation wizard.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968314 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05269: Displaying rake icon

- page_id: `243968314`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968314/Displaying+rake+icon
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:55.923+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols > Displaying icon or image
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use [IMAGE alt='' src=''] for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element is decomposed and has an inner structure. Double-click the element with rake icon to open the internal diagram.

The rake icon is shown on:

- Call Behavior actions that can refer to another Activity diagram.
- Interaction Uses that can refer to another Sequence diagram.
- Packages that can refer to another Package diagrams.
- Parts that can refer to another Internal Block diagram.
- Use cases can be realized by other behavior diagrams such as Activity, State Machine and Sequence.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide rake icon

1. Right-click the symbol and select Symbol Properties or press Alt+Enter.
2. Set the Show Rake Icon property value to false .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can use <ac:image ac:title="Rake" ac:alt="Rake"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake.png" /></ac:image>  for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element is decomposed and has an inner structure. Double-click the element with rake icon to open the internal diagram.</p><p>The rake icon is shown on:</p><ul><li>Call Behavior actions that can refer to another Activity diagram.</li><li>Interaction Uses that can refer to another Sequence diagram.</li><li>Packages that can refer to another Package diagrams.</li><li>Parts that can refer to another Internal Block diagram.</li><li>Use cases can be realized by other behavior diagrams such as Activity, State Machine and Sequence.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Rake icon on symbol" ac:alt="Rake icon on symbol"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake_icon.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To hide rake icon</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the symbol and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> or press Alt+Enter.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Rake Icon</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561797 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05270: Displaying Rake icon on symbol

- page_id: `249561797`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561797/Displaying+Rake+icon+on+symbol
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T12:23:35.674+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Working with model elements in SysML v1
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use rake icon for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element has an internal structure. Double-click the element to open the internal diagram. The rake icon is shown by default on the symbol.

To hide rake icon

1. Right-click the symbol.
2. Select Symbol Properties from shortcut menu or press Alt+Enter.
3. Set the Show Rake Icon property value to false.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can use rake icon for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element has an internal structure. Double-click the element to open the internal diagram. The rake icon is shown by default on the symbol.</p><p>To hide rake icon</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the symbol.</li><li>Select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> from shortcut menu or press Alt+Enter.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Rake Icon</strong> property value to false.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:alt="Rake icon on symbol" ac:title="Rake icon on symbol"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake_icon.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967110 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05271: Displaying related elements

- page_id: `243967110`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967110/Displaying+related+elements
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T12:33:44.013+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When trying to extend your model or visualize additional elements on the diagram pane, you can represent symbols that are related to a selected symbol via relationships.This functionality allows for displaying shapes and paths among those shapes which have already been created in the model.

After selecting the **Display Related Elements** command, the **Display Related Elements** dialog opens. This dialog consists of the following sections (see the figure below):

- Element box (1) shows the name of the selected element shape of which you are going to display its related elements.
- Relations area (2) lists all relationship types which connect the selected element shape with other elements.
- Expand Relations area (3) allows you to select the direction of the relationships which you want to display on the diagram pane.
- Scope area (4) allows you to select the search scope of the elements related to the selected element.
- Depth area (5) allows you to specify the search range for the selected relationships.
- Always create new symbols option (6) allows you to create a symbol even if a related element already has a symbol in the diagram.
- Layout related elements option (7) lays out new symbols by using the default diagram layout.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following procedure will guide you through using the **Display Related Elements** command and dialog when you want to represent all related elements via selected relationship types of the selected shape on the diagram pane. The functionality works for the entire diagram, single shapes, or numerous selected shapes.

To display related elements

1. Select a diagram frame or any number of shapes on the diagram pane.
2. Open the **Display Related Elements** dialog in one of the following ways:
  - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display Related Elements .
  - On the diagram toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_related_elements.png'] , and select Display Related Elements .
  - On the main menu, click **Analyze** > **Display Related Elements**. ImportantThe **Display Related Elements** dialog cannot be opened if the selected element shape or diagram doesn't contain any related elements. Example 1A quicker way to display related elements of the selected shape is to use its shortcut menu, and select **Display** > **Display Related Elements**. The example below demonstrates the shortcut menu of the Class named *Shipment*. It is showing the need to display related elements of the *Shipment* Class in the *Product Shipment object diagram*.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the opened dialog, specify:
  - In the Relations area (2), select check boxes of the listed relationship types.
  - In the Expand Relations area (3), select the direction of the relationships: - Incoming - in which the element participates as client. - Outgoing - in which the element participates as supplier. - Both- in which the element participates as both client and supplier.
  - In the Scope area (4), choose the location in which the related elements should be found: - Whole Project - search in the whole model. - Package - search in the package under which the selected element is created.
  - In the Depth area (5), select the search range for the relationships: - Indefinite - search for all possible relationships. - Definite - search for a specified level of relationships.
4. Select the check box of the Always create new symbols option to create a symbol even when the related element already has a symbol displayed on the diagram pane.
5. Select the check box of the **Layout related elements**option to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space=''] new symbols by using the default diagram [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space='']. NoteThe position of the selected element shape can be changed after selecting the **Layout related elements** check box. Example 2For example, if you would like to display all related elements of the *Shipment* Class that are connected through the Generalization relationship, you can find them according to the following criteria:- The direction of the generalization relation is both incoming and outgoing. 
- The related elements to be searched for are in the whole project. 
- The search depth of the relationship is one level.If you want to layout related elements after the display, but don't want to create new symbols which are already displayed in the *Product Shipment object diagram*.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click **OK**. 
According to the specified relationship type, scope, depth, direction, layout, and creation options, all related elements of the selected shape are represented as shapes and paths on the diagram pane. Example 3After opening the **Display Related Elements** dialog (Example 1), and defining the search criteria in it (Example 2), two Classes (*VendorShipment* and *CustomerShipment*) related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the *Product Shipment object diagram* pane. The figure below is the result of following the steps described in Example 1 and Example 2.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements in the diagram' space='DEVG'] (Open API)

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Inventory Control System** sample model. To open this sample go to*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\case studies\Inventory Control System.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>When trying to extend your model or visualize additional elements on the diagram pane, you can represent symbols that are related to a selected symbol via relationships.This functionality allows for displaying shapes and paths among those shapes which have already been created in the model.</p><p>After selecting the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> command, the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog opens. This dialog consists of the following sections (see the figure below):</p><ul><li data-uuid="ca7df487-8f30-4cea-9917-74db4fa3bac6"><em>Element</em> box (1) shows the name of the selected element shape of which you are going to display its related elements.</li><li data-uuid="672d4acd-fc2d-464c-a1ae-054ce7bc06b9"><em>Relations</em> area (2) lists all relationship types which connect the selected element shape with other elements.</li><li data-uuid="f82802c3-4aa4-46b6-8f8b-3b971d9850a6"><em>Expand Relations</em> area (3) allows you to select the direction of the relationships which you want to display on the diagram pane.</li><li data-uuid="3468fbf0-8baa-4ea2-859b-bd0c70885ac2"><em>Scope</em> area (4) allows you to select the search scope of the elements related to the selected element.</li><li data-uuid="f9241358-689f-4037-bc4b-bf99e729569e"><em>Depth</em> area (5) allows you to specify the search range for the selected relationships.</li><li data-uuid="ccbb0544-ea3c-451f-8017-4a9bba69ed85">Always create new symbols option (6) allows you to create a symbol even if a related element already has a symbol in the diagram.</li><li data-uuid="f8beaef8-40cb-45b8-8393-f2549d952a79"><em>Layout related elements</em> option (7) lays out new symbols by using the default diagram layout.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="The seven areas in the Display Related Elements dialog." ac:alt="The seven areas in the Display Related Elements dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_dialog_areas.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The following procedure will guide you through using the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> command and dialog when you want to represent all related elements via selected relationship types of the selected shape on the diagram pane. The functionality works for the entire diagram, single shapes, or numerous selected shapes.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display related elements</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9d77042a-ffb7-4e4b-8f82-1de35dcf3c66">Select a diagram frame or any number of shapes on the diagram pane.</li><li data-uuid="17b2882c-7df2-42de-919c-7b08a42f6963"><p>Open the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select<strong> Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements.png" /></ac:image>, and select <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</li><li><p>On the main menu, click <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8506d135-0ccf-4672-bb3e-67a2276a84cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog cannot be opened if the selected element shape or diagram doesn't contain any related elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71ae0b19-4229-488a-b7c0-35a3f7dd9599"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A quicker way to display related elements of the selected shape is to use its shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>. The example below demonstrates the shortcut menu of the Class named <em>Shipment</em>. It is showing the need to display related elements of the <em>Shipment</em> Class in the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Display Related Elements command under the Display command group in the shortcut menu of the Shipment Class." ac:alt="The Display Related Elements command under the Display command group in the shortcut menu of the Shipment Class."><ri:attachment ri:filename="shortcut_menu_display_related_elements.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="def08534-f443-4781-8249-d6b28cb11e63">In the opened dialog, specify:<br /><ul><li>In the Relations area (2), select check boxes of the listed relationship types.</li><li>In the Expand Relations area (3), select the direction of the relationships:<br />- <strong>Incoming</strong> - in which the element participates as client.<br />- <strong>Outgoing</strong> - in which the element participates as supplier.<br />- <strong>Both-</strong>in which the element participates as both client and supplier.</li><li>In the Scope area (4), choose the location in which the related elements should be found:<br />-<strong> Whole Project</strong> - search in the whole model.<br />- <strong>Package </strong>- search in the package under which the selected element is created.</li><li>In the Depth area (5), select the search range for the relationships:<br />-<strong> Indefinite</strong> - search for all possible relationships.<br />- <strong>Definite</strong> - search for a specified level of relationships.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="50c17191-5544-49dd-b79c-5d1d82982e39">Select the check box of the <strong>Always create new symbols</strong> option to create a symbol even when the related element already has a symbol displayed on the diagram pane.</li><li data-uuid="3d8990da-0abf-4070-ba67-90ce89c94202"><p>Select the check box of the <strong>Layout related elements </strong>option to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Layout" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[layout]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> new symbols by using the default diagram <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Layout" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[layout]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca55d086-99f0-4658-b091-e71aff3855e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The position of the selected element shape can be changed after selecting the <strong>Layout related elements</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d05eaf9-951d-4f91-a2e3-71340aa6934f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, if you would like to display all related elements of the <em>Shipment</em> Class that are connected through the Generalization relationship, you can find them according to the following criteria:</p><p>- The direction of the generalization relation is both incoming and outgoing.<br />- The related elements to be searched for are in the whole project.<br />- The search depth of the relationship is one level.</p><p>If you want to layout related elements after the display, but don't want to create new symbols which are already displayed in the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Display Related Elements dialog of the Shipment Class." ac:alt="The Display Related Elements dialog of the Shipment Class."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_dialog_of_class_shipment.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="45249a64-7b34-41ac-a6c6-b84e2e3b193f"><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />According to the specified relationship type, scope, depth, direction, layout, and creation options, all related elements of the selected shape are represented as shapes and paths on the diagram pane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d1e5e9e-6684-4fd6-b528-046e6f11e36c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 3</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After opening the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog (Example 1), and defining the search criteria in it (Example 2), two Classes (<em>VendorShipment</em> and <em>CustomerShipment</em>) related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em> pane. The figure below is the result of following the steps described in Example 1 and Example 2.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="VendorShipment and CustomerShipment Classes related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the the Product Shipment object diagram pane." ac:alt="VendorShipment and CustomerShipment Classes related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the the Product Shipment object diagram pane."><ri:attachment ri:filename="related_elements_of_the_shipment_class_displayed_on_the_diagram_pane.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="1c11c2b4-45cb-46c3-a748-1003c3b20dea"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="d752320d-7321-42f1-8d12-e5436b8674c0"><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Layout" /></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="2cd8f578-8ab0-4895-9f79-ca978dd876bf"><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements in the diagram" /></ac:link> (Open API)</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Inventory Control System</strong> sample model. To open this sample go to<em> &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\case studies\Inventory Control System.mdzip.</em></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966884 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05272: Displaying row elements hierarchically

- page_id: `243966884`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966884/Displaying+row+elements+hierarchically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:12.099+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables > Hierarchy in tables
- labels: ['nested-instances', 'display-nested-elements', 'display-nested-instances', 'display-mode', 'complete-tree', 'comapct-tree', 'specify-query', 'hierarchy-in-table', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Hierarchical tables are very useful for managing and reviewing data. Hierarchy in a table is a tree-like structure where all the rows are listed according to the elements containment.

**Important**

The hierarchy in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space=''] is displayed according to an instance value that is a slot value of another Instance Specification. Composite instances are displayed.

##### Displaying elements hierarchically in a tableDisplaying elements hierarchically

To display elements hierarchically

1. In the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
2. Under the Display Mode option choose: The hierarchy is created in the table by listing all the elements as they are shown in the Containment tree.
  - **Complete tree** to display row elements in a hierarchy where all the owners are represented in separate rows. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - **Compact tree**to display row elements in a hierarchy where the common owners are grouped together in a separate row. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying elements in custom hierarchyDisplaying row elements in a custom hierarchy

To display elements in a custom hierarchy

1. In the Criteria area, next to the Scope box, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='scope_query.png'] icon . [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_query_button.png']
2. Specify the expression to collect table elements:

- Select Containment to display the decomposition of elements related through the Containment relationship.
- Select Structure to display the decomposition of Part Properties related through the Composition relationship.
- Select Custom to display the decomposition of elements related through any criteria of your choice.

If you have selected Custom, please [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying expression in the Query dialog

##### Expanding/collapsing nodes in a hierarchical table

To expand/collapse nodes in a hierarchical table

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] / [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the name column. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Select the specific row, hold Ctrl, and then press the right/left arrow key on the keyboard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="32a368a3-d9a8-4850-aab3-22a4c1917f37">Hierarchical</ac:inline-comment-marker> tables <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e7b8fd12-c662-412a-a1c0-e52637a8b825">are very useful for managing and reviewing data.</ac:inline-comment-marker> Hierarchy in a table is a tree-like structure where all the rows are listed according to the elements containment.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92442073-15e8-48f5-ac69-a051ee9f98c7"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="title"><strong>Important</strong></p><p>The hierarchy in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Instance table" /></ac:link> is displayed according to an instance value that is a slot value of another Instance Specification. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966881 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 05273: Displaying/hiding element numbers in a row

- page_id: `243966881`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966881/Displaying+hiding+element+numbers+in+a+row
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:12.018+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables > Working with rows
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new table, elements represented as rows are automatically numbered in the name column. The following procedure is important for those who modify existing tables created before Version 18.4.

To display/hide element numbers in the table

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. Set the **Show Element Number** property value to *true* to display element numbers, or *false* to hide them*.*The element numbers are displayed in the name columns before the element name (see the following example). [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating a new table, elements represented as rows are automatically numbered in the name column. The following procedure is important for those who modify existing tables created before Version 18.4.</p><p>To display/hide element numbers in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Specification</strong> window of the table.</li><li><p>Set the <strong>Show Element Number</strong> property value to <em>true</em> to display element numbers, or <em>false</em> to hide them<em>.<br /></em>The element numbers are displayed in the name columns before the element name (see the following example).</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Requirement numbers are displayed in the Magic Library Requirement table." ac:alt="The Requirement numbers are displayed in the Magic Library Requirement table."><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_number_in_table.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562622 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05274: DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model

- page_id: `249562622`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562622/DIV-1+Conceptual+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:37.694+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-1-conceptual-data-model', 'div-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

393867802

393867804

393867803

**Description**

The DIV-1, a new DoDAF-described Model in DoDAF V2.0, addresses the information concepts at a high-level on an operational architecture.

The DIV-1 is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

The intended usage of the DIV-1 includes:

- Information requirements.
- Information hierarchy.

**Implementation**

DIV-1 can be represented using a DIV-1 diagram which is based in the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The DIV-1 defines each kind of information classes associated with the Architectural Description scope, mission, or business as its own entity, with its associated attributes and relationships. These entity definitions correlate to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] information elements and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''] inputs, outputs, and controls.

393867801

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating DIV-1 diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48590108-1926-459f-9e25-2339b4a933a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393867802</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4061e9b-6058-4157-a031-d160ad84aaaa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393867804</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bed3cf98-c038-4588-aab0-36b7c438fc2a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393867803</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The DIV-1, a new DoDAF-described Model in DoDAF V2.0, addresses the information concepts at a high-level on an operational architecture.</p><p>The DIV-1 is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.</p><p>The intended usage of the DIV-1 includes:</p><ul><li>Information requirements.</li><li>Information hierarchy.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation<br /></strong></p><p>DIV-1 can be represented using a DIV-1 diagram which is based in the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model" ac:alt="DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="div_1.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The DIV-1 defines each kind of information classes associated with the Architectural Description scope, mission, or business as its own <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">entity, with its associated attributes and relationships. These entity</span> definitions correlate to<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> information elements and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> inputs, outputs, and controls.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f868ca3-1090-4090-850b-8b30b31e2f55"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393867801</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating DIV-1 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````
