<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562627 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05275: DIV-2 Logical Data Model

- page_id: `249562627`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562627/DIV-2+Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:37.905+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-2-logical-data-model', 'div-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394056138

394056140

394056139

**Description**

The DIV-2 allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level data elements are included in the descriptions.

**Implementation**

DIV-2 can be represented using:

- DIV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='div_2.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

Data described in a DIV-2 may be related to Information in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space=''] or and Activity Resource (where the Resource is Data) flow object in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='']. This relation may be a simple subtype, where the Data is a proceduralized (structured) way of describing something. Recall that Information describes something. Alternatively, the relation may be complex using Information and Data whole part (and overlap) relationships.

The DIV-2 information elements can be constrained and validated by the capture of business requirements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space=''].

The information elements modeled in the DIV-2 also capture the information content of messages that connect life-lines in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description' space=''].

The DIV-2 may capture elements required due to Standards in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space=''].

394056137

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating DIV-2 diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90c63e7e-8a22-4bff-bf30-2678d1fcf697"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394056138</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69bca6a5-e0b0-4036-99fb-d81750a0819b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394056140</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="80a5478a-dddc-48ce-9fe7-42ff4d581af3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394056139</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The DIV-2 allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level data elements are included in the descriptions.</p><p><strong>Implementation<br /></strong></p><p>DIV-2 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>DIV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="DIV-2 Logical Data Model" ac:alt="DIV-2 Logical Data Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="div_2.png" /></ac:image></li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>SysML Block Definition diagram.</li></ul><p>Data described in a DIV-2 may be related to Information in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-1 High Level Operational Concept Graphic]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or and Activity Resource (where the Resource is Data) flow object in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This relation may be a simple subtype, where the Data is a proceduralized (structured) way of describing something. Recall that Information describes something. Alternatively, the relation may be complex using Information and Data whole part (and overlap) relationships.</p><p>The DIV-2 information elements can be constrained and validated by the capture of business requirements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-6a Operational Rules Model" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The information elements modeled in the DIV-2 also capture the information content of messages that connect life-lines in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-6c Event-Trace Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>The DIV-2 may capture elements required due to Standards in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="StdV-1 Standards Profile" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="StdV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65d590b1-f3f4-41f7-a0a6-4e3241ae8e59"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394056137</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating DIV-2 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562632 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05276: DIV-3 Physical Data Model

- page_id: `249562632`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562632/DIV-3+Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:38.021+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-3-physical-data-model', 'div-3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394180921

394180924

394180923

**Description**

The DIV-3 defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. The Physical Schema is one of the models closest to actual system design in DoDAF. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space=''] is actually implemented.

While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

**Implementation**

DIV-3 can be represented using:

- DIV-3 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='DIV-3.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition Diagram.

TheData Modelis one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='']) is actually implemented.

394180919

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating DIV-3 diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Physical Schema is one of the models closest to actual system design in DoDAF. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="DIV-2 Logical Data Model" /></ac:link> is actually implemented.</p><p>While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.</p><p><strong>Implementation<br /></strong></p><p>DIV-3 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>DIV-3 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="DIV-3.png" /></ac:image></li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>SysML Block Definition Diagram.</li></ul><p>The<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Data Model </span>is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="DIV-2 Logical Data Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[DIV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) is actually implemented.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc0dfe18-156c-47fd-bc5a-aaaeaeeefe5e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394180919</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating DIV-3 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970686 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05277: Document Report Designer diagram

- page_id: `243970686`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970686/Document+Report+Designer+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:53.101+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Report Designer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

983358622

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

983358634

985841017

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

983358624

#### NOTE: Note

Note

In this version, Document Report Designer diagram supports only the diagram for making the Microsoft Word document (DOCX) template.

#### Using the Document Report Designer diagram

In this section, you will learn how to create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a DOCX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.

To create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a template

1. Right-click a Package in the Containment tree, select Report Designer Diagrams > Document Report Designer Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating Report Designer Diagram.png']
2. Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.
3. Arrange diagram elements as desired. [ATTACHMENT filename='Arranging Diagram Elements.png']
4. In the Containment tree, right-click **Document Report Designer Diagram** and select **Generate Template** to generate the template. NoteOnly symbols of elements on the diagram are used to generate the code template. Other elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate the code template. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Refine more layout, style, and code logic in Microsoft Word.
6. Add the complete template to Report Wizard.
7. Generate the report from the added template. [ATTACHMENT filename='Report Generated from Added Template.png']

#### Diagram elements

Document Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive.

###### Text Box

Text Box represents free, uninterpreted static-text area in which you can type anything in single or multiple lines, from plain-format text to Velocity code, one element per paragraph. You can also place Text Box anywhere.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Text Box for holding text to be typed in.

###### Report Data Variable

Report Data Variable represents a variable of an existing Report Data model in a project. When you create the element in the diagram, the **Report Data Variable** dialog opens to select Report Data and select the variable of that Report Data.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Only Report Data in the Containment tree are supported.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Report Data Variable and its dialog for selecting Report Data and their variables.

###### Element Box

Element Box is represented as **#foreach** directive for fetching a collection of elements. After creating the Element Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the **Select Element** dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting a Class element is **#foreach($class in $Class)**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Element Box for fetching a collection of elements.

###### Page Box

Page Box is represented as **#forpage** directive for fetching a collection of elements in each document page. After creating the Page Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the **Select Element** dialog. The statement for the PageBox element is **#foreach($class in $Class)**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Page Box for fetching a collection of elements in each document page.

###### Property Box

Property Box provides a way to get an element property. You can place this element in Element Box, Page Box, and Condition Box that is contained in Element Box. The property of owner's scope element will be listed in Property Box, by which the user selects the property to show in the group.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- If the property value is a single item, the value will be printed in a single row, e.g., $class.name or $class.isActive .
- If the property value is a collection, the value will be printed in multiple rows, e.g., #foreach($member in $class.member) .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Box for obtaining an element property.

###### Element Table

Element Table is represented as **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form. After creating the Element Row in the diagram, you need to specify an element type as well as column number and row number in the **Element Table** wizard dialog. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Element Table for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Element Table wizard dialog.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

When you are back to update the element type, column number, or row number in Step 1, the table in Step 2 will be recreated with the blank content.

In Step 2 of the **Element Table** wizard dialog, the blank table is created where you can directly enter and edit text and Velocity code in the table cell. You can also press **CTRL** + **SPACE** to open the Property Guide pop-up dialog. Press **CTRL** + **SPACE** again to navigate you to Report Data Variables and Helper Tools. In this dialog, it guides you to available properties that you can select for the cell.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Property Guide pop-up dialog.

You can insert or delete columns and rows by right-clicking a cell to open the context menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The context menu for inserting and deleting rows and columns.

After creating the Element Table, you can open the **Element Table** wizard dialog again for updating the Element table through the smart manipulator button or the **Element Table** editing context menu.

###### Property Table

Property Table is represented as **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of property elements in tabular form. You can place this Property Table in Element Box to open the **Property Table** wizard dialog to select the property scope of the Table element in the Specification dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting the member property of the Class element in the first row is **#forrow($member in $class.member)$member.name#endrow**. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table (see the detail in ).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Table and the Property Table wizard dialog for selecting the property scope of the Table element.

###### Include

Include is represented as **#include** and **#includeSection** directives for including content or a document to the template. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Include/IncludeSection** dialog. After selecting the document, **$import.include(****<document_name>.docx****)** is created. There are two options to select an included document: **File** and **Attached File**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you enter a section name in the **Section** box, **$import.include(****<document_name>.docx****,****<section_name>****)** will be created instead.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Include for including content or a document to the template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Include/IncludeSection dialog for file or attached file selections.

###### Condition Box

Condition Box is a container element for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Condition** dialog. You can enter conditional statements into the statement fields. To guide available properties, you can type a dot (.) and press **CTRL** + **SPACE**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The #if statement is required for each Condition Box.
- If you want to add more #elseif statements, you can add them by entering more conditional statements.
- If you want to add an #else statement, select the Add #else option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Condition Box for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Condition dialog for entering conditional statements into the statement fields.

###### Page Break

You can place the Page Break element on the diagram to be in the template to start a new page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Page Break for starting a new page.

#### Helper Tool

Helper Tool are a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Helper Tool** dialog. You must select or search for a purpose of the function in the list to display the function description.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If the returned object is a collection of functions, select the **Create this function in #foreach directive** option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Helper Tool as a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates.

983358620

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Designer' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9bfd9d3e-138a-465d-83e0-3840e02074e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358622</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6aef6c67-73f7-48d1-84e4-4247926d69c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358634</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="beb0493c-6012-4059-b689-1de7e89f3156"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">985841017</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1ea7dc1-05bb-4e5f-8d69-19e5ea00f6be"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37f87569-bcb3-4f81-87a8-c6e645d24320"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358624</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="def2aaf1-2ee8-4c29-87ef-77edbcf43e0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this version, Document Report Designer diagram supports only the diagram for making the Microsoft Word document (DOCX) template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Using the Document Report Designer diagram</h2><p>In this section, you will learn how to create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a DOCX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a template</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a Package in the Containment tree, select <strong>Report Designer Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Document Report Designer Diagram</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating Report Designer Diagram.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.</li><li>Arrange diagram elements as desired.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Arranging Diagram Elements.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click <strong>Document Report Designer Diagram</strong>  and select <strong>Generate Template</strong> to generate the template.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bccdd6ce-5638-40b6-bb8e-811e1d2f858b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only symbols of elements on the diagram are used to generate the code template. Other elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate the code template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generating the Template.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li><p>Refine more layout, style, and code logic in Microsoft Word.</p></li><li><p>Add the complete template to Report Wizard.</p></li><li><p>Generate the report from the added template.<br /><br /></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Generated from Added Template.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h2>Diagram elements</h2><p>Document Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive.</p><h4>Text Box</h4><p>Text Box represents free, uninterpreted static-text area in which you can type anything in single or multiple lines, from plain-format text to Velocity code, one element per paragraph. You can also place Text Box anywhere.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Text Box.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Text Box for holding text to be typed in.</h6><h4>Report Data Variable</h4><p>Report Data Variable represents a variable of an existing Report Data model in a project. When you create the element in the diagram, the <strong>Report Data Variable</strong> dialog opens to select Report Data and select the variable of that Report Data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb7dd89e-a3ed-44f1-885d-9cdaa6a3bcd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only Report Data in the Containment tree are supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Data Variable.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Report Data Variable and its dialog for selecting Report Data and their variables.</h6><h4>Element Box</h4><p>Element Box is represented as <strong>#foreach</strong> directive for fetching a collection of elements. After creating the Element Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting a Class element is <strong>#foreach($class in $Class)</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Box.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Element Box for fetching a collection of elements.</h6><h4>Page Box</h4><p>Page Box is represented as <strong>#forpage</strong> directive for fetching a collection of elements in each document page. After creating the Page Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog. The statement for the PageBox element is <strong>#foreach($class in $Class)</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Page Box.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Page Box for fetching a collection of elements in each document page.</h6><h4>Property Box</h4><p>Property Box provides a way to get an element property. You can place this element in Element Box, Page Box, and Condition Box that is contained in Element Box. The property of owner's scope element will be listed in Property Box, by which the user selects the property to show in the group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4ad61ec-1f55-4a28-ae8d-e3eda91f5c62"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the property value is a single item, the value will be printed in a single row, e.g., <strong>$class.name</strong> or <strong>$class.isActive</strong>.</li><li>If the property value is a collection, the value will be printed in multiple rows, e.g., <strong>#foreach($member in $class.member)</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Box.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Box for obtaining an element property.</h6><h4>Element Table</h4><p>Element Table is represented as <strong>#forrow</strong> directive for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form. After creating the Element Row in the diagram, you need to specify an element type as well as column number and row number in the <strong>Element Table</strong> wizard dialog. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Table.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Element Table for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Table Dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Element Table wizard dialog.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d00fcc8e-2369-4be6-b56b-88005db27ff8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you are back to update the element type, column number, or row number in Step 1, the table in Step 2 will be recreated with the blank content.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In Step 2 of the <strong>Element Table</strong> wizard dialog, the blank table is created where you can directly enter and edit text and Velocity code in the table cell. You can also press <strong>CTRL</strong> + <strong>SPACE</strong> to open the Property Guide pop-up dialog. Press <strong>CTRL</strong> + <strong>SPACE</strong> again to navigate you to Report Data Variables and Helper Tools. In this dialog, it guides you to available properties that you can select for the cell.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Guide Pop-up Dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Property Guide pop-up dialog.</h6><p>You can insert or delete columns and rows by right-clicking a cell to open the context menu.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Context Menu for Inserting and Deleting Rows and Columns.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The context menu for inserting and deleting rows and columns.</h6><p>After creating the Element Table, you can open the <strong>Element Table</strong> wizard dialog again for updating the Element table through the smart manipulator button or the <strong>Element Table</strong> editing context menu.</p><h4>Property Table</h4><p>Property Table is represented as <strong>#forrow</strong> directive for fetching a collection of property elements in tabular form. You can place this Property Table in Element Box to open the <strong>Property Table</strong> wizard dialog to select the property scope of the Table element in the Specification dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting the member property of the Class element in the first row is <strong>#forrow($member in $class.member)$member.name#endrow</strong>. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table (see the detail in <ac:link ac:anchor="ElementTable"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Table Dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Table and the Property Table wizard dialog for selecting the property scope of the Table element.</h6><h4>Include</h4><p>Include is represented as <strong>#include</strong> and <strong>#includeSection</strong> directives for including content or a document to the template. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Include/IncludeSection</strong> dialog. After selecting the document, <strong>$import.include(</strong><strong>&lt;document_name&gt;.docx</strong><strong>)</strong> is created. There are two options to select an included document: <strong>File</strong> and <strong>Attached File</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db36aa00-e1fd-4c3b-b6fb-ff8cbadd6fb6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you enter a section name in the <strong>Section</strong> box, <strong>$import.include(</strong><strong>&lt;document_name&gt;.docx</strong><strong>, </strong><strong>&lt;section_name&gt;</strong><strong>)</strong> will be created instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Include.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Include for including content or a document to the template.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Include Section Dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Include/IncludeSection dialog for file or attached file selections.</h6><h4>Condition Box</h4><p>Condition Box is a container element for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Condition</strong> dialog. You can enter conditional statements into the statement fields. To guide available properties, you can type a dot (.) and press <strong>CTRL</strong> + <strong>SPACE</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b279208-3639-46c7-b587-27a4774df765"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The #if statement is required for each Condition Box.</li><li>If you want to add more #elseif statements, you can add them by entering more conditional statements.</li><li>If you want to add an #else statement, select the <strong>Add #else</strong> option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Condition Box.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Condition Box for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Condition Dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Condition dialog for entering conditional statements into the statement fields.</h6><h4>Page Break</h4><p>You can place the Page Break element on the diagram to be in the template to start a new page.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Page Break.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Page Break for starting a new page.</h6><h2>Helper Tool</h2><p>Helper Tool are a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Helper Tool</strong> dialog. You must select or search for a purpose of the function in the list to display the function description.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8fb04c8-a037-4c3e-9f6a-415ac170ff82"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the returned object is a collection of functions, select the <strong>Create this function in #foreach directive</strong> option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Helper Tool.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Helper Tool as a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93dbb6b7-1bbc-4b38-a4d0-ca645b2887bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358620</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Designer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965800 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05278: Documentation tab

- page_id: `243965800`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965800/Documentation+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:21:50.269+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Quick properties panel
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425887055

1425887058

1425887056

INLINE

The **Documentation** tab shows information associated with a selected model element in the Model Browser or on a diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Documentation tab in the quick properties panel.

To open the **Documentation** tab, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, click the Documentation tab.
- On the **Window** menu, click **Documentation**. If there is no documentation for the selected element, the text area is empty.

To write documentation for the selected model element

- In the Documentation tab, click the text area and type the text.

To turn on the HTML text

- In the Documentation tab, click the HTML check box.

The HTML toolbar opens and you can type the HTML text.

1425887054

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 05279: DOCX template in the Chart tool

- page_id: `243971298`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971298/DOCX+template+in+the+Chart+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:10.606+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Chart tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1344514374

1344514385

1344514373

**On this page**

33

1344514375

##### Creating chart objects and setting chart types and styles

To create a chart in accordance with the specified chart type and style, use the following code:

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- chartType : case-sensitive type of the created chart with one of the following options:
  - "Column"
  - "Line"
  - "Pie"
  - "Bar"
  - "Area"
  - "Surface"
  - "Radar"
- **chartStyle**: case-sensitive style of the created chart with one of the following options:
  - Column
    - "Clustered Column" (by default)
    - "Stacked Column"
    - "100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Clustered Column"
    - "3-D Stacked Column"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Column"
  - Line
    - "Line" (by default)
    - "3-D Line"
  - Pie
    - "Pie" (by default)
    - "3-D Pie"
    - "Doughnut"
  - Bar
    - "Clustered Bar" (by default)
    - "Stacked Bar"
    - "100% Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D Clustered Bar"
    - "3-D Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Bar"
  - Area
    - "Area" (by default)
    - "Stacked Area"
    - "100% Stacked Area"
    - "3-D Area"
    - "3-D Stacked Area"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Area"
  - Surface
    - "3-D Surface" (by default)
    - "Wireframe 3-D Surface"
    - "Contour"
    - "Wireframe Contour"
  - Radar
    - "Radar" (by default)
    - "Radar with Markers"
    - "Filled Radar"

If you do not specify the chart style, the default style of the chosen chart type is used.

A code example:

```text

```

##### Setting chart titles

To set the chart title, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.

##### Setting chart data categories

To create a matrix data table, set the data categories or row headers for the tag value of each data series/column by using the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

From the sample code above, the row headers of the chart are set.

##### Setting chart data series

To create a matrix data table, set the data series or column headers by using the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

##### Setting a data source for each data series

To prepare a data source for each of the data series, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

##### Printing a chart to a report

To print a chart to a report, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e58c89e0-4d52-4757-afb2-ecdc7a70b5ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1344514374</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c61827db-f9e1-4143-b962-f6d8fafbd612"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1344514385</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc62462b-dc4f-4f09-aac4-c3d0cf6f1eff"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1344514373</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="424e2402-d8cf-4b64-a411-9520513aedcf"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3c9e37a-a0cd-4736-9f49-860fac6876bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1344514375</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Creating chart objects and setting chart types and styles</h3><p>To create a chart in accordance with the specified chart type and style, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14f18f14-ed8c-4418-bba3-da7e456ef1e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[create(chartType : String, chartStyle : String) : Chart]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are:</p><ul><li><strong>chartType</strong>: case-sensitive type of the created chart with one of the following options:<ul><li>&quot;Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;Line&quot;</li><li>&quot;Pie&quot;</li><li>&quot;Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;Area&quot;</li><li>&quot;Surface&quot;</li><li>&quot;Radar&quot;<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>chartStyle</strong>: case-sensitive style of the created chart with one of the following options:</p><ul><li>Column<br /><ul><li>&quot;Clustered Column&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;100% Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Clustered Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D 100% Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Column&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Line<br /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Line&quot; (by default)</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>&quot;3-D Line&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Pie<br /><ul><li>&quot;Pie&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;3-D Pie&quot;</li><li>&quot;Doughnut&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Bar<br /><ul><li>&quot;Clustered Bar&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Stacked Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;100% Stacked Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Clustered Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Stacked Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D 100% Stacked Bar&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Area<br /><ul><li>&quot;Area&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Stacked Area&quot;</li><li>&quot;100% Stacked Area&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Area&quot;</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;3-D Stacked Area&quot;</p></li><li>&quot;3-D 100% Stacked Area&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Surface<br /><ul><li>&quot;3-D Surface&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Wireframe 3-D Surface&quot;</li><li>&quot;Contour&quot;</li><li>&quot;Wireframe Contour&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Radar<br /><ul><li>&quot;Radar&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Radar with Markers&quot;</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Filled Radar&quot;</p></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ff663a8-5a8f-4aaa-95a4-909cf4aa491e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span>If you do not specify the chart style, the default style of the chosen chart type is used</span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A code example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49b71698-6a7c-43f4-9c13-da1795a0b1d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($linechart = $chart.create("Line", "Line"))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting chart titles</h3><p>To set the chart title, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3e05969-ca81-46b0-b3e9-82e38d1e887a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[setTitle(title String)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ed7a520-5006-41c6-a511-1d11e7c6b007"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.setTitle("Line Chart")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48e15ca6-fc55-4a73-8154-b975db01cd4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting chart data categories</h3><p>To create a matrix data table, set the data categories or row headers for the tag value of each data series/column by using the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b64be4b-6660-487c-9bfe-246019575d19"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.setCategories(categories : Collection)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b744b19c-7bee-444d-b0b3-f44cdc338507"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($categories = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $categories.add("TagA"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagB"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagC"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagD"))

$linechart.setCategories($categories)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>From the sample code above, the row headers of the chart are set.</p><h3>Setting chart data series</h3><p>To create a matrix data table, set the data series or column headers by using the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b6f0dd1-8866-40ee-93f4-9c71ce1b8768"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.createSeries(seriesName : String) : DataSeries]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28dc65b3-cf97-4b7f-938b-3103889f9435"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting a data source for each data series</h3><p>To prepare a data source for each of the data series, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7793b04-cb7a-4f69-916c-5e20fc74196e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[setDataSources(dataSource : Collection)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f32c06d6-7451-4bc7-821d-d8583c928d39"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($element in $sorter.sort($MyElement))
#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))
#set($dataSources = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagA))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagB))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagC))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagD))
#set($void = $series.setDataSources($dataSources))
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Printing a chart to a report</h3><p>To print a chart to a report, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b60c62be-6788-4dcc-b7c4-1e45b0c57845"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b7d5a03-9b62-48e1-a357-7a3cfc23609b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sample Model.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A sample model used to extract values for creating a chart.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tag Values from a Model Mapped.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tag values from a model mapped into a matrix table using the Chart tool to create a chart.</h6><p>A full sample of the Chart tool code is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f8c30bd-fb4f-4f64-9858-69d2081bfa1e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('chart', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ChartTool')
#set($linechart = $chart.create("Line", "Line"))
$linechart.setTitle("Sample")

#set($categories = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $categories.add("TagA"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagB"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagC"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagD"))

$linechart.setCategories($categories)
#foreach($element in $sorter.sort($MyElement))
	#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))
	#set($dataSources = $array.createArray())
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagA))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagB))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagC))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagD))
	#set($void = $series.setDataSources($dataSources))
#end
$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Chart Printed.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart printed to a report after applying the full sample code using the Chart tool.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````
